translation: Venus: 7 in the night. Mars: a participant day and night is the Moon. Table of houses, exaltations, falls, and triplicities of the planets. Day 1901.1. I. 3. 0.n. 174 15 1 Co.n. n. 0.n. 114 0.1 b210.7 1863 1281 15 27 1 19 b21 8 bn. 28 ho. 1 170 15 b 1 ha Dd ba b m X 1170 n.i Dd 120834 Ba 12 11 18/0 227151201 Did De of movable, fixed, and common signs. Four of these signs are said to be movable, namely Aries, Cancer, Libra, and Capricorn. And four fixed, namely Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, and Aquarius. The remaining four, namely Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius, and Pisces, are common. They are called movable, fixed, or common because when the Sun enters any of them, the time moves, i.e., changes, or is fixed, i.e., perseveres in the same state, or becomes common, i.e., half of that one time will be as half of the other. For example, when the Sun enters the sign of Aries, the time changes, that is, winter turns into spring. And when it enters Taurus, the vernal time is fixed. But when the Sun enters Gemini, the time becomes common, i.e., half spring and half summer, and so on for the others. Concerning the aspects of the planets. Signs are also said to aspect each other: that is, every sign aspects the third before it and the third after it, which is the eleventh, and this aspect is called fertile and is an aspect of love and medium friendship, and it is also called sextile because it holds the sixth part of the circle, i.e., 60 degrees. For example, a planet that is at the beginning of Aries aspects that which is in Gemini before it and that which is in Aquarius after it, which is an aspect of friendship. And it aspects the fourth before it and the fourth after it, i.e., the tenth, and this aspect is called tetragonal because it holds the fourth part of the sky, i.e., 90 degrees, and it is an aspect of discord and medium enmity. And it also aspects the fifth sign before it and the fifth after it, which is the ninth, and this aspect is called trine or trine radiation because it holds the third part of the sky, i.e., 120 degrees, and it is an aspect of perfect concord and love. It aspects the seventh by opposition, and it is an aspect of perfect enmity. And if planets are in these signs thus aspecting each other, they are said to aspect each other; this is an aspect. And if two planets are in one sign, they are said to be conjoined. When a planet is in some sign, its rays will be in those signs that aspect that sign in a similar degree and minute. For example, if Mars is in the first degree of Aries and in the first minute of that degree, its rays will be in the first degree of the sign of Libra and in the first minute of that degree. Understand this for the other aspects as is most clearly shown in this figure.

translation: In Taurus, Saturn rules the belly, Jupiter the back, Mars the neck, Sol the knees, Venus the head, Mercury the feet, Luna the legs. In Gemini, Saturn rules the belly, Jupiter the genitals and what follows them, Mars the chest and what follows it, Sol the legs and ankles (that is, the heels), Venus the neck, Mercury the head, Luna the thighs. In Cancer, Saturn rules the genitals and all that follows them, Jupiter the thighs, Mars the chest, Sol the feet, Venus the arms and shoulders, Mercury the eyes and throat, Luna the head. In Leo, Saturn rules the genitals and what follows them, Jupiter the thighs and knees, Mars the belly, Sol the head, Venus the heart, Mercury the shoulders and throat, Luna the neck. In Virgo, Saturn rules the feet, Jupiter the knees and what follows them, Mars the belly, Sol the neck, Venus the belly, Mercury the heart, Luna the shoulders. In Libra, Saturn rules the knees and what follows them, Jupiter the eyes and what follows them (that is, parts of the head), Mars the genitals and what follows them, Sol the shoulders, Venus the head, Mercury the belly, Luna the neck. In Scorpio, Saturn rules the ankles (that is, the heels) and what follows them, Jupiter the feet, Mars the head, arms, and thighs, Sol the neck, Venus the genitals and what follows them, Mercury the nose, Luna the belly. In Sagittarius, Saturn rules the feet, Jupiter the legs and head, Mars the feet and hands, Sol the belly, Venus the thighs and arms, Mercury the genitals and heart, Luna the back. In Capricorn, Saturn rules the head and feet, Jupiter the knees and eyes, Mars the legs and shoulders, Sol the back, Venus the thighs and heart, Mercury the genitals and what follows them, Luna the thighs. In Aquarius, Saturn rules the head and neck, Jupiter the shoulders, chest, and feet, Mars the ankles and heart, Sol the genitals and what follows them, Venus the knees and what follows them, Mercury the thighs and neck, Luna the genitals. In Pisces, Saturn rules the shoulders, arms, and neck, Jupiter the heart and head, Mars the ankles and belly, Sol the thighs and what follows them, Venus the neck and back, Mercury the legs and genitals, Luna the thighs. Concerning the masculine and feminine degrees of the signs. There are also in each sign degrees which are properly called masculine and feminine. For from the beginning of Aries up to the 8th degree they are said to be masculine; and from the 7th to the 9th, feminine; and from the 9th to the 15th, masculine; and from the 15th up to the 22nd, feminine; and from the 22nd up to the end of Aries, masculine. We have decided to describe these as we described the terms, and here we depict a table so that the work may be more easily understood, with God's help. 1 masculine, 8 feminine, 1 masculine, 6 feminine, 7 masculine, 8 feminine, 5 masculine, 6 feminine, 6 masculine, 4 feminine, 3 masculine, 0 feminine, 5 masculine, 3 feminine, 6 masculine, 4 feminine, 4 masculine, 2 feminine, 6 masculine, 2 feminine, 2 masculine, 11 feminine, 4 masculine, 3 masculine, 8 masculine, 5 feminine, 3 masculine, 7 feminine, 8 masculine, 7 feminine, 8 masculine, 4 feminine, 8 masculine, 10 masculine, 5 feminine, 10 masculine, 5 feminine, 7 masculine, 3 masculine, 4 feminine, 10 masculine, 3 feminine, 8 masculine, 5 masculine, 2 feminine, 3 masculine, 7 feminine, 12 masculine, 6 masculine, 11 feminine, 8 masculine, 11 masculine, 5 feminine, 10 masculine, 6 feminine, 4 masculine, 2 feminine, 3 masculine, 10 masculine, 10 feminine, 10 masculine, 3 feminine, 5 masculine, 2. Concerning the lucid, dark, smoky, and empty degrees. In each of these signs there are degrees which are called lucid, and degrees which are called dark, and degrees which are called smoky, and degrees which are called empty. For some say that from the beginning of Aries up to the third degree they are dark; and from the 3rd to the 8th, lucid; and from the 8th to the 16th, dark; and from the 16th to the 20th, lucid; and from the 20th up to the 24th, empty; and from the 23rd up to the 28th, lucid; and from the 29th to the end of Aries, empty. We will make a table of these, if God wills.

For some say that from the beginning of Aries up to the third degree they are dark; and from the 3rd to the 8th, lucid; and from the 8th to the 16th, dark; and from the 16th to the 20th, lucid; and from the 20th up to the 24th, empty; and from the 23rd up to the 28th, lucid; and from the 29th to the end of Aries, empty. We will make a table of these, if God wills.

author: Alchabitius
title: The Twelfth House
text: né poſt ſenectute. dixit aledezgod domin triplicitatis domus mortis, primus fignificat mortem fecúdus precepta ſeu res antiquas.tertius almauerith idest que hereditanda funt ex mortuis. Ona domus é pegrinationű itinerű fidei atqz religiõis fapiétie phie z librop epistolar quoqz ac legatoy narrationi feu rumo ru atqz fomnior:7 initiŭ dimidie vite fignificat. Dixit alēdezgod qdis triplicitatis domus pegrinationis.pmus figt pegriatio néz oé quod accidit ei.fecundus fignificat fidé atos religioné z valitudines barum atqz modú eax.tertius fignificator eſt ſapientie fomniorú z ſtellar z augurioum z veritatis exercentium in eis,atos mendacij. Ecima domus eft regina z opex z sublimatióis: vel exaltatióis regni quoqz atos memorie z vocis.f.imperij z magiſteriop atq3 matru:z ſigt dimidiú annox vite. Dixit alédesgod q dis triplici tatis domus regie.primus ſigt opus z exaltatione.f.fedis fubli mationě z altiſſima manfione.fecundus igit voce imperij z audacia in eodé. tertius fignificat ſtabilitatem eius atqz durabilitatez. Ndecima domus est fiducie z laudis atqz fortue:amicor quoqz z miniſtrozu ac auxiliatoru:z figt fine annoruz medietatis vite. Dixit alédezgod odis triplicitatis domus fiducie. primo figt fiducia.ſcos amicos.tertius vtilitatem feu profectus eorum. Uodecima domus est inimicori z laboris atqz triſtitie inuidie ſuſurrationis caliditatis ingeniorú atqz bestiarŭ:z ſigt finé vite t qo cótingit matribus in coceptione ſua ex bono vel malo.Dixit alendezgod Dis triplicitatis domus inimicoruz.primus fignificat inimicos, fecundus labores.tertius vero beftias a pecora. I vnusquifos planeta babet in vnaques iſtarum domowz qua dam poteftate ex poteftatibus ſcilicet accidentalibus que dicuns tur gaudium:qı mercurius gaudet in afcendente. Luna in domo liij. Uenus quoqzin.v. mars in.vi.Sol in.viij. Jupiter in.xi.Sa turnus in.xij. De fortitudine angulorum. I dicitur in fignificative domozi yanguli fignificat fortitudine zpfectioné.Ladentes vero ab angulis debilitaté 7 detrimétű:nis hg.viij.7.iy.fignificant ré aptam z detectă cu fama.xij.7.vi.figni ficant occultationem a retentionem z vilitatem rey. Anguli aut z domini angulorú fignificant magnitudiné bonorú precij atcs fortune:foru titudinem z exercitationé z elongationé a cau:z pitia eop in cadentibºé có traria fortune.i.dedecus 7 cafus. De fuccedétib vero anguloy.dom fuc cedit decime.i.vndema fignificat fortitudiné z fortuna medias amicopir ex ea parte in qua erat fiducia.z illa que ſequit quarta.i.v.fignificat fortuna me dia p donatiões z veneratiões z că filiozú cá veneratione leticia z gaudio:q aut fuccedit afcendéti que eſt ſecuda ſignificat fimiliter fortuna media ex cả ſubſtantie 7 miniftrop que vero fuccedit feptime.i.octaua fignificat fimiliter fortună media ex almauerith.i.exsba quiditat a mortuis z rebus occultis. De fignificationibus dominorum angulorum. E quos fut fignificatives dominozŭ angulorú dū fuerint pites in angulis. Pritia dni aſcédétis i afcédéte fignificat ei fortuna p femetipfuz: pei acqſitione:z ſignificat pitia eii.x.pregé z ma gifteria altiora:z cû fuerit i.vii.p cóuentióes z factores atos vros res ppreſentia quos ei in arta fignificat fortunag bereditates 7 cas patrű p productiões aquarú z populatiões vel plátatiões.i.edificatióes zex res bus antigs z radicalibus.Significat quos domin decime per pritiaz ſuá in eadem decima fortuna vel per regem vel per regnú magnů z magiſteria altiora:z fignificat p pitia fuá i ſeptima regnů p victoria stentionű z ex cauf vxozúsz p pitia eius i ärta figt regnum pamicitia nobiliú z regis ei z pcau fas tribunorú z per diſpenſatons regum a cultus terrarű a edificationes ci uitatú z per diuifiones fluminú z cuſtodias ciuitatum 7 ex rebantigs:z per pitia fuá i afcendéte fignificat regna pingenia z per ppinquitaté regis reb vulgi. Pitia aút dni ſeptime in ſeptima ſignificat fortunap negociatios nest p cóuentiões:z pnutritiões quoqz z mulieres:z p fatores:r fignificat ppitia ei in quarta p cóuentiões mulierű:z p negociatiões:z p cás patrúz bereditatú z cult terre z pitia ſua in afcendente y couentides z negociatio,
translation: The twelfth house is of enemies, labor, sadness, envy, whispering, cunning, ingenuity, and beasts. It signifies the end of life and what happens to mothers in their conception, whether good or bad. Alendezgod said that the lord of the triplicity of the house of enemies: the first signifies enemies, the second labors, and the third beasts and cattle. These are what the twelve houses signify. Concerning the colors of the twelve houses: The twelve houses also signify colors, and these are: the first house, the ascendant, and the seventh are white. The second and twelfth are green. The third and eleventh are yellow. The fourth and tenth are red. The fifth and eighth are honey-colored, that is, the color of honey. The sixth and ninth are black. Concerning the joys of the planets in the houses: Each planet has in one of these houses a certain power from the accidental powers, which are called joy: Mercury rejoices in the ascendant. The Moon in the third house. Venus also in the fifth. Mars in the sixth. The Sun in the eighth. Jupiter in the eleventh. Saturn in the twelfth. Concerning the strength of the angles: It is said that in the significations of the houses, the angles signify strength and perfection. Those falling from the angles, however, signify weakness and detriment. The eighth and fourth signify a suitable and revealed thing with fame. The twelfth and sixth signify concealment, retention, and the vileness of things. The angles and the lords of the angles signify the magnitude of good things, of price, and of fortune; strength, exercise, and elongation from the cause. Their presence in the cadent houses is contrary to fortune, that is, disgrace and fall. Concerning those succeeding the angles: The house succeeding the tenth, that is, the eleventh, signifies medium strength and fortune through friends, and from that part in which there was trust. And that which follows the fourth, that is, the fifth, signifies medium fortune through gifts and venerations and with children, with veneration, joy, and gladness. That which succeeds the ascendant, which is the second, similarly signifies medium fortune from the cause of substance and ministers. That which succeeds the seventh, that is, the eighth, similarly signifies medium fortune from inheritance, that is, from what is inherited from the dead and from hidden things. Concerning the significations of the lords of the angles: These are the significations of the lords of the angles when they are present in the angles. The presence of the lord of the ascendant in the ascendant signifies fortune for himself, and through his acquisition. And his presence in the tenth signifies kingship and higher magistracies. And when he is in the seventh, through conventions and agents and your things, through the presence of those whom he signifies in the fourth, fortune, inheritances, and the causes of fathers, through the production of waters and populations or plantations, that is, buildings, and from ancient and radical things. The lord of the tenth also signifies, through his presence in the tenth, fortune either through the king or through a great kingdom and higher magistracies. And his presence in the seventh signifies a kingdom through the victory of contentions and from the causes of wives. And through his presence in the fourth, he signifies a kingdom through the friendship of nobles and his king, and through the causes of tribunes, and through the dispensers of kings and the cultivation of lands and the building of cities, and through the divisions of rivers and the guardianship of cities, and from ancient things. And through his presence in the ascendant, he signifies kingdoms through ingenuity and through the proximity of the king to the things of the common people. The presence of the lord of the seventh in the seventh signifies fortune through negotiations and through conventions, and also through nourishments and women, and through agents. And his presence in the fourth signifies through the conventions of women, and through negotiations, and through the causes of fathers and inheritances and the cultivation of land. And his presence in the ascendant signifies through conventions and negotiations.

author: Alchabitius
title: Significations of Venus
text: onis aquarum a mésure eay necno terray:7 ex qualitate animi ſigt largis tatem z verecúdiam atqz'iusticia z ex fectis pluralitatem atqs fimulationez. Quidam aút dixerit o fignificat epar z ftomachu z auré finiſtraz brachia atos vétrem:inferioza quos pectinis z inteſtina z ex colorib colorem cineri tium z viridem z boy files:z ex ſaporibus 33 dulcem:7 quátitas orbis eius est graduu.9.7 ex diebus 13 diem Jouis: ex noctib?noctez diei lune:z ans ni fridarie eius.i.dñationis eius fit.i2.7 anni marimi fut.428.maiores.79. medij.45.7 dimidiú,miozes vero.i2.7 fortitudo eius ex plagis circuli estin occidéte. Et dixit meſſeala & ex bominibus fignificat bominem album ba bentem ruborem in facie:babenteni oculos non prorfus nigros: nares no d equales z breues caluum:in aliquo dentium habentem nigredine:pulchre ftature boni animi bonis moribus:pulchri corporis. Et dixit Dorothius q fignificat bominem babentem magnos oculos:z pupillam latam barbam criſpam z babet ex regionibus alchirath babyloniam z aſen z perfidem: 7 alarmes 7 archadiam z ex partibus habens partem habitudinis pfect z fignificat fidem z appetitum in bonis:z ex operibus falubritatem fecurita tem a participationem. M 2 Ars mafculinus nocturnus malus operatur calorem z ficcitas tez z eft fignificator fraty peregrinationí: z habet ex ctatibus iunétutem vſoqz in finem iuuētutis:z7 natura eius colerica amari ſapois:z ex magisterijs omne magiſterium igneŭz qö fit p fer rum zignem:ficut est percuffio gladiorum cum martellis. Luqz ei complectitur Saturnus figt pouffionem ferri. Si Jupiter fignificat pour Honem cris. Si fol fignificat pcuffionem numorum aureop. Si venus fignis ficat magiſterium oznamétorum. Si mercurius pcuffionem acuuz. Si luna fignificat percuffionem lacearum librorum:fignificat ét cum ſolus fufcipit fignificatiõem opus medicine.Si nullus planeta ei complectif fignificat mi nutionem:z vulneratióe37 aptidez z boy fimilia. Et si complectit ei Satur nus ſigt opus medicine z vulnerum.Ethiupiter figt opus naturaruz. Et fi fol figt curam oculoy.Si venus figt opus oznaméti:vt tonfuram crinium z barbarum z abſcafionem vngularum:z si mercurius figt abſcifionem vena rum. Si luna fignificat eradicatióez dentiú z purgativez aurium. Et pſe ſigt iniurias miferoz z effuſionem fanguinis z oppreffiones p vim z abſciſſios nes viarum z iracundiam z ducatum exercitus z festinationem z inuerecú diam z peregrinationem extra patriam:z feditatem coitus: cafus puero rum id eft abortiuorum: fratres medios atque forores:fcientiam ac difcre tionem cure beftiarum. Et exinfirmitatibus febres calidas z ſanguineas 7 pustulas ſanguineas alabraba que elt rubedo corporis cum afperitate z feditate 7 cómeſtione carnium cum putredine : 7 dimidij capitis dolozeni: z ignem facrum z pauorem a cogitationes horribiles que inquietant bos mines a commouent z impediunt atque inanes reddunt: quicquid fuerit cum inflammatione caloris. Et ex qualitate animi commotivem ſcilicet anis mi z conturbationem.Si afpexerit eum Saturnus fignificat odium z maxi mam inuidiam:z ex fectis eam in qua bellum fuerit z vnitatem z celeritatez mutatióis a fide:z multitudiné beſiratióis z mutatiöez de testaméto in testa mentum:erút ti hec oia fub vnitate.Dixerut quidam y habet ex membris fel renes 2 venas a decurfum spermatis 2002fum: habet ex coloubus rus bedinem:z ex ſaporibus amarum:z quátitas orbis eius eft.8.grad.7 ex dies bus 13 diem martis:z ex noctibus noctem ſabbati:z anni fridarie eius funt ſeptē:z anni eius maximi fut.264.maiores funt.66.medij aŭtl. 40.7 dimi diusz minores.15.7 fortitudo ei in plagis circuli in meridie. Et dixit meſceas la o fignificet de imaginibus hoium boiem rubeŭ babétez capillos ruffos z faciem rotundam leuiter boies debonestátem habétem oculos croceos boubilis afpectus audacé al citum babétem in pede fignú vel maculama bij Et dixit Doz0thius & fignificat habentem acutum afpecti:z ex ptibus par tem audacie z ſignificat perfeueratiam calliditatem z fupbiam z leuitatem 7 mobilitatem z audaciam z negociationé'acuitatem z feftinatione in omni bus rebus:z habet ex partibus mundi Asce z terras romanorum vfqpin oc cidentem:z terras tuscorum. S Olpaſpectum fortunatus eft:z malus per cõiunctionem in vno ſi gno z est masculus diurnus operatur caliditatem z ficcitatem: zest fignificator patrum fi fuerit natiuitas in die fignificat regnum maxi mum z animam vitalem z lumen z ſplendorem z intellectű z pulcburudiné z mudiciam atqz fidem. Et ex etate finem iuuétutis. Et participatur vniuer fis planetis in difpofitione annorum. Et ex magisterijs regimen z principa tum:z fignificat iaculationem iaculorum z venationem purgatõez cú omni ſpecie purgandi qua corpora interius z exterius purgatur. Et ex infirmita ribus fignificat infirmitates calidas 7 ficcas in corporibus apparentes.Et ex ſubſtatia aurum plurimum z vniuerfas ſpecies ſubſtantie. Et ex qualitate ipfius animi fublimitatem prudétiam z ſequitur honeftatem.f.largita tem z gloriam:z prolixitatem métis. Et ex fectis culturaz boná z eis ſimilia. Et fignificat imperium vocis z fortitudinez celeritaris. Si complectitur ei Saturnus fignificat villicationem z buiufmodi principarum.Si iupiter figni ficat in fide principatum z religionem z etiam iudicem inter homines iudis cantem opera oppreſſor vel iniuriarum zć. Si mars fignat ducatum exers citus z inueftigationem bellorum. Si venus fignat regnum per mulieres 7 per obſequium potētum. Si mercurius fignat cófultores regum 7 opus lis b202um z hereditatum a maiorum operum.Si luna fignat opus legatowz I detectionem cófiliorum z huiufmodi similia. Et dixerüt quidá o ſol fignat imaginé vultus bois:7 pprie ex viris oculum dextrum: ex mulieribus ocu lum finiftrum:z dixerüt gdam & b3 202 7 medullam z femora. Et ex infirmi tatibus comeftionem carnium in 022:7 detrimétum ois ppie z defcenfios nem aque in oculum:ptás eius in capite. Et dixerut indi q cũ fuerit in afcés dente erit comburens z habebit fignum in facie. Et bzex coloribus quicgd videtur peregrino colore:z ex faporibus acrem. Et quátitas orbis eius est. 15.graduu.Et ex diebus diem babet dnicam:z ex noctibus noctem Jouis Anni fridarie eius funt decem.Et anni eius maiores funt. 120.maximi funt. i 460.medij.39.2 dimidium vel fm quofda3.69.7 dimidium:minores vero 19.Fortitudo eius ex plagis circuli est in oiéte. Et dixit meſceala o fignat 6 figuris hoiuz eum que b3 colorem inter croceum z nigrum.i.fufcum tectű cum rubore breui ſtature criſpum caluuz pulchri corporisiz bz ex faporibo acutum. Et dixit Dorothius & figura ſolis z lune est figura planetay q fue rint cum eiser eius que dignior fuerit in loco eorú. Si ergo velis fcire figu, ram solis ſcito o sit crocea bris partem rubois capillop oculi eius aliquás tulum crocei. Et ex'paribus habens partem futurozn.i.partem diuinatios nis z est fignificar or naturarum z ſpiritus fapiétie eleuationis 7 pfectionis fidei quoqz ſciëtiay z laudum. Et ex partibus múdi babet zamarach z cura z ferit z eius terminos 7 terras romanorum.leborus leilidad F
translation: of waters, from the measure of water and also of earth; and from the quality of the soul, it signifies generosity and modesty and justice, and from sects, plurality and simulation. Some, however, have said that it signifies the liver and stomach and the left ear, arms and belly, and the lower parts of the pelvis and intestines, and from colors, the color of ashes and green and similar ones; and from tastes, sweetness; and the quantity of its orb is 9 degrees. And from days, Thursday; from nights, the night of Monday; and its fridaria years, that is, its dominion, are 12; and its maximum years are 428, major are 79, medium are 45 and a half, minor are 12; and its strength from the regions of the circle is in the west. And Masha'allah said that among men it signifies a white man having redness in his face, having eyes not entirely black, nostrils not equal and short, bald, having some blackness in his teeth, of beautiful stature, good spirit, good morals, beautiful body. And Dorotheus said that it signifies a man having large eyes, and a wide pupil, a curly beard; and it has from regions Alchirath, Babylonia, Asen, and Persia, and Alarames and Arcadia; and from parts, it has the part of perfect habit; and it signifies faith and appetite for good; and from works, health, security, and participation.

translation: Mars is masculine, nocturnal, and evil; it produces heat and dryness; and it is the significator of brothers and peregrinations; and it has from ages youth up to the end of youth; and its nature is choleric, of a bitter taste; and from crafts, every fiery craft and what is done by iron and fire, such as the striking of swords with hammers. And if Saturn is conjoined with it, it signifies the striking of iron. If Jupiter, it signifies the striking of brass. If the Sun, it signifies the striking of gold coins. If Venus, it signifies the craft of ornaments. If Mercury, the striking of needles. If the Moon, it signifies the striking of book covers; it also signifies, when alone, the signification of the work of medicine. If no planet is conjoined with it, it signifies diminution, and wounding, and aptitude, and similar things. And if Saturn is conjoined with it, it signifies the work of medicine and wounds. And if Jupiter, it signifies the work of natures. And if the Sun, it signifies the care of eyes. If Venus, it signifies the work of ornament, such as cutting hair and beards and trimming nails; and if Mercury, it signifies the cutting of veins. If the Moon, it signifies the eradication of teeth and the cleansing of ears. And by itself, it signifies the injuries of the miserable, and the shedding of blood, and oppressions by force, and the cutting off of roads, and anger, and the leadership of an army, and haste, and shamelessness, and peregrination outside the homeland, and the foulness of coitus, the falling of children, that is, abortions, middle brothers and sisters, knowledge and discretion in the care of animals. And from illnesses, hot and bloody fevers, and bloody pustules, alabraba which is redness of the body with roughness and foulness and consumption of flesh with putrefaction; and half a headache; and sacred fire, and fear and horrible thoughts that disturb men and move and impede and render them empty; whatever is with inflammation of heat. And from the quality of the soul, namely, emotional disturbance and perturbation. If Saturn aspects it, it signifies hatred and great envy; and from sects, that in which there is war and unity and swiftness of change of faith; and a multitude of desires and change from testament to testament; all these will be under unity. Some have said that it has from the members the gall, kidneys, and veins, and the flow of semen and urine; it has from colors redness; and from tastes bitterness; and the quantity of its orb is 8 degrees. And from days, Tuesday; and from nights, the night of Saturday. Its fridaria years are seven; and its maximum years are 264, major are 66, medium are 40 and a half, minor are 15; and its strength in the regions of the circle is in the south. And Masha'allah said that it signifies among the images of men a red man having reddish hair and a round face, slightly dishonest, having yellowish eyes, of a bold appearance, audacious, having a mark or stain on his foot. And Dorotheus said that it signifies one having a sharp gaze; and from parts, the part of audacity; and it signifies perseverance, cunning, and pride, and lightness, and mobility, and audacity, and negotiation, sharpness, and haste in all things; and it has from the parts of the world Asia and the lands of the Romans up to the west, and the lands of the Tuscans.

translation: The Sun is fortunate by aspect, and evil by conjunction in one sign; and it is masculine, diurnal; it produces heat and dryness; and it is the significator of fathers. If the nativity is in the day, it signifies the greatest kingdom and a vital soul and light and splendor and intellect and beauty and cleanliness and faith. And from age, the end of youth. And it participates with all planets in the disposition of years. And from crafts, governance and principality; and it signifies the throwing of javelins and hunting, purification with every kind of cleansing by which bodies are purified internally and externally. And from illnesses, it signifies hot and dry illnesses appearing in bodies. And from substance, much gold and all kinds of substance. And from the quality of the soul itself, sublimity, prudence, and it follows honesty, that is, generosity and glory; and length of mind. And from sects, good culture and similar things. And it signifies the power of voice and the strength of swiftness. If Saturn is conjoined with it, it signifies stewardship and such principality. If Jupiter, it signifies principality in faith and religion and also a judge judging among men the works of oppressors or injuries, etc. If Mars, it signifies the leadership of an army and the investigation of wars. If Venus, it signifies a kingdom through women and through the obedience of the powerful. If Mercury, it signifies the counselors of kings and the work of books and inheritances and greater works. If the Moon, it signifies the work of legates and the detection of counsels and similar things. And some have said that the Sun signifies the image of a man's face; and specifically, in men, the right eye; in women, the left eye. And some have said that it has the brain and marrow and thighs. And from illnesses, the consumption of flesh in the mouth; and the detriment of all property; and the descent of water into the eye; its part in the head. And the Indians said that when it is in the ascendant, it will be burning and will have a mark on the face. And it has from colors whatever appears of a foreign color; and from tastes, sourness. And the quantity of its orb is 15 degrees. And from days, it has Sunday; and from nights, the night of Thursday. Its fridaria years are ten. And its major years are 120, maximum are 460, medium are 39 and a half, or according to some, 69 and a half; minor are 19. Its strength from the regions of the circle is in the east. And Masha'allah said that it signifies among the figures of men him who has a color between yellowish and black, that is, dark, covered with redness, of short stature, curly, bald, of beautiful body; and it has from tastes sharpness. And Dorotheus said that the figure of the Sun and Moon is the figure of the planets that are with them or of that which is more dignified in their place. Therefore, if you wish to know the figure of the Sun, know that it is yellowish, having a part of redness in the hair, its eyes somewhat yellowish. And from parts, having a part of future things, that is, a part of divination; and it is the significator of natures and the spirit of wisdom, elevation, and perfection of faith, also of sciences and praises. And from the parts of the world, it has Samarkand and Cura and Serit and its territories and the lands of the Romans. Leborus, Lelilad, F.

Et dixerút q dam Luna a coniunctione quidem vfque ad dimidium luminis fui pris mum erit natura eius bumida z a medietate luminis fui vsqzad impletiõez erit natura eius calo: ab impletione vſqz ad dimidium fui luminis ſecun dum erit natura eius ficcitas:z a dimidio luminis ſuiſecundi vſqz ad conius ctiõem erit natura eius frigus. Et quia diximus in boc capitulo de ſignificatione planetaruz dum complectuntur alteri planete. Similiter etiam eft cófiderandum in ces teris rebus que ſignificant planete. De bois diei z noctis quorum funt planetax. translation: If it is Fortune itself, Venus signifies courtesy and kindness, and a gentle spirit, and honest customs, and swiftness of movement, and divine obedience. If it is Jupiter, it signifies honesty, caution, and companionship, and kindness, and beauty of life. And if it is afflicted by malefics and it is Mars, it signifies a tendency to whisper among men and to use such things. If Saturn, hatred, simulation, and envy. If the Sun is in a praiseworthy aspect, it signifies a royal disposition. And if it is Mercury, it signifies rhetoric, that is, eloquence and goodness of tongue and writing. And some have said that it signifies thought and novelty of mind and weakness of sense and heaviness of tongue, and honest women, the nourishment of infants, and mothers and maternal aunts, and the preparation of foods. And others have said that it properly signifies the brain and the lung. And from colors it has saffron, and from tastes, salt. And the quantity of its orb is 12 degrees. And it signifies the left eye in men, and the right in women. And from days it has Monday, and from nights, the night of Venus. Its Firdaria years are nine, or 105 major, 520 maximum, and 66 and a half medium, and according to some, 39 and a half, and 25 minor. And its strength from the regions of the circle is to the right of the west. And Masha'allah said that among the figures of men, it signifies a white man tinged with redness, with joined eyebrows, benevolent, having eyes not entirely black, a round beautiful stature, and a mark on his face. And from earthly things it has artichoke and tinamou and alder. And some have said that it signifies childhood from the beginning of the month up to 7 days; from 7 days up to 14 days, youth; and it signifies the age of perfection, that is, middle age up to 21 days and nights; and old age up to conjunction, that is, up to the end of the month when it is conjoined with the Sun. And when it is under the Sun's rays, it signifies secrets and hidden things; and similarly every planet. And at the beginning of the month when it increases, it signifies everything that is to be done; and at its fullness, what is to be destroyed, because it decreases. And at the beginning of the month, it and the Sun signify accusers; and at the end of the month, the accused. And at the beginning of the month, it signifies gain and continence; at the end of the month, stipends and dispersions. And in opposition, it signifies contrariety. And at the beginning of the change to opposition, that is, when there are 15 degrees between it and the opposition, it signifies the beginning of contrariety and its cause. And when it has separated from opposition, it signifies the causes of exception from contrariety. And in its exit from under the Sun's rays, it signifies exit from concealment and its daughter. And in its entrance under the rays, it signifies adaptation to concealment. And the hour of its division from the Sun signifies its aptative exit from concealment. And the hour of its exit from under the rays signifies appearance, arrival from absence. And when it is in the fourth aspect of the Sun, it signifies diminution of a thing or descent from high to low. Similarly in the fourth aspect, but in the first fourth aspect, it signifies the appearance of things and gain and increase of continence; and in the second fourth aspect of the Sun, it signifies the opposite of these. And some have said that from the conjunction up to the first half of its light, its nature will be humid; and from the middle of its light up to its fullness, its nature will be hot; from its fullness up to the second half of its light, its nature will be dry; and from the second half of its light up to the conjunction, its nature will be cold. The other planets, from their rising up to their first station, will be in the nature of humidity and signify childhood; and from their first station up to the opposition of the Sun, their nature will be hot and signify youth; and from this place up to the second station, their nature will be dry and signify perfect age; and from their second station up to their first occultation, their nature will be cold and signify cold old age. And some have said that the Sun signifies the spirit, that is, the vital soul. The Moon, thought and sense. Saturn, melancholy and sadness, vitality and evil. Jupiter, wisdom and reason. Mars, anger, fury, and swiftness. Venus, play and joy. Mercury, rationality or dialectic and discipline. And because we have spoken in this chapter about the signification of the planets when they are conjoined with another planet, similarly it must be considered in other things that the planets signify. Regarding the order of the planets or their rulership when in the conception of children, when they have been in the mother's womb, it must be known that the first month from the hour of conception is of Saturn. The second of Jupiter. The third of Mars. The fourth of the Sun. The fifth of Venus. The sixth of Mercury. The seventh of the Moon. The eighth of Saturn; and therefore he who is born in the eighth month does not live, because he is born under the power of Saturn. And the ninth of Jupiter. The rulership or order of the planets in human life, that is, how they dispose the life of the native, is divided thus: The Moon begins from the native's entrance, that is, from the birth of the child, and disposes the quantity of nourishing years, which are four years. Then Mercury for ten, then Venus for eight. Afterwards the Sun for nineteen. Afterwards Mars for fifteen. Afterwards Jupiter for twelve. Then Saturn up to the end of life. Regarding the nature of the Dragon's Head and Tail. The Dragon's Head is masculine. Similarly, it is also fortune, and its nature is composed of the nature of Jupiter and Venus; and this signifies kingdom and fortune and substance. And some have said that its nature is augmentation, because when it is with benefics, it increases their fortune; and when it is with malefics, it increases their malice. And its Firdaria years are three. Its Tail, however, is evil; and its nature is composed of the nature of Saturn and Mars; it signifies dejection and fall and poverty. And some have said that its nature signifies diminution, because when it is with benefics, it diminishes their fortune; when it is with malefics, it diminishes their malice. Therefore it is said that the Head is benefic when it is with benefics, and malefic when with malefics; and the Tail is malefic when with benefics, and benefic when with malefics. And its Firdaria years are two. Regarding the hours of the day and night, which belong to the planets. If there is any day or night of any of the planets, the first hour is of that planet, and the second hour is of the other planet that succeeds it; the succession of the others is according to the order of the circles by proportion, for example: on the day of Venus, to which the day belongs, the first hour is of Venus itself, the second of Mercury, the third of the Moon, the fourth of Saturn, the fifth of Jupiter, the sixth of Mars, the seventh of the Sun, the eighth again of Venus, the ninth of Mercury, the tenth of the Moon, the eleventh of Saturn, the twelfth of Jupiter. Behold, you have 12 unequal hours of the day. Likewise for the hours of the night. The first hour of the following night is of Mars, to which the whole night belongs. The second of the Sun. The third of Venus. The fourth of Mercury. The fifth of the Moon. The sixth of Saturn. The seventh of Jupiter. The eighth again of Mars. The ninth of the Sun. The tenth of Venus. The eleventh of Mercury. The twelfth of the Moon. And thus you also have 12 unequal hours of the night. And the day of Saturday begins afterwards, whose first hour is of Saturn; and the whole day is his. Thus by counting in order, you will find the hours of the days and nights and their divisions among the individual planets, if God wills. Regarding masculine and feminine hours. The hours are also said to be masculine and feminine, because the first hour of each day and night is masculine, the second feminine, the third masculine, the fourth feminine. Thus they succeed in order, one feminine, the other masculine, up to the end of the day and night.

author: Alchabitius
title: Mars, Venus, and Mercury
text: eligere per coloré. In hoc enim dicto adest dubii qo nos foluere op3.Nam multi credunt credunto color nó oftendat virtuté rei z boc est ve ritas in terrenis propter comiftionem elementop que in eis est.ti in celeftis bus nó eft ita:qreop ſubſtatia non est vllo modo compofita ex aliquo elemé to. Et pp hoc redijt ptolemeus ad probádum hanc virtutem alio modo dis cens:z qe exiftit circa folem: 7 qe sphaera in qua eſt ſol pofita eſt ſub eo vult Dicere quod accidit:vt fit veritas res iſta quia virtus martis comburit eog exiftit circa folemiz qe ſol est ſub eor cómifcet virtus ſua cú illa solis fit calis dus multú z ficcus:ita o fine temperaméto comburit.dicit Daly abenragel mars eſt planeta calidus z ficcus igneus:destructor:iratus:victoriofus:dili gens occidere z interfectiões:rixas:litigia:cito irafcif ira foni:totú corfuum exponitin rebus fuis agēdis:prelia facit a destruit populationes. Exaltatio fua est in domo saturni.f.in capricorno q fignificat clamores impediméra z genté stulta z guerratióes. Lõuenit ſaturnus marti in infortunio z refiftit ei in frigiditate z tenebroſitate, inclinat ſoli amore ſui a defendit auxilio eius gilij qm ſol exaltak in domo ſua z gubernat eum de calore z ficcitate iz recipit ab codé: pprietas znä ſua conuenit z appropinquat pprietati z nature solis. neſcius est z obliuioſus modici intellect a diminut ſenſu nec pſiderat reruz fines:per eű z per aſcenſum z per deſcenſum ei in circulo ſuo creſcit z decre ſcit eftatis calor z byemis frigiditas oi anno.Dicit alfragan qo mars ptinet qititatem terre femel z dimidium 7 octaua ptem eius. S I per aſpectum fortuna. natură fos lis z littera gloſa non indiget.Dicit Ptolemeus cognitares est qo opus fubitatie ſolis est calefacere a dare aliquátulű ficcitatis 7 bec opera magis pprie ſentiunt z leui ſcita sunt oibus alijsp pter magnitudiné solis z ppter manifestaz mutationem quam per tempora anni facit a quanto magis accedit ad noſtra capita tanto magis calefacit.Di cit Daly abenragel ſcito o ſol est lumen 7 candela celi gubernator mundi fa ctor temporum:per eum fiunt planete oriérales z ocidentales:z per eum fi unt apparétes z occulti:z per eum mouetur omnis res ſe moués:per eúnas ſcit omnis res naſcés:crefcit omne foliuz z maturat omnis fructus. Jpfe est ſpirit celi magnus cum eo viuificant ſigna:z quodlibet fignum quádo est in eo hét maioritatem ſuper alia ſigna.quoniam ipm viuificat z illuminat 7 dat ei fortitudinem z calorem 7 applicat calorem z fortitudinem z vtutem illius figni terre:q2 natura z facta ſua apparent in oibus rebozin cúctis aiatis z in aiatis exiſtentib in terra. Et qn exit de ſigno in quo est remanetillud fignuz in ſilitudine corpis defuncti. Eſt planeta magni dominij potentie nobilitatio altitudinis z magnitudinis.eft fortuna per aſpectum z infortuna per pinctio nem corpalem. Aides enim qn algs planeta coniungit fibi:coburit z vincit eŭ z extinguit lumé z lucé ſuá. Locus ſuus in celo eſt ärtus.f.medius.7 pla netarű:ficut rex ſapiés qui per ſenſum manutenet regnum ſuů z per cófides rationem ponit ſedez ſua in medio regni ſui vt per oia latera attingat.Dedit marti militia ſuá z qß eé dur militie ſue:qr celú ſolis est ſub celo martis z có venit natura z calor fuus cuz natura 2 caloreillius. Dedit Joui ſua iudicia p pter temperie boneſtatem ſaluationé z ſue cóplexionis ſaluré : 7 qanó est in eo qualitas mala nec peccadi natura. Dedit regnum ſaturno qe omnes pla nete ſua lumina mittit ad eum z fuas cófiderationes. Dedit veneri collectio nem reddituú z vendendi redditus z emēdi:qı celú eius applicat ſuo z funt vicini.Dedit mercurio ſcribaniam:q2 ſtatus eius eft ſicut ſtatus fcriptoris re gis qui vadit qn ille vadit z ſedet qn ille fedet. Dicit lune algazilaticum:quest ſimilis algazilo regis qui facit ſua mádata z defert ea quocus pcipit. Dicit alfraganus auctoritate Ptolemei quod corp solis continet corpus terre cé ies sexagefies ſexies z aliquátulum plus. Enus fortía femina nocturna bic ponit naturam veneris a patet in littera qö dicit.Dicit pro leme venus ipſamet opera Jouis facit ppter temperamētú ſue cóplexionis excepto qo eſt Joui in re yna cótraria:qz calefacit pa rum eo qd exiſtit circa ſolem z humectatſcom lunam ppter magnalucez bet hic planeta eo qo attrahit ad ſe fumofitatem que eleuat ab humoribus terre.Dicit. Daly in comméto vult dicere quod eius calor est multum minor illo Jouis:z ppter humiditaté až facit dicimus qu est quodámodo frigida z plus bumida. Quod aut dicit eo qo exiſtit circa ſolê dixit eo q virtus ſua vo luiť cú illa ſolis. Et id qo dixit humectat ficut luna:vult dicere o venus facit humiditaté circa eaqz facit luna. Etid qo dixit pp magna luce vult dicere p modic calor facit humores currere.Dicit Daly abenragel venus est fortuna frigida a humida nocturna bylaris gaudioſa appentie bone limpida formo ſa:diligit inculationes cáratiões comestiões potatiões z vitia:eſt masueta z pauci mot. eſt mulierű fignificatrix z iacédi cũ eis amoris amicicie z ſocie, tatis.recipit marté per näm fornicatióis z limpidamēti abhorret ipz ppärie tatem nature ſue que est caliditas 7 ficcitas. Separat se ab eo z malú zinfo2 tunam fuam repellit a ſeipfa ppter manfuetudinem bona uerba folatiuz bo num.z manfuetam loquelam.Locordat cum faturno in frigiditate 7 puenie tía quá bét cũ eo in libra:qr est domus vnioz exaltatio alteri. Et diſcordat cú eo in mozib trifticijs z doloribofuis zeo q8 saturnus est vfus modici cú mu lieribus.Status veneris cum ſtatu solis eft fimilis ſtatui mulieris verſus vi 1.Dicit alfraganus o corpo veneris eft vna po de.37. ptib? terre. M Pic näm mercu Ercurius cómixtus hipote 7 cit Ptoleme:ficcitas vero z humiditas mercurij eſt per vnum equale:quoniam aliquádo deſiccat zel effugat humores eo o nú qz elongat multú a calore solis etiam nullo tempore z aliquan-s do humectat eo q pofitus eſt ſuper ſphaera lune que est circa terram magis quam omnes alie ſphaere z mutať ad hos duos ſtatus z facit uentos ppter ſerotinas mutationes eius circúquas ſolem.Dicit Daly in commento. Ault dicere q opus mercurij non est vna res sola:quoniam aliquando deficcat z aliquando humectat.cum autem deſiccat est quado soli applicat z est tunc opus ſuum in calefaciendo 7 ſiccando circa istud solis. Humectare aut fuuz eit ppter vim ſua que cómifcet cú illa lune que poſita eſt ſub eo z y est op ſuum ficut opus lune.Et Ptolemeus qui comperationez facit de opere mer curij cum illo solis inuenit o aliqui faciebat opus tale öle fol. Et qn per com parationem facit cum opere lunezinuenit o aliquádo faciebat opus tale öle luna. Et expertus fuit hoc multotiens z inuenit o ſemper alterú duoy ope rum faciebat z ſciuit pro certo o opus ſuum est from opus aliarum ſtellap cum quibus inuoluitur:z opus proprium in ſe non habet. Dicit Daly Abens ragel. Mercurius eſt planeta maleficiorum violentiarum documentorum ſcribanie computationum ſcientiarum: calidus est a ficcus forme z natu re puertibilis:maſcul cu maſculis:femine cu femininis:fortuna cũ fortuis: ifortuna cu iſortunis:bene rónatus:bi loqués aufus in loquédo:pulchre ap parentie:compoſite perſone: diligit libros 7 computationes. Placent ei ma giſteria:res bene facte rónes verificari libri ac ſcientie:agilis est in motu z p pietatis ardétis:alacer z mobilis in oibus rebºfuis;z eſt vnus triŭ planeta
translation: to choose by color. For in this saying there is a doubt which we must resolve. For many believe that color does not show the virtue of a thing, and this is true in earthly things because of the mixture of elements that is in them. But in celestial things it is not so, because their substance is in no way composed of any element. And because of this, Ptolemy returned to proving this virtue in another way, saying: and that which exists around the Sun, and that the sphere in which the Sun is placed is below it, he wants to say that it happens that this thing is true because the virtue of Mars burns that which exists around the Sun, and that the Sun is below them, its virtue mixes with that of the Sun, it is very hot and dry, so that it burns without moderation. Daly Abenragel says Mars is a hot and dry, fiery planet, a destroyer, angry, victorious, loving to kill and slaughter, quarrels, disputes, quick to anger, sounding anger, exposing his whole body in his affairs, making battles and destroying populations. His exaltation is in the house of Saturn, that is, in Capricorn, which signifies shouts, impediments, and foolish people and wars. Saturn agrees with Mars in misfortune and resists him in coldness and darkness, inclines to the Sun for love of it and defends it with the help of its rays, because the Sun is exalted in its house and governs it with heat and dryness, and receives from it: its property and nature agree and approach the property and nature of the Sun. He is ignorant and forgetful of little intellect and diminished sense, nor does he consider the ends of things: through him and through his ascent and descent in his circle, the heat of summer and the cold of winter increase and decrease every year. Alfragan says that Mars contains the quantity of the earth one and a half and an eighth part of it. If by aspect fortune. The nature of the Sun and the letter needs no gloss. Ptolemy says it is known that the work of the substance of the Sun is to heat and to give some dryness, and these operations are felt more properly and are more easily known than all others because of the magnitude of the Sun and because of the manifest change which it makes through the seasons of the year, and the more it approaches our heads, the more it heats. Daly Abenragel says know that the Sun is the light and candle of heaven, the governor of the world, the maker of seasons: through it the planets become oriental and occidental: and through it they become apparent and hidden: and through it every moving thing moves: through it every nascent thing is known: every leaf grows and every fruit ripens. He is the great spirit of heaven, with him the signs are vivified: and every sign when it is in him has greater power over other signs. Because he vivifies and illuminates it and gives it strength and heat and applies the heat and strength and virtue of that sign to the earth: because its nature and its deeds appear in all things and in all living and inanimate things existing on earth. And when it leaves the sign in which it is, that sign remains in the likeness of a dead body. It is a planet of great dominion, power, nobility, height and magnitude. It is fortune by aspect and misfortune by bodily conjunction. For you see that when any planet joins with it: it burns and overcomes it and extinguishes its light and splendor. Its place in heaven is the fourth, that is, the middle. And of the planets: like a wise king who maintains his kingdom by sense and by consideration places his seat in the middle of his kingdom so that he may reach through all sides. He gave Mars his militia and that he should be hard in his militia: because the heaven of the Sun is below the heaven of Mars and its nature and heat agree with the nature and heat of that. He gave Jupiter his judgments because of the temperance, honesty, salvation and health of his complexion: and that there is no bad quality in him nor a nature to sin. He gave Saturn the kingdom because all planets send their lights to him and their considerations. He gave Venus the collection of revenues and the selling of revenues and buying: because its heaven applies to his and they are neighbors. He gave Mercury the scribeship: because his status is like the status of the king's scribe who goes when he goes and sits when he sits. He gave the Moon the viziership: who is like the king's vizier who does his commands and carries them wherever he commands. Alfragan says on the authority of Ptolemy that the body of the Sun contains the body of the earth sixty-six times and a little more. Venus, strong, feminine, nocturnal, here places the nature of Venus and it is clear in the letter what it says. Ptolemy says Venus herself does the works of Jupiter because of the temperance of her complexion, except that she is contrary to Jupiter in one thing: because she heats a little because she exists around the Sun and moistens according to the Moon because of the great light this planet has because it attracts to itself the smokiness that rises from the humors of the earth. Daly in his commentary wants to say that her heat is much less than that of Jupiter: and because of the humidity she makes, we say that she is somewhat cold and more humid. But what he says because she exists around the Sun, he said because her virtue is willing with that of the Sun. And what he said she moistens like the Moon: he wants to say that Venus makes humidity around her that the Moon makes. And what he said because of great light, he wants to say that little heat makes humors flow. Daly Abenragel says Venus is a cold and humid nocturnal fortune, cheerful, joyful, of good appetite, clear, beautiful: she loves embraces, songs, eating, drinking and vices: she is gentle and of little movement. She is a significator of women and of lying with them, of love, friendship and society. She receives Mars by nature of fornication and purification, she abhors him by the property of her nature which is heat and dryness. She separates herself from him and repels his evil and misfortune from herself because of her gentleness, good words, good solace, and gentle speech. She agrees with Saturn in coldness and the presence she has with him in Libra: because it is the house of one and the exaltation of the other. And she disagrees with him in morals, sadness and his sorrows and because Saturn is of little use with women. The status of Venus with the status of the Sun is similar to the status of a woman towards a man. Alfragan says that the body of Venus is one part of 37 parts of the earth. Mercury is mixed with the hypothesis and Ptolemy says: but the dryness and humidity of Mercury is by an equal one: because sometimes it dries and drives away humors because it never moves far from the heat of the Sun, also at no time, and sometimes it moistens because it is placed above the sphere of the Moon which is closer to the earth than all other spheres and changes to these two states and makes winds because of its late changes around the Sun. Daly in his commentary says. He wants to say that the work of Mercury is not one thing alone: because sometimes it dries and sometimes it moistens. But when it dries, it is when it applies to the Sun and then its work is in heating and drying around that of the Sun. But its moistening is because of its power which mixes with that of the Moon which is placed below it and its work is like the work of the Moon. And Ptolemy who makes a comparison of the work of Mercury with that of the Sun found that some made such a work as the Sun. And when he makes a comparison with the work of the Moon, he found that sometimes it made such a work as the Moon. And he experienced this many times and found that it always made one of the two works and knew for certain that its work is according to the work of the other stars with which it is involved: and it has no proper work in itself. Daly Abenragel says. Mercury is a planet of evils, violences, documents, scribeship, computations, sciences: it is hot and dry, of changeable form and nature: masculine with males: feminine with females: fortunate with fortunes: unfortunate with misfortunes: well-reasoned: eloquent, bold in speaking: of beautiful appearance: of composed person: he loves books and computations. He likes magistracies: well-done things, verified reasons, books and sciences: he is agile in movement and of ardent piety: lively and mobile in all his affairs; and he is one of the three planets.

author: Alchabitius
title: Almuten Calculation and Profection
text:
ilta planeta habes maius dilum sine plures fortitudines in.5.locis pdictis a gbe accipit bylech q für locus solis locus lune loc cõiunctõis uel preuen tionis pars fortune gradus ascendés ille planeta est almuté ideft vincés.Et si aliqs habuerit dignitates in ka vel tertia uel pluribus locis ex locis pdict ille est almuté. Verbi gratia:pono o unus planeta sit dns ascendét) z dis termini eiufdé ascédétis z cú hoc dñs faciei eiufdé.talis planeta haberet in ascédente.8.fortitudines: z pono y alter planeta sit dris exaltatióis ascédes tis z dns dom? loci solis z dñs triplicitatis ptis fortune:talis planeta esset dignior almuté quas primus:qa habet plures fortitudines in locis pdictis. Et ut abbreuiet sermo coligende sűt fortitudines cuiuslibet in.5.locis predi ctis:z ille eorum qui abundauerit in numero fortitudinű ille erit almutem. I ex hoc profectio in annis na tiuitatu atqz munis. In hoc.c.docet op pses de vno signo ad aliud:z hoc dupliciter. Primo in'annis natiuita tú. Scdo in annis magnay cóiunctionu. Opus in annis natiuitatú est facile eſt śnia littere talis.cú volueris scire ad qo signú applicat annus natiuitat alicuius:vide quot anni pfecti trasierint nato z incipies a gradu ascendente natiuitatio:z dabis cuilibet anno vnú signácz vbi finitus fuerit numerus ibi erit signu pfectionis ab ascéndéte. Verbi gra:ascéndés alicuius natiuitatis fuit.io.gra.arietis in fine pmi anni venit pfectio ad.io gradu tauri. In fine secudi anni ad.io.gradum geminorez sic km hunc ordinem dando cuilibet anno signú vnum vfqz ad complementu 12.annox.tunc enim scilicet in fine 12.annorum reuertit ad locum radicis. Si autem multi anni trasierint nato subtrabe a toto numero annoy pfectoy qui tranfierunt nato.i 2.quoties po teris idest diuide p.i 2.7 numex qui remanet infra. i 2.proijce ab ascendente natiuitatis Fm modum prius dictu. Verbi gra:ascendés cuiusdam natiuita tis fuit.i3.gradus virginis:z tranfierunt nato.32.anni solares completi.fub trabam a.32.annis.i2.bis 7 remanent.8.anni completi:dabo ergo primum annum virgini:secúdum libre:tertium scorpioni:quartum sagittario:gntum capricomo: sextū aquario: septimü piscib: octauű arieti:veniet ergo pfectio ad signú qo succedit imediate.f.ad taupad cósimilé gradű gradui radicis.f. ad.i 3.eiufdem.Sicut dixi 'de gradu ascendéte:ita faciendum est de gradu so lis:z gradu lune:z gradu ptis fortune:z vbi applicuerit annus dris illius si gni est dispositor illius anni. Et ab illo accipiuntur facta de quibus natus se intromittet illo anno z fortunia z infortunia que accident sibi illo anno.Bus ctor ponit exempla in littera:ideo non est necesse multum insistere exemplis Deinde dicit auctor ca pfectio anni puenerit ad aliquê gradum alicui signi z fuerit aligs planeta vel radij alicuius planete post ipsum gra.in eodem si gno vel in eadem domo in figura z volueris scire quando pueniet profectio ad ipsum planetá vel radios eius:vide quot gradus 7 minuta sint inter gra. ad qué puenit annus z ipsum planetá vel radios eius z multiplica illos per 12.7 sexta vnius.hoc est dicere, dabis cuilibet gradui, 2.dies ⁊ sextam prem vnius ⁊ prouenient tibi dies qui sunt a principio illius anni vsqz ad tem CL リッ -

pus in quo pfectio perueniet ad planeta vi eius radios. Ratio autes quare cuilibet gradui datur.i 2.Dies:2.6.vnius est:q: hoc modo in fine anni coples tur vnum signum. Si enim multiplicaueris.30.gradus per.i 2.7 sexta vnius prouenient.365.dies qui sunt dies vnius anni. Dicit Ptolemeus in proposi tione.236.quarte partis quadripartitiful.z sciemus dispositioné facti annos rum accipiendo numex annoy natiuitatis:z pijciemus eos a quolibet los corum bylegiorú secúdum ordinationé signorum unicuiqs ligno annum accipiemus dispositorez a signo in quo applicat numerus graduú. Ide Da ly in comméto dicit quod in hoc dicto vult narrare Ptoleme qo quodlibet signum disponit in ano vno: z sic ibit signi reuolutio in quo est significator q bullibet.i 2.annis vna vice vses ad fine vitez hoc sciunt oés astrologi. rofectio auté ex annis mūdi. cet opus pfectõis in annis mundi. Sententia litere est talis. Cú volueris scire applicatiбез annoy alicuius secte vide quot trans sierint anni solares completi a pncipio illius aní in quo suit mas gna cóiunctio q significauit illam secta:z diuide illú numex annoy p.1 2.2 nu mey q remáserit infra.i 2.pijce ab ascendéte illi ani in quo suit piunctio z vbi applicuerit numerus ibi est signú pfectióis. Auctor ponit exemplū in Ira de secta sarracenou.i.machometi:z dicit & iter anni piunctionis q significauit legé illá z annū apparitiõis machometi.f.in quo incepit pdicare fuerit.6 i. ani. Alia littera 13.52.ani. Ego credo y debet esse.52.ani:7.57.dies.alioquin nó cócordabit cu illo quod dicit postea.zafcendés anni in quo suit illa con iunctio fuit signú geminor z puenit ille ánus in quo incepit predicare ad sis gnum uirginis.f.ad signum illius planete qui fuit dris signi ascendétis anni Coiunctionio.Si enim subtrabatur 2.5 i.i 2.quoties poterit remanent.3.dek ergo signo geminop prim?. scos cácro. tertius leoni z sicanus incomplet qui fuit positus in numero puenit ad signum virginis. No est nobis magna cura quot anni fuerunt ab anno illius coniunctionis vsqs ad primum an nuá arabú. Sufficiat eni nobis scire o anus ille in quo inceperat ani arabú puenit ad uirgine:ga ani arabú sunt nobis noti ex tabulis extractiois earű z poterimus incipe a vgine amɔ dicit auctor in Ira. Et qetpe auctor ipsi opa bant in prib suis pannos play:ió applicat dem sui ad anos play.z dicit cú volueris brē noticia huius reii.scire ad qo signú applicat ann? ex áno cons iunctionis q significauit secta sarracenoru vide quot ani psarum trassierút verte illos in dies ptabula extractiois eay deseruiente annis psay:aut quod multiplicent anni per. 365.tot enim dies cotinet annus pricus qi anni plici nó bit artaz diei z numero diep q puenerit tibi adde.3624.dies. tot eni di es fuerit a principio annorű arabu uſqz ad principiŭ annorū: persax. 2 q8 collectű fuerit diuide p.365.7 quarta vnius:z pueniét anni solares: si qd resi duum fuerit erűt dies anni impfecti. Et potes etiam numerum dien reduce re ad annos solares ptabulas extractióis eay deseruientę annis solaribus. Tota inténtio auctoris stat in hoc quod sciamus quot anni solares pfecti trá Kerunt ab initio annoy arabum vsqs ad tépus cósideratiois 7 damus cuiliss i
translation: If a planet has a greater share or more strengths in the five aforementioned places, from which it receives hyleg, which are the place of the Sun, the place of the Moon, the place of conjunction or prevention, the Part of Fortune, and the degree of the Ascendant, that planet is the almuten, meaning the victor. And if any planet has dignities in one or three or more of the aforementioned places, that is the almuten. For example: I assume that one planet is the lord of the Ascendant and the lord of the terms of the same Ascendant, and with this, the lord of the face of the same. Such a planet would have 8 strengths in the Ascendant. And I assume that another planet is the lord of the exaltation of the Ascendant and the lord of the house of the Sun's place and the lord of the triplicity of the Part of Fortune. Such a planet would be more worthy as almuten than the first, because it has more strengths in the aforementioned places. And to shorten the discourse, the strengths of each planet should be collected in the five aforementioned places, and the one among them that abounds in the number of strengths will be the almuten. From this comes profection in the years of nativities and of the world. In this chapter, it teaches how profection moves from one sign to another, and this in two ways. First, in the years of nativities. Second, in the years of great conjunctions. The work in the years of nativities is easy, and the meaning of the letter is this: When you wish to know to which sign the year of profection of someone's nativity applies, see how many complete years have passed since birth, and you will begin from the ascending degree of the nativity, and you will give one sign to each year, and where the number ends, there will be the sign of profection from the Ascendant. For example: the Ascendant of someone's nativity was 10 degrees of Aries. At the end of the first year, profection comes to 10 degrees of Taurus. At the end of the second year, to 10 degrees of Gemini, and so on according to this order, giving one sign to each year until the completion of 12 years. For then, at the end of 12 years, it returns to the radical place. If, however, many years have passed since birth, subtract 12 from the total number of completed years that have passed since birth as many times as you can, that is, divide by 12, and project the number that remains below 12 from the Ascendant of the nativity according to the method previously stated. For example: the Ascendant of a certain nativity was 13 degrees of Virgo, and 32 complete solar years have passed since birth. I will subtract 12 from 32 years twice, and 8 complete years remain. I will therefore give the first year to Virgo, the second to Libra, the third to Scorpio, the fourth to Sagittarius, the fifth to Capricorn, the sixth to Aquarius, the seventh to Pisces, the eighth to Aries. The profection will then come to the sign that immediately succeeds, namely to Taurus, to a degree similar to the radical degree, namely to the 13th of the same. As I said about the ascending degree, so it must be done for the degree of the Sun, and the degree of the Moon, and the degree of the Part of Fortune. And where the year applies, the lord of that sign is the disposer of that year. And from that, the deeds in which the native will be involved in that year, and the fortunes and misfortunes that will befall him in that year, are taken. The author gives examples in the text, so it is not necessary to insist much on examples. Then the author says that when the profection of the year has reached some degree of some sign, and there is some planet or the rays of some planet after that degree, in the same sign or in the same house in the figure, and you wish to know when the profection will reach that planet or its rays: see how many degrees and minutes there are between the degree to which the year has come and that planet or its rays, and multiply them by 12 and one-sixth. This is to say, you will give to each degree 12 days and one-sixth of one, and you will find the days from the beginning of that year until the time when the profection will reach the planet or its rays. The reason why 12 days and one-sixth of one are given to each degree is that in this way, at the end of the year, one sign is completed. For if you multiply 30 degrees by 12 and one-sixth, you will get 365 days, which are the days of one year. Ptolemy says in proposition 236 of the fourth part of the Quadripartitum: "And we will know the disposition of the events of the years by taking the number of years of the nativity, and we will project them from each of the hylegical places according to the order of the signs, giving one year to each sign, and we will take the dispositor from the sign to which the number of degrees applies." Indeed, Da Ly in his commentary says that in this saying, Ptolemy wants to state that each sign disposes in one year, and thus the revolution of the sign in which the significator is will go, so that every 12 years it returns once until the end of life. All astrologers know this. Profection, however, from the years of the world. This work of profection is in the years of the world. The meaning of the letter is this: When you wish to know the application of the years of any sect, see how many complete solar years have passed from the beginning of that year in which there was a great conjunction that signified that sect, and divide that number of years by 12, and project the number that remains below 12 from the Ascendant of that year in which the conjunction occurred, and where the number applies, there is the sign of profection. The author gives an example in the text about the sect of the Saracens, i.e., Muhammad, and says that between the year of the conjunction that signified that law and the year of Muhammad's appearance, i.e., when he began to preach, there were 61 years. Another text says 52 years. I believe it should be 52 years and 57 days, otherwise it will not agree with what he says later. And the Ascendant of the year in which that conjunction occurred was the sign of Gemini, and that year in which he began to preach came to the sign of Virgo, i.e., to the sign of that planet which was the lord of the ascending sign of the year of the conjunction. For if 61 is subtracted by 12 as many times as possible, 3 remain. Therefore, the first is Gemini, the second Cancer, the third Leo, and the incomplete year that was placed in the number came to the sign of Virgo. It is not of great concern to us how many years there were from the year of that conjunction until the first year of the Arabs. It is sufficient for us to know that the year in which the Arab years began came to Virgo, because the Arab years are known to us from the tables of their extraction, and we can begin from Virgo, as the author says in the text. And because the author himself and his works were in his parts for the years of the Persians, he applies his discourse to the years of the Persians, and says: when you wish to have a brief knowledge of this matter, to know to which sign the year applies from the year of the conjunction that signified the sect of the Saracens, see how many years of the Persians have passed, convert them into days by the table of their extraction serving the years of the Persians, or multiply the years by 365, for a Persian year contains that many days, because Persian years do not have the exact day, and to the number of days that you find, add 3624 days, for that many days were from the beginning of the Arab years until the beginning of the Persian years. And what is collected, divide by 365 and one-fourth, and solar years will result. If there is any remainder, these will be the days of the imperfect year. And you can also reduce the number of days to solar years by the tables of their extraction serving the solar years. The whole intention of the author is that we know how many complete solar years have passed from the beginning of the Arab years until the time of consideration, and we give to each.

author: Alchabitius
title: Parts of the Third House
