Qui si in natura sua fuerit z nul Ins planeta ei complectitur fignificat res terreas a rerum augmétationem creſcendo:z ex etate iuuétutem:z pfectum in eaiz ex operibus opa que ges nerant cognitioné predicationem z rhetoricam z negociatiões 7 extimatios nem z geometria:z diſpoſitioné reiz philofophiam z auguria z puerbia z ſcripturam:z verfificádi ſcientiam z opus numeri maxime.Luish complectit Saturnus fignificat ex opere numeri opus méſure terrarum z bereditatuz 7 numex edificiorum atqz telari.Si Jupiter Agnat numerum prallédi z nu merum libzorú diuinorum. Si Mars fignificat numex qui fit in donatiuis exercituů z pugnatium a numerum pcuſſionum flagellorum atqs clauaruz. Si Sol fignat preeſſe numero regum z ſubſtatie domor. Si Uenus fignat numerum chordarum lignoy ſeu cytbararum numerum ſonor ates fistu larum.Si Luna fignat numerum ferculorum que fiunt ad comeftiones itis nerantium extra domum. Et fignat de infirmitatibus infirmitates ſpirituas les anime.i.cogitatiões horribiles:7 inquietudiné métis atqz dubitationes 7 cetera fimilia. Significat etiam animi qualitaté zë fimulia kim cóplexionez ſuá z cóiunctioné: cú fuerit fonunat erut bonitates im genus fortune que facit eum fortunatum:7 Fm locum in quo fuerint fortunatus mercurius:z cú fuerit malus erút iniquitates Fm malum qo eu facit malum:7 Fm locum in quo factus est malus. Et fignificat ex fectis culturam vnitatis 7 borum fi milia:z boc fecreto cú bypocrisia z ſimulatione. Et dixerüt quidam o ſigni ficat femora vmbilicum z pectinem a crura neruos atqzuenas z ex coloris bus habet omnem coloré commixtuates variatú:zalez meniu qui eſt color floris lilij agreftis:z ex faporibus acetoſum. Et quátitas orbis eius eft.7.gra duú:z habet er diebus diem mercurij:7 ex noctibus noctem diei dominice. Anni fridarie eius funt.az anni eius maiores.76.maximi.460.medij.48. minores vero.20.foritudo eius in plagis circuli eſt in ſeptéirione. Et dire runt quidam mercurius a medio retrogradationis eius vis ad ſtationem fecundam fignificat pueritiamiz a ratione fecúda vſqz ad coniunctionem folis iuuentutemiz a coniunctione ſolis vios ad ftationem primam mediam etatem:z a ſtatione ſua prima vſqz ad medium retrogradationis fignificat fenectutem z cótrarietatem in coniunctione fua per directionem vlfqz ad ſtationem primam fignificat dilectionem z amicitiam z amoris imitationes: 7 ex ftatione fua fecunda ad coniunctionem iens ſignificat inquifitionem dis lectionis a concordiam:7 a tatione ſua prima iens iterum ad retrogradas tionem fignificat inquifitionem contrarietatis z diſſimilationis tarditatem zverecundiam paruam z occupationem 7 stuporem in rebus:z in medio re trogradationis fignificat celeritatem temperatam ppter ingenij debilitas tem.z apud ftationem fecundam fignificat ftuporem z occupationem ins genij z tarditatem z egreſſus ab hac celeritatem z apertionem ingenioruz ==End of OCR for page 2== ==Start of OCR for page 3== coniunctione fua foli per directionem fignificat celeritatem ex paffionem ingeniozum z latitudinem vel augmentationem eorum. Similiter Ueneri accidit in bis locis 7 planetis altiorib? Et dixit meſceala, mercurius fignifis cat ex figuris bominem non multum album neqs nigrum babentem colo rem:frontem babentem eleuatam a longam:in facie longitudinem z nafuz longum:raram babentem barbam in maxillis z oculos pulchros non ex to to nigros:longos quos habentem digitos. Et ex partibus habet parté nes gociationis: fignificat timorem infestationem z bellum zinimicicias z fes ductiones a contrarietates:fignificat quoqs pfectum magifterium z fubtilis tarem in opere z inquifitione:z in ceteris que fiunt in hominibus ex actis 7 contentionibus. Et ex terris habet adelech alcuidem z maxime in meridie: terras indorum. 7 Una fortuna feminina nocturna operať frigiditatem z humidi tatem: ex fignificatrix matrum: fi fuerit natiuitas nocturna 7 eft in ea falſa temperatum a ſignificat etatem puerilem z initiuz crefcendi. Et ex operibus habet legationes z madata:z ope ra aquarum atqz terrarum Fm quátitatem fortunij vel infortu nij vel mali o fi fuerit bona fignificat bonum & econuerfo.Significat etiam principatum fi prefuerit vel fuerit Domina aliquibus rebus ad regem perti nentibus fi fuerit fortunata a ſole in bono afpectu z in bono loco. [ Etde fubftantia fignificat argentum z terre cultum fi babuerit auctoritatem in quarta z ex fide religionem.Et ex infirmitatibus epilenſiam paraliſim guttá Caducam z vultus torſionem enodationem membrorum a commotionem: z quicquid fuerit in fimilitudine frigoris 7 humiditatis. Et qualitatem anis miam coniunctionem fuam cum planetis. Luificomplectitur for
translation: Mercury, mixed, masculine, diurnal, inclines by his nature to that which he aspects from the planets and from the signs. He signifies younger brothers, and signifies servants and the affection of concubines. He also signifies divinity and the oracles of prophets, and credulity, and work and honor or prayer. If he is in his own nature and no planet aspects him, he signifies earthly things and the augmentation of things by growth. And from age, youth, and perfection in it. And from works, works that generate knowledge, preaching, rhetoric, negotiations, estimation, and geometry, and the disposition of things, and philosophy, and auguries, and proverbs, and writing, and the science of versification, and especially the work of numbers. If Saturn aspects him, he signifies from the work of numbers, the work of measuring lands and inheritances, and the number of buildings and looms. If Jupiter, he signifies the number of prayers and the number of divine books. If Mars, he signifies the number that is in the gifts of armies and fighters, and the number of blows of whips and clubs. If the Sun, he signifies presiding over the number of kings and the substance of houses. If Venus, he signifies the number of strings of wood or citharas, the number of sounds and pipes. If the Moon, he signifies the number of dishes that are made for the meals of travelers outside the house. And he signifies from infirmities, spiritual infirmities of the soul, i.e., horrible thoughts, and restlessness of mind and doubts and other similar things. He also signifies the quality of the soul and similar things, his complexion and conjunction. When he is fortunate, there will be good things according to the kind of fortune that makes him fortunate, and according to the place in which Mercury is fortunate. And when he is unfortunate, there will be iniquities according to the evil that makes him unfortunate, and according to the place in which he became unfortunate. And he signifies from sects, the worship of unity and similar things, and this secretly with hypocrisy and simulation. And some have said that he signifies the thighs, navel, and groin, and legs, nerves, and veins. And from colors, he has every mixed and varied color, and the color of the wild lily flower. And from tastes, sourness. And the quantity of his orb is 7 degrees. And he has from days, the day of Mercury; and from nights, the night of Sunday. The years of his firdaria are 9. And his greater years are 76, greatest 460, middle 48, but lesser 20. His strength in the regions of the circle is in the north. And some have said that Mercury from the middle of his retrogradation until the second station signifies childhood; and from the second station until the conjunction of the Sun, youth; and from the conjunction of the Sun until the first station, middle age; and from his first station until the middle of retrogradation, he signifies old age and contrariety in his conjunction through direct motion until the first station, he signifies affection and friendship and imitations of love; and from his second station going to conjunction, he signifies the inquiry of affection and concord; and from his first station going again to retrogradation, he signifies the inquiry of contrariety and dissimulation, slowness and little shame, and occupation and stupor in things; and in the middle of retrogradation, he signifies temperate swiftness because of the weakness of ingenuity. And at the second station, he signifies stupor and occupation of ingenuity and slowness, and the egress from this, swiftness and the opening of ingeniousness.

Et fi complectitur ei Jupiter ſigt ſonum lectionum vel cantationű quibus vtűs tur Domini ſectarum in altaribus z locis orationis eorum in laudem dei om nipotentis. translation: His conjunction with the Sun through direct motion signifies the swiftness and passion of ingeniousness and their breadth or augmentation. Similarly, it happens to Venus in these places and to the higher planets. And Masha'allah said that Mercury signifies, from the figures of men, a man not very white nor black in color, having an elevated and long forehead, length in the face and a long nose, having a sparse beard on the jaws and beautiful eyes not entirely black, having long fingers. And from parts, he has the part of negotiation; he signifies fear, infestation, and war and enmities and seductions and contrarieties; he also signifies perfect mastery and subtlety in work and inquiry, and in other things that happen among men from deeds and contentions. And from lands, he has Adelech, Alquidem, and especially in the south, the lands of the Indians. The Moon, a feminine, nocturnal fortune, operates coldness and humidity. She is the significator of mothers if the nativity is nocturnal, and in her there is a false temperate quality, and she signifies childhood and the beginning of growth. And from works, she has embassies and messages, and works of waters and lands according to the quantity of fortune or misfortune or evil, so that if she is good, she signifies good, and vice versa. She also signifies principality if she presides or is the mistress of some things pertaining to the king, if she is fortunate with the Sun in a good aspect and in a good place. And from substance, she signifies silver and cultivated land if she has authority in the fourth house, and from faith, religion. And from infirmities, epilepsy, paralysis, gout, falling sickness, facial distortion, disarticulation of limbs, and commotion; and whatever is similar to coldness and humidity. And the quality of the soul, her conjunction with the planets. If she aspects

author: Alchabitius
title: Planetary States in Themselves and Mutually
text: Differentla tertia in bis que accidunt planetis ſeptem in femetipfis z quid accidat eis abinuicem. I quia iam annuente deo iam pegimus effe planetarum in femetipfisiz quid fignificent: profequamur narrando quid eis accidat in femetipfis z abinuicem id est a quibusdam er ga quofdam. Ignificatio planetarum in femetipfis eft vt fit planeta afcendens in circulo augis fue minor lumine z magnitudis ne atos curfu ideft cum fuerit inter ipſuz z fu am augez minus.90.gradibus ante vel retro fi autem fuerint inter ipſuz z augem.90.gra dus equales erit equalis in lumine z magnis tudie atqz cursu.ſi vero fuerit extra bec duo loca.i.plus.go.erit defcendens de circulo augis z apparebit maioz lamine 7 magnitudie ac curſu velociouz si fuerit ponio eius equata min.180.gra. erit augens numerum.z si fuerit plus erit minuens numeruz:z si fuerit poz tio eius equata ex.180.gra.vel.360.erit nec augens nec minuens: zfiaddať equatio equata ſuper medium curſum dicitur augens curſum vel numerý: z fi minuitur eius equatio dicitur minuens numerum.Et quando alige pla netarum altioruz.3.vadit plus medio curſu ſuo dicitur augere curfum:zfi minus vadit dicitur minuere curſumiz ſi vadit tátum dicitur curſu equalis quando aliquis planetarum inferiozum vadit plus medio curſu ſolis erit augens curfum.quando vadit minus dicitur minuens curſum:z quádo va dit tantum erit curſu equalis. Luminarium autem effein cursu eft ficut effe trium altiorum planetarum. Sit quos planeta feptétrionalis cum tranfies rit genzabar fuum.i.cum tranfierit per viam Solis iens a meridie in ſepren trionem ille trafitus.i.illa abſciſio circulorum dicitur gézabar.cum ergo pla neta tranfierit ipfam abſciſionem z fuerit inter planetam z ipfam abſcifio nem minus.90.gra.erit planeta ſeptétrionalis aſcendész cum fuerit a.90. vſqz ad.iso.erit feptétrionalis defcendens: cum tranſierint hunc numeruz idest cum babuerit plus iens ad.270.erit meridianus deſcendens:7 cútrás flerint hunc numerum iens ad.360.erit meridianus afcendens,hoc est effe planetarum in femetipfis. E effe aut planetay ab inuicé tractemus.f.gd accidat.5 planetis erga luminaria:z dicemus ex hoc qo dixit Ptolemeus de almu gea hoc est de viſione inuicem faciei ad facié.boc est cû fuerit in ter planeta z folem:dum fuerit planeta occidentalis.i.dum fegk ſolem ti quátum est inter domu illius planete z domú ſolis de fignis:aut cu fuerit inter ipſuz planetá z lună cu fuerit oriétalis a luna.i.duz fuccedit luna tantú quátum eft inter domu planete z domú lune ex fignis.i. cú fuerit planeta tm diſtás a ſole poſt quantú diſtat domus eius a domo fos Us:fimiliter & luna dicif: qu cũ planeta ſucceſſerit foli z fuerit a fole faturnus Lin.6.figno: iupiter in.5.mars in.4.venus in.3.mercurius in.2.túc or effe in almugea folis.Silr cú fuerit inter vnúquécs planetá z luná fuccedété bic numerus fignor vniufcuiusos planete dicitur eſſe in almugea lune. Deductoria.i.fecuritate planete. boc ductoria planete.i.vt fit planeta in ſuo haiz.i.in parte fibi ppia z aliquo angulorum afcendentisiz aliquod luminarium: fimiliter in loco fibi confimili in quadrante videlicet in aliquo an gulo ita fit planeta in die orientalis a fole:in nocte occidentas lis a luna z omnis planeta dicitur effe in ſua ductoria 13 quofda cum fuerit inter planetam orientalem z folem.60.gra.Et omnis planeta ex quo tegiť a radijs folis donec appareat de ſubradijs vocat cõbustusiz duz incipit intrare radios dr incepiffe coburi:z dum abſcodiť fub radijs z fuerit ppe folem p.12.gra,dicit oppreffus z cu fuerit cu fole in vno gradu fuerints inter eos.i6.minuta:vel infra z latitudo eius fikr vocat vnitus:z cú tráfie, rit eandem vnioné petés exitú donec videať vocat euafus. Et ex quo appas rent tres altiores de ſubradijo z incipiút oxiri.i.apparere mane ante folem: boc eft cú fuerint ppingores circulo bemiſperij orientalis Donec veniát ad oppofitioné vocant oriétales dextriz ex quo tráfierit oppofitioné donec có ingătur iterú foli vocatur occidétalis Aniſtri. Uenus vo z Mercuri ex quo Tepat aliqs cop a gradib folis in medio retrogradatióis ſuerz apparet dos nec coburant a fole i ſua directióe vocatur onétales: 2 ex quo ſepant a fole in medio directõis fue dóec iterú cóburáť in retrogradatõe ſua vocat occis détales:7 cú fuerit in oztu fuo.i.cu fuerit oriétales vocáť dextri:z direé maf. z occiduo fuo.i.cú fuerit occidétales vocát finiſtriz dir eé feminei: z túc für fortiores. Zres vo altiores poftqz exeût de subradijs folis vocanť oriéta les z dir augmétari in fortitudine vſqz ad.30.gra.a ſole z vocáť post k viqs ad.30.gra.alios oriétales fortes vel fortiores:z cú tráfierint folé.60.gra.vo cantur planete oriétales eûtes ad debilitaté donec veniát ad retrogradatio nem túc noiant oriétales retrogradi:Donec fint a veniat in oppoſitoe:7 poft boc erút occidétales retrogradi cú tráfierint oppoſities:donec pueniat ad Directioné:deinde a directiõe vſqz ad.60.gra.poft directidez donec fit logis tudo eop a fole.30.graduŭ dicunt occidétales fortes:deinde a longitudine 60.gra.poft directioné dicunt occidétales eûtes ad debilitate:deinde fiunt occidétales debiles donec intrét fub radijs fol. Inferiores quos ex quo ſepant a fole:z ipſi retrogradi vocanť oriétales debiles:ficos effe no defiftit Donec veniat ad directidez ſua:z túc fiût oriétales fortes:donec logitudo eo rú fit a fole ficut logitudo folis ab eis fuit du cepiffent debilitari cutes ad re trogradatões:deinde fiút oriétales debiles dõec fupponát radijo folis poft boc fút cõiuncti:deinde cobuſti euntes ad apparitioez donec videatur:z ex quo feparant a fole in directõe vſqz ad.60.gradus fút fotiores:z 2.60.v ad boza fue retrogradatiõis erűt occidétales fortes:z donec fuerint cu fole erunt debiles: planeta cu exierit de ſubradys ſolis:z nulli planete iunctua fuerit dicek o fit in lumine ſuo;boc eft effe planetay cum luminaribus.
translation: The third difference is in what happens to the seven planets in themselves and what happens to them mutually. Since, by the grace of God, we have already discussed the state of the planets in themselves and what they signify, let us proceed to narrate what happens to them in themselves and mutually, that is, from some to some. The signification of the planets in themselves is that if a planet is ascending in the circle of its apogee, it is lesser in light, magnitude, and speed, that is, when there are less than 90 degrees between it and its apogee, either before or after. If there are exactly 90 degrees between it and its apogee, it will be equal in light, magnitude, and speed. But if it is outside these two places, i.e., more than 90 degrees, it will be descending from the circle of the apogee and will appear greater in light and magnitude and swifter in speed. If its portion is equal to less than 180 degrees, it will be increasing in number. And if it is more, it will be decreasing in number. And if its portion is equal to 180 degrees or 360 degrees, it will neither increase nor decrease. And if the equation added to the mean course is called increasing the course or number, and if its equation is decreased, it is called decreasing the number. And when any of the three superior planets goes more than its mean course, it is said to increase the course; and if it goes less, it is said to decrease the course; and if it goes just as much, it is said to be equal in course. When any of the inferior planets goes more than the mean course of the Sun, it will be increasing the course. When it goes less, it is said to be decreasing the course; and when it goes just as much, it will be equal in course. The state of the luminaries in their course is like the state of the three superior planets. A planet is also northern when it crosses its genzabar, that is, when it crosses the path of the Sun going from south to north. That transit, that is, that intersection of circles, is called genzabar. Therefore, when a planet has crossed that intersection and there are less than 90 degrees between the planet and that intersection, the planet will be ascending northern. When it is from 90 to 180 degrees, it will be descending northern. When it has passed this number, that is, when it has more, going to 270 degrees, it will be descending southern. And when it has passed this number, going to 360 degrees, it will be ascending southern. This is the state of the planets in themselves. Let us now discuss the state of the planets mutually, that is, what happens to the five planets with respect to the luminaries. And we will say from this what Ptolemy said about almuten, that is, about seeing each other face to face. This is when it is between a planet and the Sun, while the planet is occidental, that is, while it follows the Sun, as much as is between the house of that planet and the house of the Sun in signs. Or when it is between that planet and the Moon, when it is oriental from the Moon, that is, while it succeeds the Moon, as much as is between the house of the planet and the house of the Moon in signs. That is, when the planet is as distant from the Sun as its house is distant from the Sun's house. Similarly, it is said of the Moon: when a planet succeeds the Sun and Saturn is 6 signs from the Sun, Jupiter 5, Mars 4, Venus 3, Mercury 2, then it is said to be in the almuten of the Sun. Similarly, when this number of signs for each planet is between any planet and the succeeding Moon, it is said to be in the almuten of the Moon. The security of a planet, that is, that a planet is in its hayz, that is, in its proper part and in one of the angles of the ascendant and one of the luminaries; similarly in a place similar to it in the quadrant, namely in an angle, so that the planet is oriental from the Sun by day, occidental from the Moon by night. And every planet is said to be in its security by some when there are 60 degrees between the oriental planet and the Sun. And every planet, from when it is covered by the Sun's rays until it appears from under the rays, is called combust. And when it begins to enter the rays, it is said to have begun to be combust. And when it is hidden under the rays and is near the Sun by 12 degrees, it is called oppressed. And when it is with the Sun in one degree, and there are 16 minutes or less between them, and its latitude is similar, it is called united. And when it has passed that union, seeking exit until it is seen, it is called escaped. And from when the three superior planets appear from under the rays and begin to rise, that is, appear in the morning before the Sun, this is when they are closer to the circle of the eastern hemisphere until they come to opposition, they are called oriental dextrous. And from when they have passed opposition until they conjunct the Sun again, they are called occidental sinister. Venus and Mercury, from when either of them separates from the degrees of the Sun in the middle of its retrogradation and appears until they are combust by the Sun in their direct motion, are called oriental. And from when they separate from the Sun in the middle of their direct motion until they are combust again in their retrogradation, they are called occidental. And when they are in their rising, that is, when they are oriental, they are called dextrous and direct masculine. And in their setting, that is, when they are occidental, they are called sinister and direct feminine. And then they are stronger. The three superior planets, after they emerge from under the Sun's rays, are called oriental and are said to increase in strength up to 30 degrees from the Sun. And after that, up to another 30 degrees, they are called strong or stronger oriental. And when they have passed the Sun by 60 degrees, they are called oriental planets going to debility until they come to retrogradation. Then they are called oriental retrograde until they are in opposition. And after this, they will be occidental retrograde when they have passed opposition until they come to direct motion. Then from direct motion up to 60 degrees after direct motion, until their longitude from the Sun is 30 degrees, they are called strong occidental. Then from a longitude of 60 degrees after direct motion, they are called occidental going to debility. Then they become weak occidental until they enter under the Sun's rays. The inferior planets, from when they separate from the Sun, and they are retrograde, are called weak oriental. They do not cease to be so until they come to their direct motion. And then they become strong oriental until their longitude from the Sun is like the Sun's longitude from them when they began to weaken going to retrogradation. Then they become weak oriental until they are under the Sun's rays. After this, they are conjunct. Then combust, going to appearance until they are seen. And from when they separate from the Sun in direct motion up to 60 degrees, they are stronger. And from 60 degrees up to the time of their retrogradation, they will be strong occidental. And as long as they are with the Sun, they will be weak. When a planet has emerged from under the Sun's rays and is not joined to any other planet, it is said to be in its own light. This is the state of the planets with the luminaries.

author: Alchabitius
title: Planetary Aspects and Conjunctions
text: De bis que accidunt planctio ad ſeinuicem. She autem illorum erga ſeinuicem hoc est idemo cõiunctio.i. cum fuerint duo planete in duobus fignis afpicientible z fue rit leuior in figno fuo minus gradibus q3 fuerit ponderofioz in figno fuo:fuerintqz inter eos.6.gra.vel infra:tunc dicit q leui or eat ad cóiunctionem ponderofionis:z cu gradus cop fuerint equales perficitur coniunctio cop: cum tráfierit eum erit ab eo feparatus cóiunctio bec dicitur cóiunctio longitudinis. Coiunctio vo latitudinis é: vt duo planete iungitur per latitudinem.Et fi fuerit applicatio cõunctióis oportet:yt fit latitudo eorum equalis in vna parte:z si fuerit cõiunctio ex op pofitione oportet vt fint latitudines comm equales:ita vt latitudo vnius fit afcendens in feptétrione:z alterius defcendés in ſeptétrione vel vnius afcé dens in meridie:z alterius deſcédés in meridie:aut fi fuerit applicatio ex ali quo aliorum afpectuum:boc eft & fit latitudo vnius ſeptétrionalis aſcédés: z alterius meridiana Descendensiz ecouerso in meridierz bec est applicatio latitudinis que dicta est. Et cú ſeparatur vnus planeta ab alio z nulli plas netarumiungitur özdiu in eodem ſigno fuerit dicitur curfu vacuus. Et cum fuerit planeta in aliquo ſignoiz aliquis planeta no afpexerit hoc fignú alter planeta oždiu in eodem fuerit dicitur feralis vel agreftis. Et cum ſe paratur planeta leuis a planeta ponderofioriz iunctus fuerit alteri.traffert naturam primi ad ſecundum. Tranſfert etiam planeta naturam alio modo boc est vt planeta leuis iungatur planete ponderofiozife:z ipe ponderofioz iterum alteri ſe ponderofiozi:tunc melius transfer naturam leuioris ad pós derofiorem. Si autem non iungitur vnus istorum alteriz iungitur vter 03 alteri:tunc ſi aſpexerit ille tertius planeta aliquem locorum circuli reddit lumen eorum ad locum ipfum. hoc vocatur redditus luminis. Iterum fe vnus planeta non iungitur alteri:ſed alter planeta tertius in vtrofqz traſtn lit lumen:tunc bec coniunctio dicitur etiam redditus luminis. Sequitur prohibitio z fit duobus modis. Uno.f.ex coniuctione hoc cum fuerint tres planete in vno ſigno:ſed in diuerfis gradibusz fuerit põderofios plus gra dibus:tunc ille qui est medius phibet priorem illum.f.qui est minusgradis bus ne iungatur ponderofiori donec pertranfeat eum. Secundo modo vt duo planete fint in vno fignorr leuioriungatur ponderofiozi:alter quoqgiú gatur eidem ponderofiori per aſpectum:ille ergo qui est cum eo in vno ſis gno afpicientem probibet a ponderofioris cóiunctioneſi fuerint tamé gras dus illius quiiungituriz ipfius qui afpicit equales.i.vnius numeri. Si vero ille qui afpicit fuerit propios gradui ponderofionis erit coniunctio afpicien tis. Et fi coniungitur planeta domino illius figni in quo fuerit: vel domino exaltationis feu domino ceterarum dignitatum in quibus fuerit: dicit puls fare idest mittere naturam illius planete Domini.f.eiuſdez dignitatis adeŭ. Eth fuerit planeta in aliqua dignitati fuarutu z fuerit iunctus alij plane te qui babeat etiam partem dignitatis in eodem locomittit ei etiam vtran naturam fuam:fcilicet z illius cui iungitur; bec miffio omnis vocatur al cobolidest receptio. Unc fequitur redditus.i.quado iungitur planeta alicui planete qui cóburitur.i.q fuerit ſub radijs folis vel fuerit retrogradus redditos ei virtutem pp debilitatem ſua quá non valet retinere: tunc fi fuerint vtriqs planete in angulo vel fuccedétibus angulozum erit redditus cumpficuo.fimiliter fi fuerit qui coniungit ei tátum in angulo:z ille cui cóiungitur receperit eum. Si aute fuerit plane ta qiungit cadés ab angulo z ille cui cóiungit in angulo vel in ſequenti an guloum:aut fi vtriqs fuerint cadentes erit redditus cum detrimento. Nde fequitur almenē.i.refrenatio que fit quado planeta vult có iungi alteri:ſed anteqžiungať accidit retrogradatio z fic destrui tur eius cõiunctio. Panc fequit alichorad.i.cótrarietas accis dens:bec fit cum aliquis planeta leuis fuerit multox graduum in figno z alter illo ponderofios minus gradibus tertius quoqz leuior primo volens cóiungi ponderohorised anteqz ei coniungatur fit re trogradus ille leuior qui habet plures gradus 7 iungiť z applicatur illi pós derofo.z tranfiens illum iungitur etiam alteri planete leuion: z fic destruiť coniunctio illius prioris cum ponderofo. S Equitur alfazim.i.frustratio.b quos fit cum aliqs planeta petit con iunctionem alterius planete.fed anteqz perueniat ad eum mutatur iſte in aliud fignum z erit aliquis planeta in paucis gradibus afpis ciens ipſum fignum erunt radij eius in initio figni.Luzexierit ſequés pla neta de primo ligno iungitur ifti afpicienti z annullatur coniunctio quá bas bebat cum illo.f.cum primo. 7 Inc fequitur abfcifio luminis boc eft quádo aliquis planeta pes tit cõiunctionem alterius z fuerit in ſecundo figno a figno illius cui iungitur alter planeta:ſed anteqžiungatur ei pus fit ille qui est in fecúdo fignoretrogradus.coniungiturqs ei z abfcindit lus men'ſuum a planeta qui volebat cõiungi ei:fimiliter fi fuerit pla neta iens ad cóiunctionem alterius planete:z ipſe alter planeta cui vult iűs gi petat cóiunctionem alterius planete ſe poderofiozis:fed anteqz pueniat levis ad gradus ponderofionisiungitur ipſe ponderofus alteri ſeipſo póde roſtori:z abfcindit lumen illius a planeta primo leuion. De locis fortunatis z malis. Unt quoqz bis planetis loca in quibus cofortantur z in quibus debilitatur: loca in quibus fiunt fortune:z loca in quibus fiunt mali. Sed loca in gbus fiunt fortune funt B:vt.f. fint in aſpecti bus bonopi.in aliquo aſpectu vtili.f.fextili:autin trino afpectu qui a qbufdam ita nominatur exagona radiatio quos ac trigona: z vt fint mali cadentes ab eis fintqz ſeparati ab ifortuna:z iuncti fortune:aut fint ob ſeſſi a fortunis vel radijs comm.i.vt fint iter duas fouunas vel inter radios fortunay. Chec eft.n. obseffio yt babeat planeta fortunam vel radios eius
translation: Regarding what happens to planets in relation to each other. Their relation to each other is conjunction, that is, when two planets are in two aspecting signs, and the lighter planet is in its sign with fewer degrees than the heavier planet in its sign, and there are 6 degrees or less between them, then it is said that the lighter planet goes to the conjunction of the heavier one. And when their degrees are equal, their conjunction is perfected. When it passes it, it will be separated from it. This conjunction is called a conjunction of longitude. A conjunction of latitude is when two planets are joined by latitude. And if there is an application of conjunction, their latitude must be equal in one part. And if there is a conjunction by opposition, their latitudes must be equal, so that the latitude of one is ascending in the north and the other descending in the north, or one ascending in the south and the other descending in the south. Or if there is an application from any other aspect, this means that the latitude of one is northern ascending and the other southern descending, and vice versa in the south. And this is the application of latitude that has been mentioned. And when one planet separates from another and is not joined to any other planet as long as it is in the same sign, it is called void of course. And when a planet is in a sign and no other planet aspects this sign, as long as it is in the same sign, it is called feral or wild. And when a lighter planet separates from a heavier planet and is joined to another, it transfers the nature of the first to the second. A planet also transfers nature in another way, that is, when a lighter planet joins a heavier planet, and that heavier planet in turn joins another heavier planet, then it better transfers the nature of the lighter to the heavier. If, however, one of these is not joined to the other, but each is joined to another, then if that third planet aspects any place in the circle, it returns their light to that place. This is called the return of light. Again, if one planet is not joined to another, but a third planet transfers light to both, then this conjunction is also called the return of light. Prohibition follows and occurs in two ways. One, namely, by conjunction, when there are three planets in one sign, but in different degrees, and the heavier one has more degrees, then the one in the middle prohibits the former, that is, the one with fewer degrees, from joining the heavier one until it passes it. In the second way, two planets are in one sign, and the lighter one joins the heavier one; another also joins the same heavier one by aspect. Therefore, the one who is with it in one sign prohibits the aspecting one from conjunction with the heavier one, if, however, the degrees of the one who joins and the one who aspects are equal, that is, of the same number. But if the one who aspects is closer to the degree of the heavier one, the conjunction will be of the aspecting one. And if a planet joins the lord of the sign in which it is, or the lord of exaltation, or the lord of other dignities in which it is, it is said to pulsate, that is, to send the nature of that planet, the lord, that is, of the same dignity, to it. And if a planet is in any of its dignities and is joined to another planet that also has a part of dignity in the same place, it also sends both its nature, namely, and that of the one to which it is joined. This entire sending is called alchobol, that is, reception.

author: Alchabitius
title: Return of Light and Refrenation
text: De bis que accidunt planctio ad ſeinuicem. She autem illorum erga ſeinuicem hoc est idemo cõiunctio.i. cum fuerint duo planete in duobus fignis afpicientible z fue rit leuior in figno fuo minus gradibus q3 fuerit ponderofioz in figno fuo:fuerintqz inter eos.6.gra.vel infra:tunc dicit q leui or eat ad cóiunctionem ponderofionis:z cu gradus cop fuerint equales perficitur coniunctio cop: cum tráfierit eum erit ab eo feparatus cóiunctio bec dicitur cóiunctio longitudinis. Si aute fuerit plane ta qiungit cadés ab angulo z ille cui cóiungit in angulo vel in ſequenti an guloum:aut fi vtriqs fuerint cadentes erit redditus cum detrimento. Nde fequitur almenē.i.refrenatio que fit quado planeta vult có iungi alteri:ſed anteqžiungať accidit retrogradatio z fic destrui tur eius cõiunctio. translation: Now follows the return, that is, when a planet joins another planet that is combust, that is, under the sun's rays, or is retrograde, it returns its virtue due to its weakness, which it cannot retain. Then, if both planets are in an angle or succeeding angles, the return will be with profit. Similarly, if the one joining it is only in an angle, and the one to whom it joins has received it. But if the planet joining is cadent from an angle, and the one to whom it joins is in an angle or in a succeeding angle, or if both are cadent, the return will be with detriment. Now follows almenē, that is, refrenation, which occurs when a planet wants to join another, but before it joins, retrogradation occurs, and thus its conjunction is destroyed.

author: Alchabitius
title: Alichorad and Alfazim
text: De bis que accidunt planctio ad ſeinuicem. She autem illorum erga ſeinuicem hoc est idemo cõiunctio.i. cum fuerint duo planete in duobus fignis afpicientible z fue rit leuior in figno fuo minus gradibus q3 fuerit ponderofioz in figno fuo:fuerintqz inter eos.6.gra.vel infra:tunc dicit q leui or eat ad cóiunctionem ponderofionis:z cu gradus cop fuerint equales perficitur coniunctio cop: cum tráfierit eum erit ab eo feparatus cóiunctio bec dicitur cóiunctio longitudinis. Coiunctio vo latitudinis é: vt duo planete iungitur per latitudinem.Et fi fuerit applicatio cõunctióis oportet:yt fit latitudo eorum equalis in vna parte:z si fuerit cõiunctio ex op pofitione oportet vt fint latitudines comm equales:ita vt latitudo vnius fit afcendens in feptétrione:z alterius defcendés in ſeptétrione vel vnius afcé dens in meridie:z alterius deſcédés in meridie:aut fi fuerit applicatio ex ali quo aliorum afpectuum:boc eft & fit latitudo vnius ſeptétrionalis aſcédés: z alterius meridiana Descendensiz ecouerso in meridierz bec est applicatio latitudinis que dicta est. Et cú ſeparatur vnus planeta ab alio z nulli plas netarumiungitur özdiu in eodem ſigno fuerit dicitur curfu vacuus. Et cum fuerit planeta in aliquo ſignoiz aliquis planeta no afpexerit hoc fignú alter planeta oždiu in eodem fuerit dicitur feralis vel agreftis. Et cum ſe paratur planeta leuis a planeta ponderofioriz iunctus fuerit alteri.traffert naturam primi ad ſecundum. Tranſfert etiam planeta naturam alio modo boc est vt planeta leuis iungatur planete ponderofiozife:z ipe ponderofioz iterum alteri ſe ponderofiozi:tunc melius transfer naturam leuioris ad pós derofiorem. Si autem non iungitur vnus istorum alteriz iungitur vter 03 alteri:tunc ſi aſpexerit ille tertius planeta aliquem locorum circuli reddit lumen eorum ad locum ipfum. hoc vocatur redditus luminis. Iterum fe vnus planeta non iungitur alteri:ſed alter planeta tertius in vtrofqz traſtn lit lumen:tunc bec coniunctio dicitur etiam redditus luminis. Sequitur prohibitio z fit duobus modis. Uno.f.ex coniuctione hoc cum fuerint tres planete in vno ſigno:ſed in diuerfis gradibusz fuerit põderofios plus gra dibus:tunc ille qui est medius phibet priorem illum.f.qui est minusgradis bus ne iungatur ponderofiori donec pertranfeat eum. Secundo modo vt duo planete fint in vno fignorr leuioriungatur ponderofiozi:alter quoqgiú gatur eidem ponderofiori per aſpectum:ille ergo qui est cum eo in vno ſis gno afpicientem probibet a ponderofioris cóiunctioneſi fuerint tamé gras dus illius quiiungituriz ipfius qui afpicit equales.i.vnius numeri. Si vero ille qui afpicit fuerit propios gradui ponderofionis erit coniunctio afpicien tis. Et fi coniungitur planeta domino illius figni in quo fuerit: vel domino exaltationis feu domino ceterarum dignitatum in quibus fuerit: dicit puls fare idest mittere naturam illius planete Domini.f.eiuſdez dignitatis adeŭ. Eth fuerit planeta in aliqua dignitati fuarutu z fuerit iunctus alij plane te qui babeat etiam partem dignitatis in eodem locomittit ei etiam vtran naturam fuam:fcilicet z illius cui iungitur; bec miffio omnis vocatur al cobolidest receptio. Unc fequitur redditus.i.quado iungitur planeta alicui planete qui cóburitur.i.q fuerit ſub radijs folis vel fuerit retrogradus redditos ei virtutem pp debilitatem ſua quá non valet retinere: tunc fi fuerint vtriqs planete in angulo vel fuccedétibus angulozum erit redditus cumpficuo.fimiliter fi fuerit qui coniungit ei tátum in angulo:z ille cui cóiungitur receperit eum. Si aute fuerit plane ta qiungit cadés ab angulo z ille cui cóiungit in angulo vel in ſequenti an guloum:aut fi vtriqs fuerint cadentes erit redditus cum detrimento. Nde fequitur almenē.i.refrenatio que fit quado planeta vult có iungi alteri:ſed anteqžiungať accidit retrogradatio z fic destrui tur eius cõiunctio. Panc fequit alichorad.i.cótrarietas accis dens:bec fit cum aliquis planeta leuis fuerit multox graduum in figno z alter illo ponderofios minus gradibus tertius quoqz leuior primo volens cóiungi ponderohorised anteqz ei coniungatur fit re trogradus ille leuior qui habet plures gradus 7 iungiť z applicatur illi pós derofo.z tranfiens illum iungitur etiam alteri planete leuion: z fic destruiť coniunctio illius prioris cum ponderofo. S Equitur alfazim.i.frustratio.b quos fit cum aliqs planeta petit con iunctionem alterius planete.fed anteqz perueniat ad eum mutatur iſte in aliud fignum z erit aliquis planeta in paucis gradibus afpis ciens ipſum fignum erunt radij eius in initio figni.Luzexierit ſequés pla neta de primo ligno iungitur ifti afpicienti z annullatur coniunctio quá bas bebat cum illo.f.cum primo. 7 Inc fequitur abfcifio luminis boc eft quádo aliquis planeta pes tit cõiunctionem alterius z fuerit in ſecundo figno a figno illius cui iungitur alter planeta:ſed anteqžiungatur ei pus fit ille qui est in fecúdo fignoretrogradus.coniungiturqs ei z abfcindit lus men'ſuum a planeta qui volebat cõiungi ei:fimiliter fi fuerit pla neta iens ad cóiunctionem alterius planete:z ipſe alter planeta cui vult iűs gi petat cóiunctionem alterius planete ſe poderofiozis:fed anteqz pueniat levis ad gradus ponderofionisiungitur ipſe ponderofus alteri ſeipſo póde roſtori:z abfcindit lumen illius a planeta primo leuion. De locis fortunatis z malis. Unt quoqz bis planetis loca in quibus cofortantur z in quibus debilitatur: loca in quibus fiunt fortune:z loca in quibus fiunt mali. Sed loca in gbus fiunt fortune funt B:vt.f. fint in aſpecti bus bonopi.in aliquo aſpectu vtili.f.fextili:autin trino afpectu qui a qbufdam ita nominatur exagona radiatio quos ac trigona: z vt fint mali cadentes ab eis fintqz ſeparati ab ifortuna:z iuncti fortune:aut fint ob ſeſſi a fortunis vel radijs comm.i.vt fint iter duas fouunas vel inter radios fortunay. Chec eft.n. obseffio yt babeat planeta fortunam vel radios eius
translation: Now follows alichorad, that is, accidental contrariety. This occurs when a lighter planet has many degrees in a sign, and another, heavier one, has fewer degrees, and a third, lighter than the first, wanting to join the heavier one, but before it joins it, that lighter one which has more degrees becomes retrograde and joins and applies to that heavier one. And passing it, it also joins another lighter planet, and thus the conjunction of the former with the heavier one is destroyed. Now follows alfazim, that is, frustration. This occurs when a planet seeks the conjunction of another planet, but before it reaches it, this one changes into another sign, and there will be some planet in few degrees aspecting that sign, and its rays will be at the beginning of the sign. When the succeeding planet leaves the first sign, it joins this aspecting one, and the conjunction it had with that one, that is, with the first, is annulled.

title: Abscission of Light

translation: Now follows the abscission of light. This is when a planet seeks the conjunction of another, and is in the second sign from the sign of the one to which another planet joins, but before it joins it, the one in the second sign becomes retrograde. It joins it and cuts off its light from the planet that wanted to join it. Similarly, if a planet is going to the conjunction of another planet, and that other planet to which it wants to join seeks the conjunction of another planet heavier than itself, but before the lighter one reaches the degrees of the heavier one, the heavier one joins another heavier than itself, and cuts off its light from the first lighter planet.

title: Fortunate and Unfortunate Places

translation: Regarding fortunate and unfortunate places. There are also places for these planets in which they are strengthened and in which they are weakened: places in which fortunes occur, and places in which misfortunes occur. But the places in which fortunes occur are these: that they are in good aspects, that is, in some useful aspect, that is, a sextile, or in a trine aspect, which by some is called hexagonal radiation and trigonal; and that the malefics are cadent from them, and are separated from misfortune, and joined to fortune; or are besieged by fortunes or their rays, that is, that they are between two fortunes or between the rays of fortunes. For this is obsession, that a planet has fortune or its rays.

author: Alchabitius
title: Planetary Fortification and Debilitation
text: ante ſe z aliam fortunamivel eius radios poſt ſe. Et quidas vocant hoc altis fert idest veneratio: aut fint vniti cum fole in vno gradu: aut fint in afpectu etus ſextili:fiue trino vel confimili aſpectu lune z luna tunc fit fortunata:z ve curfu fint veloces aucti lumine z numero:aut fint in dignitatibus fuis vť in ſuo baim:ideft in ſua ſimilitudine vt fit videlicet placta mafculinus in figno masculino:z femininus in figno feminino 7 diurnus in die fuper terram zin nocte fub terra nocturnus in nocte fuper terrazz in die fub terra z reliqua z vt fint in fignis in quibus habuerint dignitates feu poteftates fuas: vel in gaudys fuis vřin gradibus lucidis recepti:z ex fortitudine corum eft vt fint afcendentes in ſeptentrione:aut fint feptentrionales:vel fint afcendétes in circulo augis fue: aut in statione fecunda boc est qui fuerint in ftatione vbi Dirigantur a retrogradatione:aut fint exeuntes de subradijs folis: aut fint in angulo:vel in fequenti eius:aut fint tres altiores orientales a ſole: si eu afpexerint fextili:aut trino afpectuerit illis boc augmentum fortitudinis: aut fuerint in quartis mafculinis z fol cuz fuerit in illis quartis mafculinis: aut in fignis masculinis erit fortis etiam nifi fit in libza: quia ibi cadit. Et ex fortitudine trium inferiorum est vt fint occidentales a ſole:aut in quartis fes mininis z ex fortitudine lune:vt sit in nocte ſuper terraz z in die fub terra in loco feminino; vel in ſigno feminino:7 cum fuerit in exaltatione ſolis. De infortunio planetarum. Xinfortunio vero planetarum a destructione eorum est vt sint in coniunctione malorum:aut in oppofitione eozum: vel in cos rum tetragona:aut trigona:aut exagona radiatióe:aut fi fuerit inter eos i corpus mali vel radios eius minus termino planes te:aut fuerint in terminis malorum:aut domib corum:aut fint mali eleuati fuper eos a.r.vel.ri.a locis comzz multo deterius fi non reces perint eos. Aut fuerint in coniunctione folis:vel oppofitione eius:vel in tes tragona radiatione:aut fuerit in capitibus fuozum genzabar:aut cum cau dis fuis:aut cum capite draconis vel cauda fintos inter eos 7 inter aliquem iftorum fcilicet locorum.22.gradus vel infra:z maxime fi fuerit luna in alis quo eorum:fimiliter z fol tunc magis impeditur ab eis.i.a capite vel cauda cum fuerint inter ipſum z vnum eorum etiam.4.grad. ante vel retro:aut fuerint planeta obſeſſi inter duos malos: hoc est vt fit planeta in ſigno ali quo z cum eo malus:vel radij elus ante ſeiz malus viradij eius poſt ſe:aut ſeperetur a malo per coniunctionem:vel per aſpectum:ziungatur tali mo do alteri malo:aut fuerit malus: vel radij eius in figno quod est ante eum: idest in fecundo ab eo:z in ſigno quod eſt poſt euz:ideft in.12.ab eo fuerit als ter malus:vel radij eius. Similiter dicitur de fignis & fint obſeſſa. Quod fi afpexerit fortuna vel fol eundem planetam obſeſſum:vel fignum obseffum a trino:vel a fextili aſpectu fueritqz inter eú z coniunctioné minus ſepté gra dibus foluit ipſa malicia vel obseffio.Et ex hoc vt fit planeta retrograa
translation: before it, or another benefic or its rays behind it. And some call this elevation, that is, veneration: or they are united with the Sun in one degree: or they are in its sextile aspect: or trine or similar aspect of the Moon, and the Moon is then fortunate: and they are swift in course, augmented in light and number: or they are in their dignities, as in their own terms, that is, in their own likeness, so that a masculine planet is in a masculine sign: and a feminine planet in a feminine sign, and a diurnal planet in the day above the earth and in the night below the earth, a nocturnal planet in the night above the earth and in the day below the earth, and the rest, and that they are in signs in which they have dignities or their powers: or in their joys, or in bright degrees received: and from their strength it is that they are ascending in the north: or they are northern: or they are ascending in the circle of their apogee: or in the second station, that is, those which are in the station where they are directed from retrogradation: or they are emerging from under the Sun's rays: or they are in an angle: or in its succedent: or they are three degrees higher and oriental from the Sun: if the Sun aspects them with a sextile: or trine aspect, this will be an increase of strength for them: or they are in masculine quarters and the Sun, when it is in those masculine quarters: or in masculine signs, it will also be strong unless it is in Libra: because there it falls. And from the strength of the three inferior planets it is that they are occidental from the Sun: or in feminine quarters, and from the strength of the Moon: that it is in the night above the earth and in the day below the earth in a feminine place; or in a feminine sign: and when it is in the exaltation of the Sun. Concerning the misfortune of the planets. Indeed, concerning the misfortune of the planets and their destruction, it is that they are in conjunction with malefics: or in opposition to them: or in their square: or trine: or sextile radiation: or if there is between them a malefic body or its rays less than the term of the planet: or they are in the terms of malefics: or their houses: or malefics are elevated above them from the 10th or 11th places, and much worse if they do not receive them. Or they are in conjunction with the Sun: or its opposition: or in square radiation: or it is in the heads of their genitures: or with their tails: or with the head of the Dragon or the tail, or there are between them and any of these, namely, places, 22 degrees or less: and especially if the Moon is in any of them: similarly, the Sun is then more hindered by them, that is, by the head or tail when they are between it and one of them, even 4 degrees before or behind: or the planets are besieged between two malefics: this is that the planet is in some sign and with it a malefic: or its rays before it, and a malefic or its rays behind it: or it is separated from a malefic by conjunction: or by aspect: and is joined in such a way to another malefic: or a malefic: or its rays are in the sign which is before it: that is, in the second from it: and in the sign which is after it: that is, in the 12th from it, there is another malefic: or its rays. Similarly, it is said of signs that they are besieged. But if a benefic or the Sun aspects the same besieged planet: or besieged sign from a trine: or from a sextile aspect, and there is between it and the conjunction less than seven degrees, that malice or siege is dissolved. And from this, that the planet is retrograde.

author: Alchabitius
title: Planetary Debilities and Affections
text: ==Start of OCR for page 1== dus:vel fub radijs folis combuſtus:aut cadens ab afcendente:aut ex debis litate eorum:vt fint tardi curſu:aut in ſtatione prima:boc est quando ſtant: vt retrogradi fiant:aut fint in gradibus tenebrosis:aut fint masculini i fignis femininis:7 gradibus femininis in die fub terra:z in nocte fuper terram: aut fint feminini in ſignis masculinis:z in gradibus mafculinis in nocte fub terra:z in die fuper terram:aut fint in oppofitione dignitatum fuarum:feu poreftatum:aut defcendentes in meridie vel meridiani:aut cadentes ab an gulis:vel a fuccedentibus angulorum:aut in domibus cadentibus:aut fint in via combusta que eſt medietas vltima libre:z pma medietas ſcorpionis: aut iungantur planete retrogrado vel impedito feu cadenti:aut non fint re cepti. Aut fint tres altiores a folo occidentales: aut in quartis femininis: z Debilitas folis est:vt fit in fignis femininis:aut in quartis femininis:nifi fit in domo nona:z debilitas trium inferiozum eft vt fint orientales:aut in quar, tis mafculinis. De amicicia z odio planetarum. I dicitur in planetis o fint quidam eorum ſe inuicem diligens tes 7 odientes:nam de diligentibus quidam antiquorum dixes runt & Jupiter diligit omnes planetas z ſit amicus coz 'z ipfi illius preter martem.Ueueris amici funt omnes planete z om nes diligunt eum preter faturnum.Saturni autem amici fűt Jupiter Sol z Luna z inimici eius Mars z Uenus zipſa plus babet eum odio. Et amica martis eſt Uenus z ceteri planete odio habent euz 2 plus Jupiter z Sol. Solis vero amici funt Jupiter z Uenus:7 inimici Mars Mercurius z Luna. Mercurij amici funt Jupiter Uenus: 2 Saturnus tantum:7 inimici eius funt Sol z Luna z Mars.Lune autem amici fűt Jus piter z Uenus z Saturnus:z inimici Mars 7 Mercurius. Lapitis draсо nis amici funt Jupiter z Uenus:zinimici Saturnus z Mars. Caude vero amici Saturnus 7 Mars:inimici Sol z Luna Jupiter z Uenus. Et füt alie ſpecies inimicicie.cum fuerint duo planete habentes domos fuas oppofis tas:vt Mars z Uenus: 7 cum coniunguntur dicitur apertio portarum. Zertia quos ſpecies inimicicie est vt fint duorum planetarum exaltationes opposite.Fortior autem planetarum amicicia eſt:vt concordet planeta cum planeta in natura z in qualitate atqz ſubſtantia z poteftate ficut concordat Mars cum fole:quia vieras concordat in caliditate z ficcitate z acuitione z celeritate: est dominus exaltationis eius in qua apparet eius fotitudo:7 vti concordat Lunar Uenus in frigore z humiditate:7 est domina eraltas tionis eius.Cum autem concordauerunt duo planete in natura z ſubſtatia: vt Jupiter z Uenus funt amici. ==End of OCR for page 1== ==Start of OCR for page 2== Differentia quarta in expofitione nominum aftrologorum.ranb I proſequamur núc expoſitionem nominű astrologomm. Initium borum est. Loniunctior res ſignificantes destrus ctiões feu mutationes que fiunt in boc feculo ex cõiunctios nibus:quay numerus est fex:maior autem omnibus cóiun ctionib eft coniunctio faturní z iouis in initio arietis z b fit in.960.anis. Secunda aut cop piunctio eft in initio vnis ufcuiufqs triplicitatis z b fit i.240.anis. Jungútur.n.in vnaquas triplicitate duodecies:z fortaffis faciút in vnaquaqs triplicitate, 13. piunctiones:deinde mutat eop coniunctio ad triplicitatez que huic fuccedit. Lertia auté con iunctio eft faturní z martis in initio cancriq fit in oibus 30.anis. Quarta quoqz eft coniuctio iouis z faturni in vnoquoqz ſigno q fit in oibº. 20.anis. Quita é defcéio luminaris maioris in púctu egnoctij vernalis tpis.f.ins greffus folis i capite arietis que fit i omni áno. Sexta eft piuntio luminas rium z oppofitio eorum que fit in dimidio cuiusqz menfis lunaris. Et quádo dicitur afcendés cóiunctionis dicit vel fignificat afcendens mundi.i.fignuz quod afcédit bora introitus folis in predictű punctum egnoctij vernalis in initio.f.illius anni in quo debet fieri coniunctio predicta. De annimodar.i.gradus afcendétis nativitatúinueſtigatione. Tex boc annimodar qo eſt inueftigatio gradus aſcédétis alicui nas tiuitatis:7 putant multi astrologorum & inueniatur peundem idem gradus aſcédétis natiuitatis alicuius:fed hoc falfum eft:z bociá pa tefeci in libro meo quem feci de annimodar:ſed inuenit peudem dignios ois bus gradibus circuli poſt gradum afcendétis in eadem boza km cursuz nas turalem:z cócordat multoties.i.accidit aliquádo q inueniat peum gradus afcendétis. Luius rei cognitio eft vt cófideret gradus cóiunctióis vel grad preventionis a fuerit ante ipſaz natiuitaté: 7 fi fuerit piunctio illi natiuitatip pioz vocatur ipſa natiuitas piunctionalis: fi fuerit preuétio illi ppios dicit preuétionalis.i.in qualicuqs cop fuerit natiuitas in cõiunctione.f.vel preuven tione eodem mo z noie natiuitas pdicta núcupaturiz qe gradus cõiunctios nis eſt idem gradus in quo iungūtur luminaria nulla eſt inquifitio:nec indis get expoſitive:in preuétione aut qe vnüquodos luminarium eft in gradu ſuo idest in diuerfis gradibus. Neceſſe eſt nobis fcire qué boy graduŭ velit itel ligi gradum preuétionis: z iam dixit Ptolemeus: qe gradus illius lumina ris qo fuerit fuper terram est gradus preuétionisiz gda sSapiétum dixerunt si evenerit in preuétione o vnú luminariű fit in gradu oviétis z alterum in gradu occidétis:tunc gradus oriētis erit gradus preuétionis. Et dixit Uellis us:qu gradus preuétionis est gradus in quo fit impletio volés intelligi gra dum lune:fed quia bocé opus Ptolemei oportet in boc nos redire ad ei? fentétiam.cum ergo gradus cóiunctióis vel gradus preuétionis certiffime patuerit cóftitues gra.afcédétem p extimatidez hore natiuitatis 7 quattuor angulos z reliquas domos:deinde afpicies gra.cóiunctióis:vel gradum ps ==End of OCR for page 2== ==Start of OCR for page 3== ventionis que fuerit ante natiuitatemiz go planetay in eo fuerit dignior vel fortior in eadem bora cóiunctionis vel puentionis per multitudiné dignita tum feu poteſtatú.i.cófiderabis qs planetap babeat mai dominiu in eodez loco. Punc ergo planetá equabis ad boza opinatá natiuitatis.deinde afpis cies vtruz sit gradus illius planete in figno in quo eft ppior gradui.x.dom aut gradui afcendétis z cui horú ppios fuerit facies buncanguluzad iſtar gradus ipfius planete rei minutiiz diuides.rii.domus per eŭ dixit Ptoles meus fi planete plures cóuenerint in dominio eiufdem loci z fuerint equas les in fortitudine coſtitues dominum loci illum qui fuerit dis baim:qfi in boc ctiá cóuenerint cóſtitues eum qui velocius 03 mutari ab effe fuo ad id qo fuerit melius.i.eu q voluerit exire de aliquo figno in quo fuerit:z intrare aliud in quo babuerit plures dignitates:vel fi fuerint oriétales a fole eliges eű a foli fuerit ppinqozz no fuerit fub radijs. Si vo in angulo fuerit ille eft eligédus q gradui eiufdem anguli fuerit ppinquio:z hoc intellige. I ex hoc bylecb.i.locus vite in natinitatibus cuius scientia eft: vt primo afpicias bozam natiuitatis:que si fuerit in die accipies a fole:q si fuerit ante gradi ſigni afſcendentis p.5.gradus autin fra:aut fuerit in.to.vel.u.fiue masculinú fuerit ipfum fignum feu femininú aptus erit:vt fit bylech. Si aute fuerit in bis trib locis fin feptima vel octaua vel nona i ſignomas.inmikr aptus erit:yt sit bylech. Si vo fuerit in bis trib locis in figno feminino no erit aptus:yt ſit bylech. Pinc afpiciens Lunam que ſi fuerit in afcendéte vel in fecúda vel tertia:aut in feptima vel octaua erit apta bylech fiue masculinú siue femininű fuerit fis gnuni. Et si fuerit ante gradu ſigni afcendétis p.5.gradus vel infra:aut fue rint in io.vel.ii.aut in ärtavel quta vel in ſeptima in figno feminino apteť by lech. Si aut fuerit in aliquo istori in ſigno masculino non erit apta bylech it fuerit in die natiuitatis. Si vero natiuitas fuerit nocturna incipiamus a lus na que si fuerit in aliquo pdictozŭ locoru:km o pdiximus erit apta bylech. Si aút luna nó fuerit in eis afpiciés poft hoc ad fole.g si fuerit ante gra.7.p 5.gra.vel infra:aut fi fuerit in. 4. velin.5.erit aptus vi sit bylech fine fignuz fuerit masculini siue femininu.i vero fuerit ante afcendétem.5.gradib? vel infra:aut
translation: or combust under the Sun's rays, or falling from the ascendant, or from their debility, so that they are slow in course, or in their first station, that is, when they stand still to become retrograde, or are in dark degrees, or are masculine in feminine signs and feminine degrees by day under the earth, and by night above the earth; or are feminine in masculine signs and masculine degrees by night under the earth, and by day above the earth; or are in opposition to their dignities or powers; or descending in the south or meridian; or falling from the angles, or from the succedent houses of the angles; or in cadent houses; or are in the via combusta, which is the last half of Libra and the first half of Scorpio; or are joined to a retrograde, impeded, or cadent planet; or are not received. Or if the three superior planets are occidental, or in feminine quarters. And the debility of the Sun is to be in feminine signs, or in feminine quarters, unless it is in the ninth house. And the debility of the three inferior planets is to be oriental, or in masculine quarters. Concerning the friendship and hatred of the planets. It is said among the planets that some of them love and hate each other. For concerning those who love, some of the ancients said that Jupiter loves all planets and is their friend, and they are his, except Mars. The friends of Venus are all planets, and all love her except Saturn. The friends of Saturn are Jupiter, Sun, and Moon, and his enemies are Mars and Venus, and she hates him more. And the friend of Mars is Venus, and the other planets hate him, and Jupiter and Sun more. The friends of the Sun are Jupiter and Venus, and his enemies are Mars, Mercury, and Moon. The friends of Mercury are Jupiter, Venus, and Saturn only, and his enemies are Sun, Moon, and Mars. The friends of the Moon are Jupiter, Venus, and Saturn, and her enemies are Mars and Mercury. The friends of the Dragon's Head are Jupiter and Venus, and his enemies are Saturn and Mars. The friends of the Tail are Saturn and Mars, and his enemies are Sun, Moon, Jupiter, and Venus. And there are other kinds of enmity: when two planets have their houses opposite, like Mars and Venus, and when they conjoin, it is called the opening of the gates. The third kind of enmity is when the exaltations of two planets are opposite. The stronger friendship of planets is when a planet agrees with another planet in nature, quality, substance, and power, just as Mars agrees with the Sun, because both agree in heat, dryness, sharpness, and swiftness; he is the lord of his exaltation in which his strength appears. And just as the Moon agrees with Venus in coldness and humidity, and she is the lady of her exaltation. But when two planets agree in nature and substance, like Jupiter and Venus, they are friends.

title: Astrological Terms and Conjunctions
