translation: Fourth difference in the exposition of astrological names. Let us now proceed with the exposition of astrological names. The beginning of these is the conjunctions signifying destructions or changes that occur in this world from conjunctions, the number of which is six. The greatest of all conjunctions is the conjunction of Saturn and Jupiter at the beginning of Aries, and this occurs every 960 years. The second conjunction of these is at the beginning of each triplicity, and this occurs every 240 years. For they conjoin twelve times in each triplicity, and perhaps they make 13 conjunctions in each triplicity; then their conjunction changes to the triplicity that succeeds this one. The third conjunction is of Saturn and Mars at the beginning of Cancer, which occurs every 30 years. The fourth is the conjunction of Jupiter and Saturn in each sign, which occurs every 20 years. The fifth is the descent of the greater luminary at the point of the vernal equinox, that is, the Sun's ingress into the head of Aries, which occurs every year. The sixth is the conjunction of the luminaries and their opposition, which occurs in the middle of each lunar month. And when the ascendant of the conjunction is mentioned, it means or signifies the ascendant of the world, that is, the sign that ascends at the hour of the Sun's entrance into the aforesaid point of the vernal equinox, at the beginning, that is, of that year in which the aforesaid conjunction is to occur. Concerning the investigation of the annimodar, that is, the degree of the ascendant of nativities. From this annimodar, which is the investigation of the degree of the ascendant of a nativity, many astrologers think that the same degree of the ascendant of someone's nativity is found by it, but this is false, and I have already revealed this in my book which I made on the annimodar. But it finds by it the most worthy of all degrees of the circle after the degree of the ascendant at the same hour according to the natural course, and it often agrees, that is, it sometimes happens that the degree of the ascendant is found by it. The knowledge of this is to consider the degree of the conjunction or the degree of the prevention that occurred before the nativity itself. And if the conjunction was prior to that nativity, the nativity itself is called conjunctional; if the prevention was prior to it, it is called preventional. That is, in whichever of them the nativity occurred, in conjunction, that is, or prevention, in the same way and name the aforesaid nativity is called. And because the degree of the conjunction is the same degree in which the luminaries conjoin, there is no inquiry, nor does it need exposition. But in prevention, because each of the luminaries is in its own degree, that is, in different degrees, it is necessary for us to know which of these degrees is to be understood as the degree of prevention. And Ptolemy has already said that the degree of that luminary which is above the earth is the degree of prevention. And some wise men have said that if it happens in prevention that one luminary is in the degree of the orient and the other in the degree of the occident, then the degree of the orient will be the degree of prevention. And Vettius said that the degree of prevention is the degree in which the fulfillment occurs, wishing to understand the degree of the Moon. But because this is the work of Ptolemy, we must return to his opinion in this. Therefore, when the degree of the conjunction or the degree of the prevention is most certainly known, you will establish the degree of the ascendant by an estimation of the hour of the nativity and the four angles and the remaining houses. Then you will look at the degree of the conjunction or the degree of prevention that occurred before the nativity, and which of the planets was more dignified or stronger in it at the same hour of the conjunction or prevention by the multitude of dignities or powers, that is, you will consider which of the planets has greater dominion in the same place. Then you will equate that planet to the estimated hour of the nativity. Then you will see whether the degree of that planet in the sign in which it is is closer to the tenth house or to the ascendant, and to whichever of these it is closer, you will make this angle according to the degree of the planet itself, and you will divide the twelve houses by it. Ptolemy said that if several planets agree in the dominion of the same place and are equal in strength, you will establish as lord of the place that one who is the lord of the hayz. If they also agree in this, you will establish the one who is more likely to change from his state to what is better, that is, the one who wishes to leave a sign in which he is and enter another in which he has more dignities; or if they are oriental from the Sun, you will choose the one who is closest to the Sun and is not under the rays. But if it is in an angle, the one who is closest to the degree of that angle is to be chosen, and understand this. And from this, the hyleg, that is, the place of life in nativities, the knowledge of which is: first, you look at the hour of the nativity. If it is by day, you take it from the Sun. If it is before the degree of the ascendant by 5 degrees or less, or if it is in the tenth or eleventh house, whether the sign itself is masculine or feminine, it will be apt to be the hyleg. But if it is in these three places, but in the seventh or eighth or ninth house in a masculine sign, it will not be apt to be the hyleg. But if it is in these three places in a feminine sign, it will not be apt to be the hyleg. Then looking at the Moon, if it is in the ascendant or in the second or third house, or in the seventh or eighth, it will be apt to be the hyleg, whether the sign is masculine or feminine. And if it is before the degree of the ascendant by 5 degrees or less, or if it is in the tenth or eleventh, or in the fourth or fifth, or in the seventh in a feminine sign, it is apt to be the hyleg. But if it is in any of these in a masculine sign, it will not be apt to be the hyleg if it is in the day of the nativity. But if the nativity is nocturnal, we begin with the Moon, which, if it is in any of the aforesaid places, according to what we have said, will be apt to be the hyleg. But if the Moon is not in them, after this, look at the Sun. If it is before the 7th degree by 5 degrees or less, or if it is in the 4th or 5th house, it will be apt to be the hyleg, whether the sign is masculine or feminine. If it is before the ascendant by 5 degrees or less, or

author: Alchabitius
title: Determining the Hyleg
text: in afcendete:vel in ſecudo figno mas aptabit ét bylech. Si aút fue rit in aliquo iſtop locox in ligno femi.nó erit aptus bylech. Quod si lumina rium aliqo nó fuerit apti bylech. Aſpice poft hoc natiuitaté:vtrű sit coniun ctionalis aut puentionalis.i.vtrű fit poft cóiunctioné vel puentione. Quod si fuerit natiuitas cõiunctionalis z fuerit gradus cóiunctóis in aliquo angu lorum: vel in fuccedenti angulo:erit in loco apto bylech. Si vero pdictus gradus fuerit cadés ab his octo locis.i.ab angulis z a fuccedétibus angul nó erit bylech. Aſpicies túc gradu pris fortune:q si fuerit in aliquo angulor vel in fuccedétib angulis:erit in loco apto bylech. Si vero cadés fuerit ab bis.8.locis.Elfpicies post hoc gradu afcendentis 7 coſtitues eŭ bylech'. Si vo nativitas preuétionalis fuerit:incipies a gradu preventionis. Afpiciesos cum sicut prius fecisti in gradu cóiunctionis:z poſt ipſum gradu ptis fortus ne:poft ipſum gra.afcendétis eo ordine quo pdiximus in gradu coniunctios C ==End of OCR for page 3== ==Start of OCR for page 4== nis. Deſexu aut fignori no curabis.i.no afpicies vtri fint figna illa maf.vel femi.nifi tantumodo in luminaribus hoc eft in gradu cõiunctióis vel picue tionis zaſcédétis z partis fortune nó cóſideres vtrum fint in maf.fignis vel femi.tam in angulis q3 in fuccedétibus angulor:sed bec quattuor tm erunt tunc apta bylech cú fuerint in bis octo locis.i.in angulis vel fuccedentibus eozum:si vo fuerint in locis recedétib.i.cadétibus ab angulis nó erût apta bylech. Apparetitas y sol sit bylech aptus in die et nocte sup terraz z sub terra in.u.locis:sup terram in. 6.locis z sub terra in. 5.Luna quos apta erit bylech in die et nocte sup terram z sub terra similiter in.u.locis super terra in.5.2 sub terrain.6.2 cum bocfi fuerit luna in bis locis sub radys solis nó erit apta bylech. Aspiciés auté bylech in angulis z succedétibus cori secun dum o equátur.i2.Domus circuli per gradus bozarum afcédétis secúdum @ exponit eius opus in canone planetarum:idest in libro curſuu planetap. Lnqz equaueris domos hoc modo:omnis planeta qui fuerit ante gradum signi ascendetis vel cuiuslibet domus per.5.gradus vel infra erit fortitudo eius valida in domo que ei succedit:z omnis locus ex locis que pdiximus é aptus bylech:si aspexerit eum alige dominorum illius qui sit dominus do mus:aut dominus exaltationis:aut dominus termini:aut triplicitatis: aut Dominus faciei:z si non;non erit aptus bylech. De alcochoden. Texbocalcochoden qui est significator vite:idest dominus annorus vel dans anos quem cum volueris ſcire z iam prenoſti bylech co oz Adine quem predixi:afpicies dominum domus bylechaut dominum exaltationis eius:aut dominum termini eius:aut dominum triplicitatis:aut faciei eius quis boum fortior fuerit 7 autétioz in loco bylechiz si aspexerit bylech erit dignior alcochoden.o sino aspexerit bylech & fuerit pluris au crontatis afpicias eum qui fuerit auctoritatis minoris donec inuenias ali quem ex illis qui aspiciať: si non aspexerit bylec non poterit ille locus eſſe bylechiz tunc queres ab alio bylech z iterum queres ab eodem secúdo by lech alcocboden co ordine quo prædiximus:qsi equales fuerint duo plane te vel tres in auctoritate gradus bylechiz aspexerit vterqs erit dignioz alco choden qui fuerit fortior loco. Si vero fuerint equales in fortitudine loci.f. vbi fuerint ipfi planete erit ille alcocboden qui ppioz fuerit gradui bylech. Quibufdam autem videbatur melius inspicere a domino domus quisi aspexerit bylech ponebant eum alcochoden z non cofiderabant alterum:z i non inuenissent dominum domus aspicíentem bylech accipiebant domi num exaltationis quem si aspicere viderent bylech ponebant alcochoden eum z non cósiderabant alium:similiter faciebat de domino triplicitatis ter mini atqz faciei per ordinem:z erat Dorotbius preponens dominum termis ni in boc domino domus:Et quidam dixerunt & si equales fuerint duo plas nete veltres vel plures in dignitate:z in propinquitate aspecfus graduibys lech ille erit alcochoden qui erit in loco laudabili a sole:idest qui fuerit cum sole in yno gra per.i6.minuta aut infra:vel propinquior gradui anguli; aut ==End of OCR for page 4== ==Start of OCR for page 5== In initio oitus fui:idest cum mane apparuerit:aut fuerit in ſtatione fua ſecti da:vel in aliquo eſſe ſcilicet laudabili a fole ficut pdirimus ipſe erit alcochos den. Si vero gradus Solis fuerit bylech:7 fuerit in Ariete vel in Leone erit Sol bylechir alcochoden fimul z non cófiderabitur alius alcochoden pres ter eum:fimiliter si fuerit gradus Lune bylechiz fuerit in Zauro vel in Lau cro babeto eam pro bylech z alcochoden: fimul z non confiderabis alco, choden alium preter eam. Lmuram eft qui preeſt natiuitati:ab eo fignificatur effe nati post bylech z alcochoden: z ipse planetis ceteris eſt pluris auctoris tatis:i afcendétez in locis luminarium atqz in loco partis fortu ne in loco quoqz coniunctionis vel preventionis que est ante na tiuitatem. Quod si prefuerit aliquis planeta duobus locis aut tribus aut quattuor aut pluribus pre multitudine poteftatis sue erit ipse als muta.i.auctor z significator vite post bylecb:z alcochodé z per enz significas tur esse nati:r quidam habent eum pro alcochoden ad Dadum vitam. De profective signomm. I ex hoc signú pfectionis in annis natiuitatum atqs mundi. Na in annis natiuitatum ſcientia eft:ytafpicias quot anni traſierunt nato ex annis folaribus perfectis: afpicieſes omni ano signú vnú zinciples ab afcendere nati per ſucceſſionem signorum quoufos finitus fuerit numerus signum quod succedit est signum pfectio nis ab ascendente signum videlicet anni intrátis qué non misisti in numero boc erit in tali gradu sicut fuit in ascendente natiuitatis:z dominus eiúsdez Dicitur alcochoden cui interpretatio est dominus anni. Similiter numera bis a signo solis vnicuiqz anno signum vnum:z exibit per hoc signum profe ctióis a ſole. Similiter a signo Lune z a medio celi:r a parte fortune in simi litudinem gradus radicis.i.puncti nattuitatis.Luius rei exemplar eſt e qui dam natus est oriente capricomo.si.gradu eius sol erat in piscibus.ίς. gradu:z mediuz celi scorpionis. octo gra.z luna in libra.is.gra.z pars foitus ne in leone.i7.graziam tranfierút nato tres anni perfecti peruenietes quar tus annus ab ascendente in ariete qui est quartus a capricomovsqz ad.i7. gradum eiufdem arietis.7 erit mars alcochodem:z peruenit locus solis ad geminos vsą ad.is.gra.eiufdem signiiz peruenit luna ad capricornum.ad is.gra.z profectus a medio celi ad octo gradum aquary.z profectus a par te fortune peruenit vsqz ad.i7.gradum scorpii. Cumos peruenerit annus ad gradum profectionis:vt ad.i7.gradum arietis in boc exemplo: 7 fuerit iter ipsum z similem gradum succedentis signi ad quem debet peruenire annus sequés in eadem domo planeta vel radij eius: 2 volueris scire quando per uenerit profectio ad ipsum planetam vel ad radios eius aspicies ad sit iter gradum ad qué pueniet annus:z planetas velradios ex gradibus z minut: multiplicabis illud per.12.7 sextam illius numeri qué multiplicas vel in.i2. gradus 7.6.vnius gradus 7 quot puenerint eruut dies ex anno illo in quo cij ==End of OCR for page 5==
translation: in the ascendant, or in the second sign, it will also be apt for the hyleg. But if it is in any of these places in a feminine sign, it will not be apt for the hyleg. If none of the luminaries are apt for the hyleg, then look at the nativity: whether it is conjunct or preventional, that is, whether it is after the conjunction or prevention. If the nativity is conjunct and the degree of conjunction is in any of the angles, or in a succedent house, it will be in a place apt for the hyleg. But if the aforesaid degree is cadent from these eight places, that is, from the angles and from the succedent houses, it will not be the hyleg. Then you will look at the degree of the Part of Fortune: if it is in any of the angles or in the succedent houses, it will be in a place apt for the hyleg. But if it is cadent from these eight places, you will then look at the degree of the ascendant and constitute it as the hyleg. If the nativity is preventional, you will begin from the degree of prevention. You will look at it as you did before with the degree of conjunction, and after that, the degree of the Part of Fortune, and after that, the degree of the ascendant, in the order we mentioned for the degree of conjunction. You will not care about the sex of the signs, that is, you will not look at whether those signs are masculine or feminine, except only in the luminaries. That is, in the degree of conjunction or prevention, and the ascendant, and the Part of Fortune, you do not consider whether they are in masculine or feminine signs, both in the angles and in the succedent houses. But these four will only be apt for the hyleg when they are in these eight places, that is, in the angles or their succedent houses. If they are in receding places, that is, cadent from the angles, they will not be apt for the hyleg. It appears that the Sun is apt for the hyleg by day and night, above the earth and below the earth, in 11 places: above the earth in 6 places, and below the earth in 5. The Moon will also be apt for the hyleg by day and night, above the earth and below the earth, similarly in 11 places: above the earth in 5, and below the earth in 6. And if the Moon is in these places under the Sun's rays, it will not be apt for the hyleg. You will look for the hyleg in the angles and their succedent houses according to how the 12 houses of the circle are equalized by the degrees of the ascendant's hours, according to how its work is explained in the canon of planets, that is, in the book of the courses of the planets. And when you have equalized the houses in this way, every planet that is before the degree of the ascendant sign or of any house by 5 degrees or less, its strength will be valid in the house that succeeds it. And every place among the places we mentioned is apt for the hyleg if any of its lords looks at it, who is the lord of the house, or the lord of exaltation, or the lord of the term, or of the triplicity, or the lord of the face. If not, it will not be apt for the hyleg. Concerning the Alcochoden. The text of the Alcochoden, who is the significator of life, that is, the lord of years or the giver of years, whom you wish to know, and you already know the hyleg in the order I predicted: you will look at the lord of the hyleg's house, or the lord of its exaltation, or the lord of its term, or the lord of its triplicity, or its face, whichever of these is stronger and more authoritative in the hyleg's place. And if it aspects the hyleg, it will be more worthy of being the Alcochoden. If it does not aspect the hyleg, and it has more authority, you will look at the one with less authority until you find one of them that aspects. If it does not aspect the hyleg, that place cannot be the hyleg. And then you will seek from another hyleg, and again you will seek from the same second hyleg the Alcochoden in the order we predicted. If two or three planets are equal in authority in the degree of the hyleg, and each aspects, the one who is stronger in place will be more worthy of being the Alcochoden. But if they are equal in strength of place, that is, where the planets themselves are, that one will be the Alcochoden who is closer to the degree of the hyleg. Some, however, thought it better to look at the lord of the house, whom if they saw aspecting the hyleg, they would make him the Alcochoden and not consider another. And if they did not find the lord of the house aspecting the hyleg, they would take the lord of exaltation, whom if they saw aspecting the hyleg, they would make him the Alcochoden and not consider another. They did similarly with the lord of the triplicity, term, and face in order. And Dorotheus preferred the lord of the term in this lord of the house. And some said that if two or three or more planets are equal in dignity and in proximity of aspect to the degree of the hyleg, that one will be the Alcochoden who is in a laudable place from the Sun, that is, who is with the Sun in one degree by 16 minutes or less, or closer to the degree of the angle, or at the beginning of its rising, that is, when it appears in the morning, or is in its second station, or is in some laudable place from the Sun as we said, he will be the Alcochoden. But if the degree of the Sun is the hyleg, and it is in Aries or Leo, the Sun will be the hyleg and Alcochoden simultaneously, and no other Alcochoden will be considered besides it. Similarly, if the degree of the Moon is the hyleg, and it is in Taurus or Cancer, you will take it as the hyleg and Alcochoden simultaneously, and you will not consider another Alcochoden besides it. The Almuten is the one who presides over the nativity; from him is signified the being of the native after the hyleg and Alcochoden. And this planet has more authority than the others: in the ascendant, and in the places of the luminaries, and in the place of the Part of Fortune, and also in the place of the conjunction or prevention that is before the nativity. If any planet presides over two, three, four, or more places due to the multitude of its power, it will be the Almuten, that is, the author and significator of life after the hyleg and Alcochoden, and through him is signified the being of the native. And some consider him the Alcochoden for giving life. Concerning the Profection of Signs. From this, the sign of profection in the years of nativities and of the world. For in the years of nativities, the knowledge is this: that you look at how many years have passed for the native from the perfect solar years. And you will look at one sign each year, and you will begin from the native's ascendant through the succession of signs until the number is finished. The sign that succeeds is the sign of profection from the ascendant, namely the sign of the entering year, which you have not included in this number. This will be in such a degree as it was in the ascendant of the nativity. And its lord is called the Alcochoden, whose interpretation is the lord of the year. Similarly, you will count from the sign of the Sun one sign for each year, and from this will come the sign of profection from the Sun. Similarly from the sign of the Moon and from the Midheaven, and from the Part of Fortune, in the likeness of the degree of the radix, that is, the point of nativity. An example of this is someone born with Capricorn rising, in its 3rd degree. The Sun was in Pisces, in its 15th degree. And the Midheaven was Scorpio, 8 degrees. And the Moon in Libra, 15 degrees. And the Part of Fortune in Leo, 17 degrees. Now three perfect years have passed for the native, and the fourth year arrives from the ascendant in Aries, which is the fourth from Capricorn, up to the 17th degree of the same Aries. And Mars will be the Alcochoden. And the place of the Sun arrives at Gemini up to the 15th degree of the same sign. And the Moon arrives at Capricorn, at 15 degrees. And the profection from the Midheaven arrives at the 8th degree of Aquarius. And the profection from the Part of Fortune arrives up to the 17th degree of Scorpio. And when the year arrives at the degree of profection, as to the 17th degree of Aries in this example, and between it and a similar degree of the succeeding sign to which the next year should arrive in the same house, there is a planet or its rays, and you want to know when the profection will arrive at that planet or its rays, you will look at the distance between the degree to which the year will arrive and the planets or rays in degrees and minutes. You will multiply that by 12, and a sixth of that number which you multiply, or in 12 degrees and 6 minutes of one degree. And whatever results will be the days from that year in which...

text: fueris in quibus pueniet profectio a gradu in quo incipit vis ad gradum ad qué numeraſti poſt fiet pfectio ipfi planete. C-profectio aut ex anis mũ di dixit alchindus eft qo fuerit inter annú cóiunctionis que fignificauit far racenop ſectá z iter annú albegerat in quo cepit pamű pdicare mabomet qui fuit mus annoru arabu.52 anni folares 7.57.diesz fuit afcendés anni illius cõiunctióis pdicte ſecte fignű geminoyiz puenit pfectio eiufdem anni ad &ginem:z inter ipfum pmum annoy arabú:z primú annorum iezdagird regis perſarů fuerunt 3624.dies fcilicet pfecti.Cum ergo volueris habere notitiam buius rei accipe annos iezdygird z verte eos in dies ficut iam exs poſitú eft in azig.i.in libro curſui planetari:z adde deſuper dies qui funt in ter pzimű annorú arabű z iezdygird:7 diuide boc per.365.dies a quartam partem diei a quot diuifiones exierint tot erunt anni folaresiz q8 remanfes rit ex menfibus z diebus erit ex anno impfecto:qd cum ita collectum fuerit ex annis ipfi funt anni folares abinitio annorű arabú:prolice ergo omnian. no ſigné vnú z incipe a igine z ad quodcuqs fignú te pduxerit numerus ips ſum erit fignú ad qo puenit annus müdi ab aſcenſione coniunctionis predis cte fecte. Alijs auté extra alkindū yiſum est vt adderet ſuper annos iezda gird perfectos.6i.annos 7.ii.méles 2.12.dies.7.16.horas:ex annis perfidum qui funt fine fractione ideft fine quarta dici z extéderent bos annos in dies ac verteret dies in annos folares ficut prediximus z inciperent proſicere ab initio libre: fi volueris profectionem a figno cóiunctióis fecte fit proiectio a ſcorpione:fi vero volueris profectioné ab aſcédente regni minue ex annis iezdagird annos illos.zi7.perfidos z verte eos in annos folares ficut pdiri musiz incipe proicere a virgine.CRurfus ſi volueris profectionez ab aſcé fione profectionis mutationis cõiunctionis a triplicitate ağtica ad triplicitas té igneam minue ex annis iezdagird perfectis.i76.annos zverte illos quire manét in annos ſolares.z incipe proiicere a leone z quo peruenerit numer in eodem Agno erit pfectus vniufcuiusos initij eorü que diximus. De directione fignificatoris. I ex hoc fequiť atazir.i.directio:hoc eft vt dirigas fignificatorez aliquem ad aliquem locuz ignorú z ſcias qd fit inter eos ex gra dibus directionis accipieſes vnicuiqs graduiannů vnum. Lum ergovolueris fcire boc z fuerit ille fignificator in afcendente que uis dirigere ad aliquam ptem circuli minue afcenfiones gradus in quo fue rit fignificator quem volueris dirigere per afcenfiones regionis de afcenfio nibus gradus illius ad quem volueris dirigere eum:z quod remanferit erit gradus directionis: fi fuerit fignificatozin gradu ſeptime minues afcens hones nadir.i.oppofiti gradus in quo est fignificator de afcenfionibus nadir illius gradus ad qué volueris ipſum dirigere in illa regione. Si vero fuerit fignificator in medio celi vel in angulo terre:minues afcéfiões gradus fignis ficatoris de afcenfionib illius gradus ad que volueris euz dirigere per aſcé fhones circuli directiez quod remanferit erit gradus directionis.Si vero fue rit ille fignificator qué volueris dirigere in aliquo loco extra quattuor angu los.Afpicies longitudinem eius ab aliquo borum angulorum qui funt an gulus medij celi ates angulus terre:hoc eft vt afpicies fignificatorem qui fi fuerit inter afcendens z medium celiminues afcenfiones gradus medij ces li per circulum directum de afcenfionibus gradus fignificatoris: z fi fuerit inter.7. z medium celiminues afcenfiones gradus fignificatoris de afcen fionibus gradus medij celi per circulum directum: quod remanferit:ex licunqz borum locorum fuerit:illud diuides per partes bozarum illius diei gradus in quo fuerit ſignificatout quod exierit erunt bore longitudinis ab angulo. Et fi fuerit inter afcendens z angulum terre minues afcéfiones gra dus fignificatoris per circulum directum de afcenfionibus gradus anguli terre. Rurfus fi fuerit ſignificator inter angulum terre 2.7.minues afcefios nes gradus anguli terre per circulum directum de afcenfionibus gradus fignificatoris:z quod remanferit ex qualicunqs borum locons diuides per tempora bozaru diei nadir id'eſt oppoſiti gradus fignificatoris: quod exie rit ipfe erunt longitudines bore ab angulo. Lungs ſciueris longitudinis bozas ab angulo z volueris dirigere fignificatorem ad aliquem locum cir culi fignorumiz fuerit fignificator in medio circuli orientalis qui est a medio celi vios ad angulum terre de eis que ſuccedunt afcendens:minue afcenfio nes gradus illius in quo fuerit fignificator de afcenfionibus gradus illius ad quem volueris dirigere per circulum directum: 2 quod remanferit erit fignificator circuli directi ferua eum. Poft hoc minues afcenfiones illi gra dus in quo fuerit fignificator per afcenfiones regionis de afcenfionibus il lius gradus ad quem volueris eum dirigere:z quod remanferit erit fignifi cator regionis:poft hoc afpice refiduum quod fuerit inter fignificatorez cir culi directi:z fignificatorem regionis: accipieſs ſextam partem eius z multi plicabis in boris longitudinis ab angulo: quod fuerit erit equatio:z fi fue rit fignificator circuli directi minor fignificatore regionis: addes equatióez fuper fignificatorez circuli directi. Et fi fuerit fignificator circuli directi plus minues equationem ex eor quod remanferit erit gradus directióis.Ité fi fuerit fignificator quem volueris dirigere in medietate circuli occidenta lis que eft ab anguloterre vis ad medium celide eo quod fuccedit.7.mis nues afcenfiones nadir gradus fignificatoris quem volueris dirigere de afcenfionibus nadir gradus illius ad quem volueris dirigere eum per cir culum directum z quod remanferit erit fignificator circuli directi. Poft hoc minues afcenfiões nadir gradus ſignificatoris per afcenfiones regionis de afcenfionibus nadir gradus illius ad quem volueris eum dirigere: 7 quod remanferit erit fignificator regionis:deinde accipies refiduum quod fuerit inter fignificatorem circuli directi z fignificatorem regionis accipieſqs ſextă partem eius:z multiplicabis in bois longitudinis ab angulo:z quod fuerit erit equatio.poft hoc afpicies fignificatorem circuli directi si fuerit minor fis gnificatore regionis addes equationem ſuper ſignificatorem circuli directi: fi fuerit fignificator circuli directi plus ſignificatoze regiõis minues ectio né de fignificatore circuli directiiz qo ex eo exierit erit gradus directionis. CQB si fuerit fignificator in alią ğrta:z loc ad qué diriges eŭ i alia ärta di riges ſignificatore ad angulú q ei fuccedit:p15 diriges ab agulo ad locú qué c iij ب
translation: in which the profection will come from the degree in which the force begins to the degree to which you have numbered, then the profection of that planet will occur. The profection, however, from the years of the world, Alchindus said, is what was between the year of the conjunction that signified the sect of the Saracens and the year of Al-Biruni in which Muhammad first began to preach, which was the first year of the Arabs, 52 solar years and 57 days. And the ascendant of that year of the aforementioned conjunction of the sect was the sign of Gemini, and the profection of the same year came to Virgo. And between the first year of the Arabs and the first year of Yazdegerd, the king of the Persians, there were 3624 days, that is, complete. Therefore, when you wish to have knowledge of this matter, take the years of Yazdegerd and convert them into days, as has already been explained in the Azig, that is, in the book of planetary courses. And add to them the days that are between the first year of the Arabs and Yazdegerd, and divide this by 365 days and a quarter of a day. And as many divisions as come out, so many will be the solar years, and what remains of months and days will be from the imperfect year. When this has been collected from the years, these are the solar years from the beginning of the years of the Arabs. Therefore, project all years, one sign at a time, and begin from Virgo, and to whatever sign the number leads you, that will be the sign to which the year of the world comes from the ascendant of the conjunction of the aforementioned sect. Others, however, besides Alchindus, thought that they should add to the complete years of Yazdegerd 61 years, 2 months, 12 days, and 16 hours, from the years of the infidels, which are without fraction, that is, without a quarter of a day, and extend these years into days and convert the days into solar years as we have said, and begin to project from the beginning of Libra. If you want the profection from the sign of the conjunction of the sect, let the profection be from Scorpio. But if you want the profection from the ascendant of the kingdom, subtract from the years of Yazdegerd those 217 complete years and convert them into solar years as we have said, and begin to project from Virgo. Again, if you want the profection from the ascendant of the profection of the change of conjunction from the watery triplicity to the fiery triplicity, subtract from the complete years of Yazdegerd 176 years and convert those that remain into solar years, and begin to project from Leo, and wherever the number arrives in that sign, there will be the profection of each beginning of those things we have said. Concerning the direction of the significator. From this follows atazir, that is, direction: this is that you direct some significator to some unknown place and know what is between them from the degrees of direction, taking for each degree one year. Therefore, when you wish to know this, and that significator is in the ascendant, which you wish to direct to some part of the circle, subtract the ascensions of the degree in which the significator you wish to direct was, by the ascensions of the region from the ascensions of that degree to which you wish to direct it. And what remains will be the degree of direction. If the significator is in the seventh degree, you will subtract the ascensions of the nadir, that is, the opposite of the degree in which the significator is, from the ascensions of the nadir of that degree to which you wish to direct it in that region. But if the significator is in the midheaven or in the angle of the earth, you will subtract the ascensions of the degree of the significator from the ascensions of that degree to which you wish to direct it by the circle of direction, and what remains will be the degree of direction. But if that significator which you wish to direct is in some place outside the four angles, you will look at its longitude from one of these angles, which are the angle of the midheaven and the angle of the earth. This is that you look at the significator, which if it is between the ascendant and the midheaven, you subtract the ascensions of the degree of the midheaven by the direct circle from the ascensions of the degree of the significator. And if it is between the seventh and the midheaven, you subtract the ascensions of the degree of the significator from the ascensions of the degree of the midheaven by the direct circle. And what remains, from whichever of these places it is, you will divide by the parts of the hours of that day of the degree in which the significator is, and what comes out will be the hours of longitude from the angle. And if it is between the ascendant and the angle of the earth, you will subtract the ascensions of the degree of the significator by the direct circle from the ascensions of the degree of the angle of the earth. Again, if the significator is between the angle of the earth and the seventh, you will subtract the ascensions of the degree of the angle of the earth by the direct circle from the ascensions of the degree of the significator. And what remains from whichever of these places, you will divide by the times of the hours of the day of the nadir, that is, the opposite of the degree of the significator. And what comes out will be the hours of longitude from the angle. When you know the hours of longitude from the angle and you wish to direct the significator to some place of the circle of signs, and the significator is in the middle of the eastern circle, which is from the midheaven to the angle of the earth of those that succeed the ascendant, subtract the ascensions of that degree in which the significator was from the ascensions of that degree to which you wish to direct it by the direct circle, and what remains will be the significator of the direct circle; keep it. After this, you will subtract the ascensions of that degree in which the significator was by the ascensions of the region from the ascensions of that degree to which you wish to direct it, and what remains will be the significator of the region. After this, look at the remainder that is between the significator of the direct circle and the significator of the region; take its sixth part and multiply it by the hours of longitude from the angle; what results will be the equation. And if the significator of the direct circle is less than the significator of the region, you will add the equation to the significator of the direct circle. And if the significator of the direct circle is more, you will subtract the equation from it, and what remains will be the degree of direction. Likewise, if the significator which you wish to direct is in the middle of the western circle, which is from the angle of the earth to the midheaven of what succeeds the seventh, you will subtract the ascensions of the nadir of the degree of the significator which you wish to direct from the ascensions of the nadir of that degree to which you wish to direct it by the direct circle, and what remains will be the significator of the direct circle. After this, you will subtract the ascensions of the nadir of the degree of the significator by the ascensions of the region from the ascensions of the nadir of that degree to which you wish to direct it, and what remains will be the significator of the region. Then you will take the remainder that is between the significator of the direct circle and the significator of the region; take its sixth part and multiply it by the hours of longitude from the angle, and what results will be the equation. After this, you will look at the significator of the direct circle; if it is less than the significator of the region, you will add the equation to the significator of the direct circle. If the significator of the direct circle is more than the significator of the region, you will subtract the equation from the significator of the direct circle, and what comes out of it will be the degree of direction. If the significator is in another quadrant, and the place to which you direct it is in another quadrant, you will direct the significator to the angle that succeeds it, then you will direct from the angle to the place to which you wish to direct it.

title: Decan and Novenary Lords

Sup I ex boc dominus orbis in natiuitatibus boc est vt afpicias dos minum boze in qua oritur natus dabisos ascendens z dominum boze anno primo natiuitaris eius z fignificabiť per eé eius ideft Dominum boze fanitasvel infirmitas corporis nati sicut fignifica tur per dominum afcendentis radicis.i.natiuitatis: 7 dabis dos minum fecúde bore ab ea fecude domui ab afcendente in fecúdo anno z fi gnificabitur per eum eſſe fuum in eodem anno effe fubstantie ze.ficut fignifi catur per effe domini domus ſubſtantie:z dabis dominum tertie hose ab ea tertie domui ab afcendente tertio anno z ſignificabitur per effe illius in eo dem anno effe fratrum z fo20mm z ceterorum sicut fignificabit per effe dos mini tertie domus.z dabis dominuzhose quarte domui quarte ab afcendés te in anno quarto:z fignificabitur per effe suum in eodem anno effe patrum z eorum qui fignificantur perdomuni quartam Smiliter facies de dominis borarum per succeftionem dabis.f.dominum vniufcuiuscs boze domui ex ==Start of OCR for page 1== domibus radicalibus eritos domino bore.iz.ab bora natiuitatis domus.iz. ab afcendente radicis z annus.i2.7 domin.iz.bove a natiuitate radicis afcé dentis 7 anni.13.7 dominus.i4.bore ab ea dom ſubſtantie '7.14.anni.Et no minatur dominus hore prime dominus afcendentis hore. Dominus.2.bo re dominus bore domus substantie z dominus hoze tertie dominus bore Domus fratrum:z fic de alijs. Silr domin vniufcuiufqs baruz bozarū nominatur nomine domus:z nominatur etiam orbis dominus 7 fignificat per eum omni anno ficut fignificatur per alcochoden:boc est per dominum an ni:z quidam aftrologorum ponunt dominum orbis.f.afcendentis radicis anno primo 2.2.anno planetam qui huic fuccedit.z in tertio anno planetaz qui buic fuccedit hout dictum eft in domino'boze. I ex boc alfridarie diſpoſitio:hoc eft cum natiuitas fuerit diur na preerit in initio vite diſpoſitioni eius fridarie fol fecúdú quás titatem annorum fridarie eius qui funt decem:poſtboc planeta qui fuccedit foli qui est venus cui fridarie anni funt octo.7 poft venerem planeta qui fuccedit ei z eſt mercuriusz anni eius fút tredecim deinde luna: 7 anni fridarie eius funt nouem:deinde ſaturnus z anni fridarie eins funt.ii.Deinde iupiter z anni fridarie eius funt.i2.deinde mars 7 anni fridarie eius funt.7.deinde caput draconis z anni fridarie eius funt.3 poſt boc cauda:z anni cius.fridarie funt duo fiunt auté anni collecti fimul.75.anni.poft hoc reuertitur diſpoſitio ad folemiz fimiliter vfqz ad viti mum planetarum. Si autes fuerit natinitas nocturna incipit diſpoſitio a lus na diſponetaz annos fridarie eius qui funt nouez.fimiliter planeta poſt pla netam ficut prediximus in fole. Lus diſpoſuerit planeta annos' fridarie fue difponet ppue primam ſeptimam folum.i.feptimam partem fridarie fui.des inde pricipatur ei in fecunda ſeptima:planeta qui eum fuccedit:poft hoc pri cipatur ei in tertia ſeptima planeta.tertius qui fuccedit ſecudunuz ita donec participatur ei planeta qui est ante eum in vltima ſeptima ex annis fridarie eius: z est vnicuiqz iudiciú cú pticipať alteri ſuper natiuitate. Lex hoc almanar.i.fupereminentia qua dicitur q planeta vas dit fuper planetam:bocest vt afpicias curfum planete medium z locum eius equatum:z fi fuerit locus eius equatus minus mes dio curſu eius erit afcendens a medio circuli fui vſes ad ſumitas tem circuliz fi fueritlocus eius equatus plus medio curſu erit defcendens a medio circulifui vfqs ad infimum eius: z fi fuerit locus eius equatus equalis medio curſui erit in medio circuli fui deinde locum plane te minues de medio curſu ſuo poſtaz boc certiffime cognoueris.i.locú equa tum:vel econuerfo minorem de maiori eorum fubtrahendo z multiplica bis quod remanferit per ſeptem z diuides per. 22.7 quod de diuifione exierit erit boc omni planete pro quantitate eius aſcenſionis vel defcenſionis.De venere 7 mercurio accipimus quod eft inter vtraqs loca ideft y est inter loss ==End of OCR for page 1==
translation: which succeeds until you come to some novenary from which the degree is: and the planet will be the lord of that novenary: for example: if the sign from which that degree was, was from the triplicity of Aries: then the first novenary of that sign will be Mars, lord of Aries: the second novenary of the genus will be Venus, lady of Taurus: and the third novenary will be Mercury, lord of Gemini. Similarly up to the ninth novenary. If the sign was from the triplicity of Cancer, the first novenary will be the Moon: the second novenary will be the Sun, lord of Leo: and the third will be Mercury, lord of Virgo. And if the sign was from the triplicity of Libra, the first novenary will be Venus, lady of Libra: the second will be Mars, lord of Scorpio: and the third will be Jupiter, lord of Sagittarius. Similarly in the triplicity of Capricorn, its first novenary is Saturn, lord of Capricorn: and the second similarly Saturn, lord of Aquarius: and the third Jupiter, lord of Pisces. Then what follows according to the order of succession of the signs: for example: if a planet or some house from the houses, that is, was in the 19th degree of Aquarius, if the sign is divided into nine divisions, the 19th degree will be in the sixth division, and since the sign of Aquarius is in the triplicity of Libra, its first novenary is Venus, lady of Libra: the second Mars, lord of Scorpio: and the third Jupiter, lord of Sagittarius: the fourth Saturn, lord of Capricorn: and the fifth also Saturn, lord of Aquarius: and the sixth Jupiter, lord of Pisces: and thus the novenary of the 19th degree of Aquarius will be Jupiter. The method of finding the lord of the decan. From this, you will divide the ascendant into three parts, and each division will be 10 degrees: and you will give the first division to the lord of the ascendant, and the second to the lord of the fifth sign from it, and the third to the lord of the ninth: for the ascendant, the fifth, and the ninth are always one triplicity: for example: from the beginning of Aries up to its tenth degree, the decan is Mars. And if it is from the 10th degree up to the 20th degree, its decan will be the Sun, lord of Leo. And if it is from the 20th up to its end, its decan will be Jupiter, lord of Sagittarius. From this, the lord of the orb in nativities, that is, you will look at the lord of the hour in which the native is born, and you will give the ascendant and the lord of the hour in the first year of his nativity, and by him, that is, the lord of the hour, will be signified the health or illness of the native's body, just as it is signified by the lord of the radical ascendant, that is, of the nativity: and you will give the lord of the second hour from it to the second house from the ascendant in the second year, and by him will be signified his being in that year, the being of substance, etc., just as it is signified by the being of the lord of the house of substance: and you will give the lord of the third hour from it to the third house from the ascendant in the third year, and by the being of that one in that year will be signified the being of brothers and sisters and others, just as it will be signified by the being of the lord of the third house. And you will give the lord of the fourth hour to the fourth house from the ascendant in the fourth year: and by his being in that year will be signified the being of fathers and those who are signified by the fourth house. Similarly, you will do with the lords of the hours by succession, that is, you will give the lord of each hour to the radical house, and the lord of the 12th hour from the hour of nativity will be the 12th house from the radical ascendant and the 12th year. And the lord of the 13th hour from the nativity of the radical ascendant and the 13th year. And the lord of the 14th hour from it will be the house of substance and the 14th year. And the lord of the first hour is named the lord of the ascendant of the hour. The lord of the second hour is the lord of the hour of the house of substance, and the lord of the third hour is the lord of the hour of the house of brothers: and so on for the others. Similarly, the lord of each of these hours is named by the name of the house: and he is also named the lord of the orb, and by him is signified every year, just as it is signified by the Alcochoden: that is, by the lord of the year: and some astrologers place the lord of the orb, that is, of the radical ascendant, in the first year, and in the second year the planet that succeeds him, and in the third year the planet that succeeds him, as has been said in the lord of the hour. From this, the Alfridaria disposition: that is, when the nativity is diurnal, the Alfridaria Sun will preside over the disposition of his life in the beginning, according to the quantity of his Alfridaria years, which are ten: after this, the planet that succeeds the Sun, which is Venus, whose Alfridaria years are eight. And after Venus, the planet that succeeds her, which is Mercury, and his years are thirteen: then the Moon: and her Alfridaria years are nine: then Saturn, and his Alfridaria years are eleven. Then Jupiter, and his Alfridaria years are twelve: then Mars, and his Alfridaria years are seven: then the Head of the Dragon, and his Alfridaria years are three: after this, the Tail: and her Alfridaria years are two. The collected years together are 75 years. After this, the disposition returns to the Sun, and similarly up to the last of the planets. But if the nativity is nocturnal, the disposition begins with the Moon, and she will dispose of her Alfridaria years, which are nine. Similarly, planet after planet, as we have said for the Sun. When a planet has disposed of its Alfridaria years, it will dispose of its own first seventh part of its Alfridaria only. Then in the second seventh, the planet that succeeds it will participate: after this, in the third seventh, the third planet that succeeds will participate, and so on until the planet that is before it participates in the last seventh of its Alfridaria years: and each has a judgment when it participates with another over the nativity. From this, the Almanar, that is, the preeminence by which it is said that a planet goes over a planet: that is, you will look at the mean course of the planet and its equated place: and if its equated place is less than its mean course, it will be ascending from the middle of its circle up to the summit of the circle: and if its equated place is more than its mean course, it will be descending from the middle of its circle down to its lowest point: and if its equated place is equal to its mean course, it will be in the middle of its circle. Then you will subtract the planet's place from its mean course after you have most certainly known this, that is, the equated place: or conversely, subtracting the smaller from the larger of them, and you will multiply what remains by seven and divide by 22: and what comes out of the division will be this for every planet according to the quantity of its ascension or descension. For Venus and Mercury, we take what is between both places, that is, what is between the places.

author: Alchabitius
title: Planetary Strength and Aspects
text: ==Start of OCR for page 2== cam solis z veneris e mercurij z facimus vt ſupia: quia per hoc ſcimus qs eorum fortiot sit in circulo augis:ſed venus 7 mercurius cum fuerit aliquis eorum orientalis z fueritl locus eius equatus minus loco solis accipies res siduum quod est inter locum solis 7 locum eius: faciesqs cú eo quéadmodu feceris vt dictum eſt in planetis altioribus ex multiplicatione z diuisione. Et proprior ac fortior sit significatio planetarum dum abierint super seinuicem i.dum abierit alter super alterum in coniunctione. In opposito de auté z in quarto aspectu erit significatio eor minus apparens 7 debilior. Et tamen cum vnus illorum fuerit afcendens z alter descendens tuncafcendens eos rum vadit supra descendentem. Lum vero ambo fuerint descendentes:ils le qui fuerit minoris descensionis vadit super illú qui fuerit maioris vel plus ris descensionis. CSi autem ambo fuerint afcendentes ille qui est maioris ascensionis vadit super illum qui fuerit minoris ascensionis. Dicit quos z aliomodo idest vt scias latitudinem vtrorumqz planetarum:quia septem trionalis eorum vadit super meridianum. Et si fuerint ambo septemtriona les ille qui fuerit pluris latitudinis vadit super eum qui fuerit minoris latitu dinis Si autem fuerint vtriqs meridiani ille qui fuerit minoris latitudinis vadit super eum qui fuerit maioris latitudinis ideo dicitur oluna vadit super iouem in bis duobus operibus. Similiter si fuerit aliquis duoruz plas netarum in medio circuli nullam latitudinem habens tunc afcendens septe trionalis vadit super eum qui caret latitudine z vadit ipse qui sine latitudine est super descendentem meridianum. Et ex hoc apertio portarum dicitur cum iungitur planeta inferior planete superiori z fuerintcum hoc domus corum opposite. I ex hoc albuzic z est res q valde seruabant indi. Nam ipsi nus merant post coniunctionem solis z lune.i2.bozas z applicat eas soli diuidit.i2.bozas trinas.i.in tres partes.s.in baiz solis.z ius dicant super omnes quattuor horas sim iudicium dominorum triplicitatis solis bora coniunctionis:deinde dant veneri post.iza bozas z diuidunt eas iterum trinas z iudicant super omnem trinam diuisios nem sm dominos triplicitatis veneris bora coniunctionis:post hoc faciunt similiter cum mercurioz alijs planetis per successionem eoruz donec reuers titur orbis ad solem post.84.horas z non cessant sic facere frequenter do nec venerit coniunctio secunda.Et quidam dicunto albuzic sic boc q post coniunctionem sint.i2.bore inequales que vocantur combusteiz non opor tet aliquem in bis incipere aliquod opus:z post horas.i.sunt.72.bore inco buste in quibus est vtilis operum inceptio z post bas.72.horas icombustas sunt.i2.combuste similiter viqs ad coniunctioné & succedit.post hoc diuidunt bas horas.i2.in tres diuisiones idest in partes. Dixeruntos cum quis inces pit in quattuor boris primis deambulare vel bellare timenda erit perditio animesue: tin quattuor boris secundis timendum erit detrimentum corpo ris sui sine amissione anime:z in quattuor bois vltimis timédű est detrimé tum substantie sue z suorum & omnium que possidét. ==End of OCR for page 2== ==Start of OCR for page 3== Differentia quinta in commemoratione vniuerfarum partium. translation: We do as above with the Sun, Venus, and Mercury, because by this we know which of them is stronger in the circle of the apogee. But when Venus and Mercury, if either of them is oriental and its place is equal to less than the Sun's place, you will take the remainder, which is between the Sun's place and its place, and you will do with it as you have done, as was said, in the higher planets, by multiplication and division. And the signification of the planets is more proper and stronger when they have gone over each other, that is, when one has gone over the other in conjunction. In opposition, however, and in the quartile aspect, their signification will be less apparent and weaker. And yet, when one of them is ascending and the other descending, then the ascending one goes above the descending one. But when both are descending, the one with the lesser declination goes above the one with the greater or more declination. If, however, both are ascending, the one with the greater ascension goes above the one with the lesser ascension. It also says in another way, that is, so that you know the latitude of both planets, because their northern one goes over the meridian. And if both are northern, the one with the greater latitude goes over the one with the lesser latitude. If, however, both are southern, the one with the lesser latitude goes over the one with the greater latitude. Therefore, it is said that the Moon goes over Jupiter in these two operations. Similarly, if either of the two planets is in the middle of the circle, having no latitude, then the ascending northern one goes over the one that lacks latitude, and the one without latitude goes over the descending southern one. And from this, the opening of the gates is said to occur when an inferior planet joins a superior planet, and their houses are opposite. And from this, Albuzic, which is a thing that the Indians greatly observed. For they numbered 12 hours after the conjunction of the Sun and Moon, and applied them to the Sun, dividing the 12 hours into three parts, that is, into the hours of the Sun. And they judge over all four hours according to the judgment of the lords of the triplicity of the Sun at the hour of conjunction. Then they give to Venus after 12 hours and divide them again into three parts, and judge over every third division according to the lords of the triplicity of Venus at the hour of conjunction. After this, they do similarly with Mercury and the other planets in their succession until the orb returns to the Sun after 84 hours, and they do not cease to do so frequently until the second conjunction comes. And some say that Albuzic is this: that after the conjunction there are 12 unequal hours, which are called combust, and no one should begin any work in these. And after these hours, there are 72 uncombust hours in which the beginning of works is useful. And after these 72 uncombust hours, there are 12 combust hours similarly until the conjunction that follows. After this, they divide these 12 hours into three divisions, that is, into parts. They said that when someone begins to walk or wage war in the first four hours, the loss of life will be feared. In the second four hours, the detriment of one's body will be feared without the loss of life. And in the last four hours, the detriment of one's substance and one's own and all that they possess is to be feared.

author: Alchabitius
title: The Fifth Difference: Parts

Et proprior ac fortior sit significatio planetarum dum abierint super seinuicem i.dum abierit alter super alterum in coniunctione. In opposito de auté z in quarto aspectu erit significatio eor minus apparens 7 debilior. Et tamen cum vnus illorum fuerit afcendens z alter descendens tuncafcendens eos rum vadit supra descendentem. Lum vero ambo fuerint descendentes:ils le qui fuerit minoris descensionis vadit super illú qui fuerit maioris vel plus ris descensionis. CSi autem ambo fuerint afcendentes ille qui est maioris ascensionis vadit super illum qui fuerit minoris ascensionis. Dicit quos z aliomodo idest vt scias latitudinem vtrorumqz planetarum:quia septem trionalis eorum vadit super meridianum. Et si fuerint ambo septemtriona les ille qui fuerit pluris latitudinis vadit super eum qui fuerit minoris latitu dinis Si autem fuerint vtriqs meridiani ille qui fuerit minoris latitudinis vadit super eum qui fuerit maioris latitudinis ideo dicitur oluna vadit super iouem in bis duobus operibus. translation: The fifth difference in the commemoration of all parts. And because we have completed the expositions of those things that follow from the names of the astrologers, let us now pursue the commemoration of all parts. And when we have noted the place of the parts, take from that place to that place and project this from the ascendant or from another place. We say that you add to that whatever is between the two places of the degrees of the ascendant, and project from the ascendant or from the beginning of the sign from which you project. An example of this is the operation of the Part of Fortune, because you take whatever is between the Sun and the Moon in degrees, and you add to those degrees of the ascendant sign. Whatever is collected, you project it from 30 to 30 degrees, and you begin to project from the beginning of the ascendant sign, and where the number ends, there is the Part of Fortune. And at night, conversely, from the Moon to the Sun, and you add the degrees of the ascendant sign above, and project this from 30 to 30, and begin to project from the beginning of the ascendant sign. Let us begin here, therefore, after the Part of Fortune, the 12 parts of the houses, and let us begin from the ascendant. The first house is the Part of Life, and it is taken by day from Jupiter to Saturn, and at night conversely, and it is projected from the ascendant. The Part of Bile is taken from the degree of conjunction or prevention that was before the nativity up to the degree of the Moon, and it is projected from the ascendant. The Part of Future Things, that is, the Part of the Sun, is taken by day from the Moon to the Sun, and at night conversely, and it is projected from the ascendant. The Part of Love and Concord, that is, the Part of Venus, is taken by day from the Part of Fortune to the Part of Future Things, and at night conversely, and it is projected from the ascendant. The Part of Stability and Trust of the Ascendant of the Duration of the Native is like the Part of Concord and Love. The Part of Animosity and Audacity, that is, the Part of Mars, is taken by day from Mars to the degree of the Part of Fortune, and at night conversely, and it is projected from the ascendant. The second house is the Part of Substance, and it is taken by day and night from the lord of the house of substance to the degree and minute of the house of substance, and it is projected from the ascendant. The Part of Poverty and Smallness of Ingenuity, that is, the Part of Mercury, is taken by day from the Part of Future Things to the Part of Fortune, and at night conversely, and it is projected from the ascendant. The Part of Beatitude and Triumph and Victory, that is, the Part of Jupiter, is taken by day from the Part of Future Things to Jupiter, and at night conversely, and it is projected from the ascendant. The third house is the Part of Brothers, and it is taken by day from Saturn to Jupiter, and at night conversely, and it is projected from the ascendant. The Part of Benevolence of Brothers is taken by day from the Sun to Saturn, and at night conversely, and it is projected from the ascendant. If Saturn is under the rays, it is taken by day from the Sun to Jupiter, and at night conversely, and it is projected from the ascendant.

author: Alchabitius
title: Fourth House Parts
text: ==Start of OCR for page 4== Uarta domus pars patrum est.z accipitur in die a sole in satur's numirin nocte econuerſo:z proijcitur ab ascendente.pars mortis patris accipitur in die a saturno in iouem z in nocte eco uerſo:z proijcitur ab ascendente. Pars annorum accipitur in die a domino domus solis in saturnum:7 in nocte cconuerſo:? proijcitur ab ascendente. Et si fuerit in domo sua sol vel aliqua domorum sa turni accipe in die a sole in saturnum:z in nocte econuerſo:z proijce ab ascé dente z non consideres vtrú saturnus sit apparens vel sub radijs.pars hereditatum z possessionum accipitur in die ac nocte a saturno in lunam:et proijcitur ab ascendente. Pars nobilitatis nati:z eius de quo sit dubita tio vtrum sit filius illius patris cui imponitur:an alterius accipitur in die a gradu solis in gradum exaltationis sue:z proijcitur ab ascendente. Quod si sol fuerit in gradu exaltationis sue in die z luna in nocte in gradu exaltas tionis sue erit pars in gradus ascendentis:z erit significatio gradus ascens dentis. Pars finis rerum accipitur in die z nocte a saturno vis ad domi num domus coniunctionis:aut peruentionis:z addit desuper gradus ascé dentis:z proijcitur ab ascendente. Uinta domus est pars filiorumiz accipitur in die a Ioue in Sas turnum de nocte ecouerſo z proijcitur ab ascendente. C-Pars que significat tempus in quo debet filius fieri:z numerum con sen masſculorum seu feminarum 7 quid mulier pariet accipitur in die ac nocte a marte in Ioué:z pijcitur ab ascendéte. Pars Scientie filij masſculi z femine accipitur in die z nocte a Luna in Iouemiz p ijcitur ab ascendente. C-Pars per quas scitur natus de quo sit interrogatio vtrum sit masſculus vel femina accipitur in die a domino domus lune in Lu nam:z in nocte ecouerſost proijcitur ab ascendente. Pars significans fili um cum peruenerit iupiter ad eum:idest ad gradú partis significantis filiú accipitur in die ac nocte a marte in iouem z pijcit ab ascendete. C-Pars dis lectionis z gaudij accipitur in die ac nocte a venere in saturnum: 7 proijcit ab ascendente. Pars scientie esse filiorum accipitur in die ac nocte a luna in venerem z proijcitur ab ascendente. Exta domus est pars infirmitatis z azemena.i.debilitatis insepa rabilis alicuius membrop accipitur in die a saturnoin martem in nocte econuerſo:z proijcitur ab ascendente.pars seruoru accipitur in die a mercurio in lunam:z in nocte econuerſo:z pa ijcitur ab ascendente. Et dixit alédezgod accipitur in die a mer curio in partem fortune: z in nocte econuerſo. Et dixit:oportet yt vtatur vtrisqz partibus infimul. 8 Eptima domus est pars desponsationis virorum seu coniugij:z ac cipitur in die ac nocte a sole in venerem: 7 proijcitur ab ascendente. Et bec pars similis est parti mandatorum rumorum. Et pars des sponsationis mulierum vel coniugij ab hermete accipitur in die ac nocte a venere in saturnuni:z proijcitur ab ascendente. Et bec pars congruit par ti cultus terre. Item pars desponsationis mulierum secundum velium ==End of OCR for page 4== ==Start of OCR for page 5== accipitur in die ac nocte a sole in marteiz proiicit ab ascendente. Pars de lectionis ac voluptatis accipiť in die ac nocte a venere in gradum sepſtime:z proiciť ab ascendéte. C-Pars nuptiaruz accipitur in die ac nocte a saturno in venerem:z proiicitur ab ascendente., Liaua domus est pars mortis z accipitur in die ac nocte a luna in gradu z minuti domus octave:z additur desuper & ambus lauerit saturnus in signo suo 7 prolicitur ab initio signi saturni. Pars planete qui interficit accipiť in die a dño gradus ascés dentis in gradú lune:z in nocte ecouerſorc.C-Pars anniin quo timet nato mo28:7 paupertas impedimētum ac destructio accipit in die ac nocte a saturno in gradum dni domus coniunctionis vel preuétióis que sus It ante natiuitaté ré. C-Pars ligatióis 7 carceris: vtrú liberet ab covel no accipiť in die a saturno in ptem fortune z in nocte econuerſo. Ona domus est pars peregrinatióis accipitur in die ac nocte a domino domus none in gradů z minutú domus none:z proici tur ab ascédente.
