translation: Judgments or indications should be made according to the ninth sphere, and not according to the eighth. For since he wishes to change these degrees, it is consequent that the degrees according to which judgments ought to be made do not change. Albumazar in his introduction sets forth these degrees, namely, masculine and feminine, lucid and shadowy, etc. And he says there that if a planet in a nativity or interrogation of a male is in a masculine degree, or in a nativity of a female or interrogation in a feminine degree, its signification will be stronger. Concerning lucid, shadowy, and famous degrees, he says that if a planet falls in a lucid degree, its signification will be stronger in the signification of honors and it signifies beauty. And if it falls in a shadowy degree, it signifies hardness and slowness and a horrible and dark and evil thing. And when it falls in a dusky, or shadowy, or smoky degree, or in an empty degree, it signifies something slightly horrible. And if a planet falls in a degree of a well, its beauty and appearance will depart, and it will be weakened in its signification. For when fortunes fall into them, they are weakened in their signification. But when malefics fall into them, their signification is weakened. And perhaps fortune will signify an accidental event because of their weakness over evil. Concerning degrees that increase fortune, he says the ancients thought that there would be degrees in the circle that increase fortune. And they said that when planets are not in places signifying the native's fortune, and the Moon or Part of Fortune is in these degrees, or these degrees themselves are ascending, they increase the native's fortune. And if the planets signify a fall for him, these degrees move him to exaltation by some quantity with a certain motion. Concerning degrees of sublimity, he says: Some have said that when the Ascendant is one of these degrees, or the Sun is in a diurnal nativity, or the Moon in a nocturnal nativity in some of them, and it is in the best place in the figure. And when with this the planets of the root of the nativity signify fortune, it will lead the native to sublimity and a seat of nobility, and he will rule lands and cities, and possess much wealth. And because, with God's help, the author first determined concerning the essential being of the circle of signs, here he determines concerning its accidental being. And it is divided into two parts: first, it continues the things said; second, it follows there: for the circle of signs. And that is divided into two parts: first, it determines concerning the accidental division of the circle of signs. Second, it determines concerning the properties and significations consequent to that division; and it begins there: the fourth part which is from the Ascendant. And that is divided into two parts: first, it sets forth the properties and significations of the quarters. Second, it sets forth the significations of the individual houses; and that begins there: and one of the houses. First, he says after completing the essential being of the circle of signs, now he wishes to treat of its accidental being. Afterwards he says that the circle figures at every hour such a figure which is divided into four parts, which the circle of the horizon and the circle of the meridian divide. And each of these quarters is divided into three equal parts according to the division of the ascending sign in the direct circle. And in this way the whole circle is divided into 12 parts which are called towers or houses, whose explanation is in the canons of the tables. Two great circles passing through the poles of the world are to be understood: one is the meridian circle; and the other is the circle passing through the point of the zodiac which is in contact with the eastern horizon, and through the opposite point, namely, which is in contact with the western horizon, intersecting the meridian circle above the poles of the world. These two circles divide the whole heaven into four parts which are sometimes equal to each other, sometimes unequal; but for the most part they are unequal. For if the beginning of Aries or the beginning of Libra is in the Ascendant, these quarters are equal. In all other dispositions, however, they are unequal. Afterwards, let the portion of the equinoctial circle intercepted between the meridian circle and the circle passing through the beginning of the Ascendant be understood to be divided into three equal parts. Similarly, let the other portion of the equinoctial circle which is from the Ascendant to the lower part of the meridian circle, namely, to the angle of the earth, be understood to be divided into three equal parts. Then let great circles passing through the aforesaid divisions and through the poles of the world be imagined. Then the portion of the zodiac intercepted between any two of the first circles is one house. This is Ptolemy's position; and this is commonly held. There is also another imagination concerning the houses which Abraham Avenezre holds. And he imagines circles passing through the divisions of the equinoctial and through the intersection of the meridian circle and the horizon in the northern part. This method is not commonly held; therefore, I do not care to explain it much. To find the beginnings of the 12 houses of the astrolabe is not difficult; indeed, it is easy, whose doctrine to interpose here would be long and superfluous, since it has been sufficiently set forth in the canons of the astrolabe. But whoever wishes to equalize the houses by tables will find complete doctrine in the canons of the tables of the primum mobile. Then the author says the beginning of this division is the Ascendant, whose beginning is at the point of the hemisphere on the eastern side. Then follows the second house; then the third; and so on to the twelfth. The fourth and significations of the circle of signs consequent to its accidental division. And he says that the fourth part of the circle which is from the Ascendant to the Midheaven, namely, the 12th, 11th, and 10th houses, is called the eastern masculine approaching. And it signifies the beginning of life and is called puerile sanguine. And the other fourth is from the Midheaven to the degree of the west, namely, the 9th, 8th, and 7th houses, is called the southern feminine receding from us; and it signifies the middle age which is called the perfection of youth; and it is called aestival and choleric. The third fourth which is from the degree of the west to the angle of the earth, namely, the 6th, 5th, and 4th houses, is western masculine approaching us; and it signifies the end of life; and it is called autumnal melancholic; and it is of middle age. The fourth part which is from the angle of the earth to the Ascendant, namely, the third, second, and first houses, is northern feminine receding from us; and it signifies what happens to a man after his death as regards the memory of men, namely, whether good or evil is said of him. And this part is called senile phlegmatic defective and hibernal. And this is a correct comparison which the author sets forth here, just as that which he set forth in the essential division of the circle, except that he adds that this signifies the beginning and middle and end of life and what happens to a man after his death. Afterwards he says that the whole half which is from the Midheaven going through the Ascendant to the fourth house is called the ascending half. And the other half which is from the fourth house going through the degree of the west to the Midheaven is called ascending. And the whole half which is

Jócz cú volueris ſcire intétioné qrentie:vide qs planetay béat plures fortitudis in aſcédēte in illa boza qi venit ad te aio in terrogádizz vide in qua domo ex.i 2.Domibofit ille planeta. z dicas fm figni ficatões illius domus in qua fuerit. Uerbi gra. Pono paſcédés fit leoz fol beat plures fortitudies in gradu afcendétesz pono op fol fitin. 2. domo dico o intétio fua eft petere de ſubſtatia ſua vel de lucro vel de aliquo confimili. Et si fol fuiffet in terra domo diriſſem o vellet petere aligd de eſſe fratris vr fratrű z fic de alijs domib ſuo mo. Et nueniens ad te tenuerit aliqua rezin manu ſua vel alio modo eam occultauerit:z ſi volueris fcire ſubſtantia ? na turam illius rei afpice planeta fortiorem in afcendéte fi afpexerit afcendens erit illa res de subftantia figni afcendentis. Si non afpexerit afcendés erit & ſubſtátia figni in quo eſt planeta. Si fuerit fignum terrenterit de natura ter re vel aliä res naſcit de terra. Si fuerit fignú aquaticű:erit aliä res de na tura ağ:vlqnaſcit iaä. Si fuerit aereűzerit aliq res & na aeris. Si fuerit figni f igneu erit aliq res qopat pigne. Et si volueris fcire colore rei afpice drum bore z im coloré illi iudica.De colozib planetap dicet poftea vbi loquer de naturis planetap dño gredete. Et si volueris ſcire vty res ſit noua vel antiq aſpice luna:fi fuerit fup terra erit noua.fi fuerit fub terra erit atiä. Et si volues ris ſcire vty fit lõga vel breuis:aſpice driz termini grad anguli terre a fi fue rit in auge fui circuli erit res loga. Si fuerit in oppofito augis erit breuis.Si in lõgitudib medijs mediocris iter longú z breve. Dec eft ſnia Ptolemeiin 90.ppone cétilogi. Lú afpexerit fignificator aſcédés c.Dicit Daly Abenras gel fialigs qfierit a te de vita ſua vty fit loga uel breuis:afpice dim afcédét luna. Si fuerit ſalui ab ifortunis fignificát longa vita. Et si fuerit cobuſti vľ ifortiati ſignificat puá vitá. Notádú é circa nutritioné.f.uty puer fit vital vel nó. Dicit Daly Abéragel qn dni triplicitat luminaris téporis vult dicere dni triplicitatis figni in quo eſt ſol ſi fuerit natiuitas diurna vel figni lune in natiuitate nocturna fuerit in afcédéte:velin.io.domo:vel.it.vel.5.eft fignifi catio bone ac leuis nutritōis. Et si fuerint in ſeptima:ſignificat mala nutritio nem:z anxia atqz laboriofaz. On grad afcédens z luminare tpis dánati fue rint:fignificat q nó nutriek niſi dni triplicitatis fuerint ſalui z firmi i angut. Qn luna fuerit ifortunata in aſcédéte:nutritioné no ſignificat. An luna fue rit in.4.domo iucta corpali cú ifortuna aut de ärta uel oppofitõe afpicit in fortuna fignificat o no nutrieť z mater ei eſt in piculo:aut forfita moriet.Di cit Ababa Auenezre. Si dis afcendétis fuerit cobust natus no viuet ſpaciú 8.diep. Portata fuit mibi natiuitas vnius pueri cuius aſcédens erat uirgo z Mercuri fuit in piſcib i detriméto ſuo retrograd z cobuster ego credi di buic dicto z dixi o puer nó uiueret pocto dies z mortus fuit in fexta die. Dicit Daly Abéragel:piuctio planetap humanitaté no fignificat. Uult dices re cóinctio pluriű planetarú in aſcédéte nó ſigt nutritioné. Et ponit ibi vnuz exemplu notabile. Dicit vocauit me rex noſtre ciuitatis:z vna ex mulieribus pepat filiú z fuit aſcendés libra. 8. gradus termin Mercurij:z fuerüt in eo Jupiter Uenus Mars z Mercuri z cóuenit ibi vna focietas astrologozn 7 glibz eox fuam opinionez dixit:z ego tacui. Rex dixit mibi quid babes q no loqueris. Cui reſpondi date mibi terminum trium dierü:quia si filius ve ster tranfierit tertium diem erit de ipſo miraculu magni. Et quando natus cópletas habuit.24. horas pofuit ſe ad loquēdum:z locut fuit z fecit signú cú manu z rex multum expauefcebat inde. Et dixit poffibile eé y diceret ali quá pphetiá:vel aliquod miraculum. Et rex iuit ad natú:z nos cú eo ad aus diendum quid diceret. Et infás dixit:ego sum nat infortunatz natus sum ad iudicádů amiſſionem regni z destructioné gentis almanam. Dine ftatim cecidit natz mort est. De his qui moriuntur anteqz recipiunt cibum di cit Ptolemeus in dripartito.generaliter dico q qi aliqo luminare fuerit i aliquo angulorum z fuerit fimiliter aliqua infortuna participationé habés cum eo in longitudine que fit cu eo gradu per gradu: vel ipſum afpicies in figura duop alterum equalium. Nec habuerit cum eo fortuna participatio nem de figura. Et diſpoſitor luminarium loci inuent fuerit in locis planeta rum infortunarum natus ille non recipiet cibum z morieť bora qua naſcet fij
translation: that which is above the earth is called the right, and that which is below the earth is called the left. Then it says that the first house, 7, 4, 2, 7, and 10 are called angles. And 2, 5, 8, and 11 are called succedents to the angles. But 3, 6, 9, and 12 are called cadent from the angles. Then it adds a notable point: it says that when a planet is in an angle or in a succedent, it is said to be effective; and if it is in the cadent houses, it is said to be ineffective. Each of these houses, the author says, has its own proper significations. The 12 houses, and all of this can be divided into four parts: in the first part, it sets out the significations of the 12 houses as they pertain to human existence. In the second part, it sets out the significations of the houses as they pertain to colors. In the third part, it sets out certain rules that can be called aphorisms. In the fourth part, it teaches how to choose significators. The first part begins at the beginning. The second begins there: they signify: and the 12 houses' colors. The third there: and it is said in the signification of the houses. The fourth there: and when you wish to know the planet ruling the matter. The first part can be divided into twelve parts according to the significations of the 12 houses, and where they begin will be clear. First, it says that each house signifies something about human existence. For the first house, which begins from the eastern horizon and is called the ascendant, signifies the bodies of men and life: the beginnings of all works in interrogations, speeches, rumors, and other such things. And it signifies the thought of the querent, that is, what the querent has in mind, and it signifies the beginning of life. And the lord of the first triplicity of the first house, the first, signifies life and the nature of the native or querent, and his delights and desires, what he loves or hates: and what good or evil befalls him at the beginning of life. And the second lord of the triplicity of this house signifies the life and body and strength or health of the native, and the middle of life. The third lord of the triplicity signifies the same as the second, and signifies by itself the end of life. From this letter, it can be gathered that if the first lord of the triplicity of this house is stronger than the other two, the first part of life will be better, and so you should understand for the other two. Concerning this house, certain things are to be noted. First, it is to be noted concerning the intention of the querent, which Ptolemy says in the 94th proposition of the Centiloquium: The place of the stronger significator in the ascendant is that which is in the mind of the querent. Therefore, when you wish to know the intention of the querent, see which planet has more strength in the ascendant at that hour when he comes to you to ask, and see in which house out of the 12 houses that planet is. And you should speak according to the significations of that house in which it is. For example, I assume the ascendant is Leo, and the Sun has more strength in the degree of the ascendant, and I assume that the Sun is in the 2nd house. I say that his intention is to ask about his substance or about profit or about something similar. And if the Sun had been in the third house, I would have said that he wanted to ask something about the existence of a brother or brothers, and so on for the other houses in their own way. And if, when he came to you, he held something in his hand or concealed it in some other way, and if you wish to know the substance and nature of that thing, look at the stronger planet in the ascendant. If it aspects the ascendant, that thing will be of the substance of the ascendant sign. If it does not aspect the ascendant, its substance will be of the sign in which the planet is. If it is an earthy sign, it will be of the nature of earth or some other thing born from the earth. If it is a watery sign, it will be some other thing of the nature of water, or born from water. If it is airy, it will be some other thing of the nature of air. If it is a fiery sign, it will be some other thing that operates by fire. And if you wish to know the color of the thing, look at the lord of the hour and judge its color. Of the colors of the planets, it will be said later when we speak of the natures of the planets, God willing. And if you wish to know whether the thing is new or old, look at the Moon: if it is above the earth, it will be new; if it is below the earth, it will be old. And if you wish to know whether it is long or short, look at the lord of the term of the degree of the angle of the earth, and if it is in the apogee of its circle, the thing will be long. If it is in the opposite of the apogee, it will be short. If in the middle longitudes, it will be moderate between long and short. This is the opinion of Ptolemy in the 90th proposition of the Centiloquium. When the significator aspects the ascendant, etc. Haly Abenragel says that if someone asks you about his life, whether it will be long or short: look at the lord of the ascendant and the Moon. If they are safe from misfortunes, it signifies a long life. And if they are combust or unfortunate, it signifies a short life. It is to be noted concerning nourishment, that is, whether a child will live or not. Haly Abenragel says that when the lords of the triplicity of the luminary of the time (meaning the lords of the triplicity of the sign in which the Sun is, if it is a diurnal nativity, or of the Moon in a nocturnal nativity) are in the ascendant, or in the 10th house, or 11th, or 5th, it is a signification of good and easy nourishment. And if they are in the seventh, it signifies bad nourishment: and anxious and laborious. When the degree of the ascendant and the luminary of the time are afflicted, it signifies that he will not be nourished unless the lords of the triplicity are safe and firm in the angles. When the Moon is unfortunate in the ascendant, it does not signify nourishment. When the Moon is in the 4th house, corporally conjoined with an unfortunate planet, or aspects an unfortunate planet from the 4th or by opposition, it signifies that he will not be nourished and his mother is in danger: or perhaps she will die. Aben Ezra says: If the lord of the ascendant is combust, the native will not live for the space of 8 days. A nativity was brought to me of a child whose ascendant was Virgo, and Mercury was in Pisces in its detriment, retrograde and combust. And I believed this saying and said that the child would not live for eight days, and he died on the sixth day. Haly Abenragel says: the conjunction of planets does not signify humanity. He means that the conjunction of several planets in the ascendant does not signify nourishment. And he gives a notable example there. He says: the king of our city called me, and one of the women gave birth to a son, and the ascendant was Libra, 8 degrees of Mercury's term: and Jupiter, Venus, Mars, and Mercury were in it, and a society of astrologers gathered there, and each of them gave his opinion: and I was silent. The king said to me: what do you have that you do not speak? To whom I replied: give me a term of three days: because if your son passes the third day, it will be a great miracle concerning him. And when the native had completed 24 hours, he began to speak: and he spoke and made a sign with his hand, and the king was very frightened by it. And he said it was possible that he would say some prophecy: or some miracle. And the king went to the native: and we with him to hear what he would say. And the infant said: I am born unfortunate: and I am born to judge the loss of the kingdom and the destruction of the German people. Then the native immediately fell and died. Concerning those who die before they receive food, Ptolemy says in the Quadripartitum: generally I say that when any luminary is in any of the angles, and there is also some unfortunate planet having participation with it in longitude, which is degree by degree with it: or aspects it in a figure of two equals. And it does not have a fortunate planet participating with it in figure. And the disposer of the luminaries of the place is found in the places of the unfortunate planets, that native will not receive food and will die at the hour he is born.

author: Alchabitius
title: Dragon's Tail in Ascendant
text: . Dicit abrabă cu fuerit cauda draconis in aſcédéte natiuitatis nat ille erit ces cus 7 ſcoz distátia eiva gradu aſcédétis erút áni. Uerbi gra:ſi fuerit in.io.gra du ab afcédéte cecabit in.io.ano.Electiones huisdom funt inceptiones re ra.ficut icipere edificare vel aliqd cófife.Dicit byſpaléſis in electionib?pme dom.Si fciueris natiuitatem alicui caueas in electione ſua ne ponas fignú in aſcédente in quo fuit ifortuna in fua nativitate. Si aút ignoraueris natinis tate elige ſcom nam rei pro qua fit electio.verbi gra:ſi fit electio pro incipien do sribere aliqué libzú fortificet mercuri. Si pro eundo ad bellus fortificet mars. Si si pro edificado ponat signum firú in aſcédéte.Dicit idé byspaléks volés bora sanguinis minuédi eligere fac vt sit luna in trino vel sextili aspe ctu martis. 7 caue ne fit coniuncta cum eo corpatr vel in ärto velin oppofito ei. Et fac ut sit luna in ſigno bumoris minuendid Ecunda domus. Dic auctor ponit fignificatio nes ſche domus.Et dicit q se cuda dom fignificat ſubſtatia z victů z auxiliatores z miniftros z ſignificat fine iunétutis.Et Anduzgá dixit vide qs extribº dnis triplicitatis dom ſubſtatie sit fortior in ſe.i.in loco in quo eft in figura a bit facies signiﬁcatore ſubſtatie. Qui si fuerit in decima domo bébit ſubſtátiá a rege. Et si fuerit in domo fiducie.i.in vndecima erit meli? Alia lits tera bet si fuerit in domo fidei erit magis. Credo qopma littera fit uera.Dos mus enim fiducie est domus ſubſtatie regis. Et pmus dis triplicitatis dat ſubſtátia in pnripio vite:qui si fuerit fortunat erit nat diues in pncipio vite. secund dat ſubitátiá in medio vite:qui si fuerit fortunatus erit natus diues in medio vite:terti dat ſubſtatia in fine vite.qui si fuerit fortunat natus erit diues in fine vite. Et sikr intellige de ifortunijs eorum ideft si aligs eorum fue rit infortunat amittet natus ſubſtätiam in illa pte vite que ei attribuit.Dicit byſpaléfis in canonibus huius dom cósidera vtrum dis ſede domus sit in aſcédente uel dat vim suam domino aſcendentis z nó sit in domo lapſa si sic bébit lucrum sine labore. Et si econtra fuerit fit econuerfo.Et si Jupiter sues rit in natali angularis uel sit in ſuo honore nel beat vim in aſcendente uel có sideret ptem fortune:uel sit dis domus solis in natali diei:uel dñus domus lune in natali noctis:uel sit dis hore non deerit nato cenfus in vita sua:quia bic idest Jupiter est dis ſubſtatie. Si vero dis ascendétis afpiciat dominu ſubſtatie uel suam domum uel ptem fortune afpectu inimicicie destruet sub ſtatiam manu sua ppria. Si dis aſcendentis fuerit in secúda domo infotu natus dabit pprio uelle ſubſtantiam suam. Si uero sit ibi planeta infortuna` tus qui nó sit dñs aſcendentis capiet ab ipso vi siue furto. Si capricorn sit afcendens natus erit cupidus z auarus:qi dis afcédentis z dominas secun de sunt idem fcilicet saturnus.Dicit Ptolemeus in canonib? buius domus res ptinentes ad lucra ſubſtatiequomodo erút conuenit nobis perquirere a parte fortune solummodo quá ſcimus semper per id quod estinterſolem z luná i pijciť ab aſcendente in nafcentibus de die z in nafcentibus de nocte. Juxta quod notandum eſt qu pars fortune secundum Ptolemeum proiicit boc modo. subtrabať verus loc solis a uero loco lune 7 quod remáserit cos putatur a gradu afcendentis & vbi numerus finitur ibi eſt parè fortune Hoe mo iubet Ptolemens sempr facere siue fuerit in die siue in nocte. De hoc pli xius dicetur deo concedéte in expofitione quinte differétie.ibi.n.babet locu. Poftea dicit ptolemens iudicium buius est isti manerici. Uolo dicere acci pe planetas gubernatores signi.Dicit ibi Daly qo regula p quá scimus nati diuitias est accipe planetas gubernantes locu ptis fortune z prepones illi qui habuerit ibi plures dignitates. Deinde dicit Ptolemeus 7 qn gubernas tores partis fortune fortes a potétes fuerint:erit natus magnay diuitiarum maxime fi duo luminaria testificabūtur ibi testimónio cócordato:id eſt aspe ctu trino vel sextili. Ptolemens scias qo Saturnus signiﬁcat qo diuitie nati erunt ex parte mulierű:aut p labore terre:aut p maria. Et Jupiter signiﬁcat quod diuitie erunt pro senescalia vel pro donis bonorum z religioforuz. Et Mars signiﬁcat quod diuitie erunt producedo milites:aut gubernado bo mines armorum. Et Uenus signiﬁcat quod diuitie erunt pdonis amicorú z mulier. Et Mercurius signiﬁcat qo ditie erunt pro scientijo z mercimos nijs. Dicit Daly abenragel debemus in hoc primit infpicere ad stellas ﬁras que sut prime in sche magnitudinis. Et si aliquá cay inuenerimus in gradu ascendentis:uel in gradu decime domus:autin gradu septime:aut in gradu örte:aut i gradu solis si natiuitas fuerit diurna:vk'in gradu lune sinatiuitas fuerit nocturna. Et melior boy locoy oium est gra.ascendés.deinde gra.io. Domus:deinde gradus septime:deinde arte. Et si depdictis stellis fuerit in duob locis:vel in trib:aut plurib i natus fuerit cum hoc de pgenie regis erit rex altus. Et si de pgente regali no fuerit:habebit potétia z mádatű simi le potétie z mandato regis z pueniet ad magnam nobilitatem'z alta dnium 7 pcipiet ciuitatibus vllis 7 genri. Et scias qö stelle ﬁxe.f.primi honoris eles uant boiem ad alté gradum z magnam dignitaté z permutat de infimo ad supremus. Doc idem dicit ptolemeus in.2 9. ppofitione centiloquij.stelle ﬁx xe dant dona modum excedétia:sed multotiens finiunt in malum. Dicit Da ly in comento eiufdem ppofitionis cum be stelle sole prefuerint:ideft sine te stimonio fortunay planetaruz erit mors illorum mors peffima. Dicit Paly Abenragel quádo pars fortune fuerit cadésiz in malo loco:vfuerit sub ra, dije solis:aut in radijs infortune z dominus eius in loco peregrino ideft in loco in quo nullam habet dignitatem z dominus secunde domus nó aspes xerit ascedés:natus erit laboriofusz pauper zanguſta z misera uita uiuet. Noé bon sol neqz mars neqz saturn in secuda domoin aliqua natiuitate necinde nat bonu bebit:qmfol distruet planetas:7 aufert eis lumé:z mars est signiﬁcator impedimétorum laborum a defectus substantie Saturnus signiﬁcat pauptatez z indigentia z pcins boc toto est si dis dom? substantie fuerit cobust cú danatióe ptis fortue Dicit abraba quenezre si nolueris sci re quis naſcétium fuerit pauper uel diues aspice dium secúde domus q si fuerit fortunatus erit diues:si cobuſtus vel retrogradus vel in casu suo erit paup:sicut dictú est in natiuitatibita intellige de queſtionib?. De electióib que ﬁunt in bac domo dicit byspaléſis si volueris emere aliqua rez causa lu cradi aspice vt sit luna in aspectu trino vel sextili solis:z caueas ne sit subluce i iij
translation: Abrabam says that if the Dragon's Tail is in the ascendant of a nativity, that native will be blind, and the distance from the ascendant in degrees will be the years. For example, if it is in the 10th degree from the ascendant, he will be blind in the 10th year. Elections of this kind are the beginnings of things, such as beginning to build or to do something. Hyspalensis says in the elections of the first house: If you know someone's nativity, be careful in their election not to place a sign in the ascendant in which there was an infortune in their nativity. But if you do not know the nativity, choose according to the nature of the thing for which the election is made. For example, if the election is for beginning to write a book, Mercury should be fortified. If for going to war, Mars should be fortified. If for building, a fixed sign should be placed in the ascendant. The same Hyspalensis says, when wishing to choose a time for bloodletting, make sure that the Moon is in a trine or sextile aspect to Mars. And be careful that it is not conjunct with him bodily, or in a square or opposition to him. And make sure that the Moon is in a sign of diminishing humor. The second house. The author here sets forth the significations of the second house. And he says that the second house signifies substance and sustenance and helpers and ministers, and it signifies the end of youth. And Anduzgar said, see which of the three lords of the triplicity of the house of substance is stronger in itself, that is, in the place where it is in the figure, and it will signify substance. If it is in the tenth house, he will have substance from a king. And if it is in the house of trust, that is, in the eleventh, it will be better. Another letter says if it is in the house of faith, it will be more. I believe that the first letter is true. For the house of trust is the house of the king's substance. And the first lord of the triplicity gives substance at the beginning of life; if he is fortunate, the native will be rich at the beginning of life. The second gives substance in the middle of life; if he is fortunate, the native will be rich in the middle of life. The third gives substance at the end of life; if he is fortunate, the native will be rich at the end of life. And understand similarly about their misfortunes, that is, if any of them is unfortunate, the native will lose substance in that part of life which is attributed to him. Hyspalensis says in the canons of this house, consider whether the lord of the second house is in the ascendant or gives its strength to the lord of the ascendant and is not in a fallen house. If so, he will have gain without labor. And if the opposite is true, it will be the reverse. And if Jupiter is angular in the nativity, or is in his honor, or has strength in the ascendant, or aspects the Part of Fortune, or is the lord of the house of the Sun in a day nativity, or the lord of the house of the Moon in a night nativity, or is the lord of the hour, the native will not lack wealth in his life, because this, that is, Jupiter, is the lord of substance. But if the lord of the ascendant aspects the lord of substance or its house or the Part of Fortune with an aspect of enmity, he will destroy substance with his own hand. If the lord of the ascendant is in the second house unfortunate, he will give his substance by his own will. But if there is an unfortunate planet there who is not the lord of the ascendant, he will take from him by force or theft. If Capricorn is the ascendant, the native will be greedy and avaricious, because the lord of the ascendant and the lord of the second are the same, namely Saturn. Ptolemy says in the canons of this house, concerning matters pertaining to gains of substance, how they will be, it is fitting for us to inquire only from the Part of Fortune, which we always know by that which is between the Sun and the Moon, projected from the ascendant in day nativities and in night nativities. According to which it should be noted that the Part of Fortune according to Ptolemy is projected in this way: subtract the true place of the Sun from the true place of the Moon, and what remains is counted from the degree of the ascendant, and where the number ends, there is the Part of Fortune. Ptolemy commands to always do this, whether it is by day or by night. More will be said about this, God willing, in the exposition of the fifth difference, for there it has its place. Afterwards, Ptolemy says the judgment of this is in this manner. I mean, take the planets governing the sign. Daly says there that the rule by which we know the native's wealth is to take the planets governing the place of the Part of Fortune and prefer the one who has more dignities there. Then Ptolemy says, and when the governors of the Part of Fortune are strong and powerful, the native will have great wealth, especially if the two luminaries testify there with a concordant testimony, that is, a trine or sextile aspect. Ptolemy, know that Saturn signifies that the native's wealth will be from women, or by labor of the earth, or by the sea. And Jupiter signifies that wealth will be from stewardship or from gifts of good and religious people. And Mars signifies that wealth will be from producing soldiers or governing men of arms. And Venus signifies that wealth will be from gifts of friends and women. And Mercury signifies that wealth will be from sciences and merchandise. Daly Abenragel says that in this we must first look at the fixed stars which are of the first magnitude. And if we find any of them in the degree of the ascendant, or in the degree of the tenth house, or in the degree of the seventh, or in the degree of the fourth, or in the degree of the Sun if the nativity is diurnal, or in the degree of the Moon if the nativity is nocturnal. And the best of all these places is the degree of the ascendant, then the degree of the tenth house, then the degree of the seventh, then the fourth. And if any of the aforesaid stars are in two places, or in three, or more, and the native is of royal lineage, he will be a high king. And if he is not of royal lineage, he will have power and command similar to the power and command of a king, and he will attain great nobility and high dominion, and he will rule over cities and nations. And know that the fixed stars, that is, of the first honor, elevate a man to a high degree and great dignity and change him from the lowest to the highest. Ptolemy says the same in the 29th proposition of the Centiloquium: fixed stars give gifts exceeding measure, but often end in evil. Daly says in the commentary on the same proposition, when these stars alone preside, that is, without the testimony of fortunate planets, their death will be a most evil death. Daly Abenragel says, when the Part of Fortune is cadent, that is, in a bad place, or is under the rays of the Sun, or in the rays of an infortune, and its lord is in a peregrine place, that is, in a place where it has no dignity, and the lord of the second house does not aspect the ascendant, the native will be laborious and poor and will live a narrow and miserable life. Neither the Sun nor Mars nor Saturn is good in the second house in any nativity, nor will the native have good from it, because the Sun destroys planets and takes away their light, and Mars is the significator of impediments, labors, and lack of substance. Saturn signifies poverty and indigence, and this is especially true if the lord of the house of substance is combust with the damnation of the Part of Fortune. Abrabam says, if you wish to know which of those born will be poor or rich, look at the lord of the second house. If he is fortunate, he will be rich; if combust or retrograde or in his fall, he will be poor, as has been said in nativities, so understand in questions. Concerning elections made in this house, Hyspalensis says, if you wish to buy something for the sake of gain, make sure that the Moon is in a trine or sextile aspect to the Sun, and be careful that it is not under the light in the third.

author: Alchabitius
title: Sun and Ascendant Lord
text: Solis: melius eſt:vt dis afcendétis det vim planete exiſtenti in decima vr vndecima domo:ita qo recipiens no fit retrogradus.Si Jupiter fit in afcés dente quartadecima vel vndecima vel ſecunda domo bonú eft.Aspice etiaz vt fit pars fortune in bono loco:z melius est vtdominus partis eam afpis ciat yel Luna vel Sol. Ertia domus Pic ponit fignificatiões tertie dos mus:z dicit quod tertia domus fis gnificat fratres z forores ppinquos a cognatos z dilectos:z fis gnificat fidem ac religionem mandata z legationes mutatiões: itinera breuia. Et fignificat eſſe vite ante mortem. Et dicit An duzgaz dominus triplicitatis huius tertie domus primus fignificat fratres maiores.Secundus mediocres. Tertius minores:7 corum status 2 dignis tas iudicatur fecúdum ſtarus iftorum dominorum.Dicit Daly Abenragel quando fol fuerit dominus tertie domus ab afcendente z combufferit do minum fecunde domus aut partem ſubſtatie fignificat quod natus perdet fubftantiam occafione fratrum: 7 foluet pro eis impofitiones amiffiones z damna multa. Quando dominus tertie domus z pars fratrum vel Jus piter fuerint infignis communibus vel ſignis multorum filiorum z fortune afpexerint eos afpectu amiciciesz cum receptione:fignificat quod natus il leprimogenitus eſt a fratribus fuis: quod habebit fratres potentes z bos nos.Si dominus domus fratrum fuerit cõbustus aut pars fratrum fuerit combusta vel fi fol fuerit in domo fratrum:aut in oppofito Jouis;he funt fo gnificationes paucomm fratrum z quod destruentur z expergentur. Quâs do dominus domus fratrum infortunatus fuerit ſuper terram: fignificat quod Danum z occafio perueniet in fratribus qui fuerint ante enz. Et si bas buerit iftud infortunium eo existente fub terra malum:z damnum istud pers ueniet ad fratres qui erunt poſt eum. Et hoc modo dices in bono z fortuna quando fortunatus fuerit in locis bonis z fortibus. Sidominus domus fratrum fuerit in afcendente:fignificat natum effe primogenitum:vel erit fo lus fine boc quod unquá fratres habebit vel forores:vel quando inter afcés dens z medium celi nó fuerit aliquis planeta fignificat fimiliter quod natus eſt primogenitus. Et fi aliqui fratres naſcétur poſt eû perdentur. Et fi fore aliquis ex eis remanſerit:ſemper tamen erit maior 7 melior. Quando in alis qua renolutione annorum nati fuerit Dominus tertie domus in decima ab afcendéte natiuitatis aliquis fratrum eius mozietur in illo anno. Similiter fi dominus tertie radicis natiuitatis fuerit in decima domo revolutióis:hoc est ideo qe decima domus ab afcendéte eſt octaua a domo tertia. Si aliquis quefiuerit a te de ſtatu fratris fui afpice fignum tertium ab afcendente que eſt domus fratrum a dominum fue triplicitatis 7 Dominum illius domus z que ex fortunis uel infortunis afpicit ipſum 7 in quo loco eft:quia fiinuene, ris dominum tertie domus in fexta domo uel applicante año fexte donna: aut onm fexte in tertia.dicas quod frater eius eft infirmus.Et fi ipfum inuer neris in quinta vel.i i.dicas quod frater eius non eft in loco fuo:qe iuit extra locú fuuz. Et fi inueneris domini tertie domus infortunati; aut in.i 2.Dicas qå frater eiveſt in anxietate aut in ifirmitate. Et fi inueneris drim tertie cum Marte ambos piunctos a combustioné intrates fub radys ſolis.dicas qu nó euadet ab infirmitate. Et fi ambo cobuſti fuerint indica modis omnibus mortez. Et hoc modo infpicias fiinterrogatus fueris in reb ptinétibus ad alias domus vt o pře vel filio vel vrove re.Dicit byſpaléſis volés inciperep ximum iter fac ut planeta fortuna afpiciat domuz tertiam uel eius dim ita qỏ nó fit ibi malus vel eius quart afpectuel oppoſit. Si eſt luna in tertia Domo bonus eft. Cancer Leo z Lapricornus mali funt p breui itinere. Luna in aſcédéte uel in ärta domo in omni electióe malú. Cauendú eſt ne Luna sit in via cobusta q est a.i 9.gradu libre vis ad tertin Scorpionis. Laueudú eſt etiam ne fit luna ppe caput uel cauda draconis infra.i2.gradus. Barta domus bic ponit fignificationes quarte domus.Et Et dicit & arta domus fi gnificat pies z bereditates 7 fines reruz:z fignificat thefauros z omnesres occultas 7 abſcóditas:z ſignificat finem vite. Et dic anduzgaz dis triplicitatis quarte domus pmus fignificat patres.fecundus fignificat ciuitates a terras.tertius fignificat inem rerum a carceres. Dicit Paly abenragel. Afpice ſtatum patris a dominis triplicitatis quarte dom?. Si dominus triplicitatis quarte domus primus fuerit in domo vel in exal, tative ſua:aut in ſuo baiz receptus a fole vel Joue z fuerit in alto loco medij celi uel in quinta domo fignificat bonum ſtatum pris nobilitatez altitudiné z'diuitias poſſe z longa vitam z bonam fortuna z boc fi fignum in quo fuit bic planeta fuerit maſculinu:tamen ſi ſignú ipſum fuerit femininű fignificat matri ea que diximus:7 maxime fi planeta fuerit feminin. Quid fit baiz ers ponet poſtea deo cócedente.Et fi fecúdus dis triplicitatis ärte domus fue rit fotiat př bébit terras z poplabit eas a laborabit in eis 7 bébit ex hac p te bona vtilitaté aut lucy. Et fi fuerint bi planete in eſſe dinerſo ſeu cótrario Zium iudica. Et si dis triplicitatis ärte dom tertius fuerit fortis z fortuna tus ut prediximus:pater erit homo qui extrabet thefauros z vineas icidet: 7 aptabit lapides preciosos. Et si fuerit in diuerſo ſtatu ab eo qd dixim erit incifor lapidum vel laborabit in materijs malis z fetétibus. Si ſol in natiui tatibus diurnis vel ſarurnus in nocturnis. Et ps pris filr afpexerint aſcédés uel ei dim de ato nel oppoſitióe fignificat o pater nati filiuz abhorrebit z odiet en z pcurabit vt interficiat. Et fi luna z pars matris afpexerint aſcédés uel ei dominum abborribilitas illa z maliuolétia in filiú erit a matre. Dixit bermes quarta domus est fouea planetarum quonia eſt in fundo circuli ce li z infirmitate:z eſt in trino aſpectu.i2.domus.queeft domus tenebrarum laborum anxietatum a carcerum : z eft gaudium infortune maioris:z est in fextili aſpectu fexte domus que est domus tenebroſa infirmitatis a damniz gaudium ifortune minoris. Quádo aliquis planeta fuerit in quarta domo zin fuo cafu est ficut homo in folitudine fuffocatus donec exeat inde. Dicit Daly abenragel quádo pars fortuune fuerit ifortunata in figno masculino ludica quod pater pus moziek quam mater. Si in ſigno feminino mater pil us mozieť quá pater. Pars patris accipitur & die a fole in ſaturnú:z de nos f iiij
translation: Sun: It is better that the lord of the ascendant gives strength to the planet existing in the tenth or eleventh house, so that the recipient is not retrograde. If Jupiter is in the ascendant, fourteenth, eleventh, or second house, it is good. Also, look for the Part of Fortune to be in a good place, and it is better that the lord of the Part or the Moon or the Sun aspects it. The third house here sets forth the significations of the third house, and says that the third house signifies brothers and sisters, relatives and kin, and loved ones; and it signifies faith and religion, messages and embassies, changes, short journeys. And it signifies the state of life before death. And Anduzgazar says that the first lord of the triplicity of this third house signifies older brothers. The second, middle brothers. The third, younger brothers; and their status and dignity are judged according to the status of these lords. Daly Abenragel says that when the Sun is the lord of the third house from the ascendant and burns the lord of the second house or the Part of Substance, it signifies that the native will lose substance on account of brothers, and will pay for them impositions, losses, and many damages. When the lord of the third house and the Part of Brothers or Jupiter are in common signs or signs of many children, and the Fortunes aspect them with friendly aspect and with reception, it signifies that the native is the firstborn among his brothers, and that he will have powerful and good brothers. If the lord of the house of brothers is combust, or the Part of Brothers is combust, or if the Sun is in the house of brothers, or opposite Jupiter; these are significations of few brothers, and that they will be destroyed and scattered. When the lord of the house of brothers is unfortunate above the earth, it signifies that harm and occasion will come to the brothers who were before him. And if this misfortune occurs while he is below the earth, it is bad; and this harm will come to the brothers who will be after him. And in this way you will speak in good and fortune when he is fortunate in good and strong places. If the lord of the house of brothers is in the ascendant, it signifies that the native is the firstborn, or will be alone without ever having brothers or sisters; or when there is no planet between the ascendant and the midheaven, it similarly signifies that the native is the firstborn. And if any brothers are born after him, they will be lost. And if any of them remain, he will always be older and better. When in any revolution of the native's years, the lord of the third house is in the tenth from the ascendant of the nativity, one of his brothers will die in that year. Similarly, if the lord of the third radical house of the nativity is in the tenth house of the revolution; this is because the tenth house from the ascendant is the eighth from the third house. If someone asks you about the state of his brother, look at the third sign from the ascendant, which is the house of brothers, and its lord of the triplicity and the lord of that house, and what aspects it from fortunes or misfortunes, and in what place it is: for if you find the lord of the third house in the sixth house, or applying to the lord of the sixth house, or the lord of the sixth in the third, you will say that his brother is sick. And if you find him in the fifth or second, you will say that his brother is not in his place, because he has gone out of his place. And if you find the lord of the third house unfortunate, or in the twelfth, you will say that his brother is in anxiety or in sickness. And if you find the lord of the third with Mars, both conjoined and entering combustion under the Sun's rays, you will say that he will not escape from sickness. And if both are combust, indicate death in all ways. And in this way you will look if you are asked about things pertaining to other houses, such as a father or son or wife. Hyspalensis says, wanting to begin a journey, make sure that a fortunate planet aspects the third house or its lord, so that there is no malefic there or its square aspect or opposition. If the Moon is in the third house, it is good. Cancer, Leo, and Capricorn are bad for a short journey. The Moon in the ascendant or in the fourth house is bad in every election. Beware that the Moon is not in the via combusta, which is from the 19th degree of Libra to the third of Scorpio. Also beware that the Moon is not near the head or tail of the Dragon within 12 degrees. The fourth house here sets forth the significations of the fourth house. And it says that the fourth house signifies fathers and inheritances and the ends of things; and it signifies treasures and all hidden and concealed things; and it signifies the end of life. And Anduzgazar says that the first lord of the triplicity of the fourth house signifies fathers. The second signifies cities and lands. The third signifies the end of things and prisons. Daly Abenragel says: Look at the state of the father and the lords of the triplicity of the fourth house. If the first lord of the triplicity of the fourth house is in its house or in its exaltation, or in its hayz, received by the Sun or Jupiter, and is in a high place of the midheaven or in the fifth house, it signifies a good state of the father, nobility, high position, riches, power, and a long life and good fortune, and this if the sign in which this planet was is masculine: however, if the sign itself is feminine, it signifies to the mother what we have said; and especially if the planet is feminine. What hayz is, he will explain later, God willing. And if the second lord of the triplicity of the fourth house is strong, the father will have lands and will cultivate them and will work in them and will have good utility or profit from this part. And if these planets are in a diverse or contrary state, judge them. And if the third lord of the triplicity of the fourth house is strong and fortunate as we have said, the father will be a man who extracts treasures and cuts vines, and prepares precious stones. And if he is in a state diverse from what we have said, he will be a stone cutter or will work in bad and stinking materials. If the Sun in diurnal nativities or Saturn in nocturnal ones. And the Part of the Father similarly aspects the ascendant or its lord from a square or opposition, it signifies that the native's father will abhor and hate him and will try to kill him. And if the Moon and the Part of the Mother aspect the ascendant or its lord, that abhorrence and malevolence towards the son will be from the mother. Hermes said that the fourth house is the pit of the planets because it is at the bottom of the celestial circle and in infirmity; and it is in a trine aspect to the twelfth house, which is the house of darkness, labors, anxieties, and prisons; and it is the joy of the greater infortune; and it is in a sextile aspect to the sixth house, which is the dark house of sickness and damages, the joy of the lesser infortune. When any planet is in the fourth house and in its fall, it is like a man suffocated in solitude until he comes out of it. Daly Abenragel says that when the Part of Fortune is unfortunate in a masculine sign, judge that the father will die before the mother. If in a feminine sign, the mother will die before the father. The Part of the Father is taken from the Sun to Saturn by day, and from the Moon to Saturn by night.

author: Alchabitius
title: Part of Mother and Father
text: cte ecotra z proiicitur ab afcendente. Pars matris accipitur in die a venere in lunam & de nocte econtrario z proiicitur ab afcendente.Si domin partis patris fuerit ſuper terram a dominus partis matris fub terra iudica o ma ter prius moriek q3 pater. Et fi cótrarium fuerit contrarium iudica.Dicit Da ly abenragel dixit albezen filius albezib. Quadm die cum effet cuz abolabē 7 fuit cum eo meraefo venit ad me gdam senex a dedit mibi chartam ynam in qua fcripta erat vnius natiuitatis figura.z dixit mihi quod afpicerezin ea dem figuraiz ego quefiui ob eo cuius erat illa figura feu natiuitas qui reſpo dit quod erat cuiufdam filij ſui z dum infpicerem a cogitareni in ea abolabé accepit eam de manu mea z aliquantulű afpexit in ea z vixit. Primum gin bac natiuitate videmus eſt y natus iste onus est de adulterio quia nó eft fis lius illius qui dicit o filius eius eft. Ego diri vnde babes hoc z refpondit.fe nex ifte dicit quod bec natiuitas est filij ſui z pater nati cuius eſt bec natiui tas iam funt quattuor anni y deceffit z obiit eodemanno quo natus onus fuit. Et ego quefiui vnde babebat boc.z ipse dixit:ego afpexiad gradum pa tris zinueni eum in oppoſito matris z non erat inter cos plus vno gradu. Et inueni ptem prisin.ii.domo ab aſcendéte que eft octaua a quarta z erat inter pteni z ſaturnum vnus gradus ppter qo iudicauiq pr buius natian. no quo natus fuit ortus deceffit. z bic fener dicit qö eft filius eius vnde nato adulterinus eft.z tunc fenes dixit:ego no ſum pater buius nati ſed eft filius filij mei z filius meus.pater eius deceffit eodem anno natiuitatis iftius ficut bic bomo bonus dixit. Vinta domus, bic ponit auctor fignificationes gute domus Dicit quod bec do, mus fignificat filios tam mafculos qž femininos. Et fignificat di lectiones z legatos fiue nuncios 7 dona. Et futurum fit poft mortem ex laude vel vituperatione. Et dixit anduzga quod do minus triplicitatis domus filiowz pmus fignificat filios z vitam eop.Secu dus defignat dilectieōs.Leriti fignificat legatos fiue nuncios.Dicit Ptole meus in rónib buius domus cóuenit ut infpiciamus ad planetas exiſtétes In loco zenith capitum noftrop z ad locum qui ſequit illum.z est locus fortu ne z ad locum pticipem cum illis in figura.Dicit Daly in comento locus qut eft zenith capitu nostrozum eft.io.domus.z locus qui fequif eft.11.domus q est domus fortune. Et regula qua ſciemus eé filioruz eft accepta a planetio exiftétib in bis locis aut exiſterib in locis pricipatib cu eifdem ficut eſt aſce dens 2.7.domus qe vtraqs cap pricipat cú io.r.u. Et find inuenerim in bis locis planetă ifpiciemus fi quos inenerim in.4.00mo.vel.5.2 accipiemus inde fignificatione.Dicit Ptolem z luna veneréz Joué iudicabim in dan do filios.Solé marté z ſaturnum in auferédo z dabimus mercuriŭ pticipa tové cũ quocus cori qui fecum pticipet in figura Dicit Daly in comento.Si fuerit in locis pdictis aligs explanetis forunis fignificat o bébit filios. SI aligs ex infortunis nullum bébit filium z fi babuerit erunt debiles 2 panci. Et fi mercurius pticipationé babuerit cum fortunis erit fortuna:fi cuzinfor tunis erit ifonuna.Dicit Ptoleme zéppofitio.i44.quarte partisres ptis culares filiorum poteris fcire per coſiderationem qui bene infpexeris in quo libet eorum ad planetam dantem filios ita quod eum ponas loco aſcéden tis 7 ſcies cetera pricularia filiorum generaliter ficut per natiuitatem.Dicit Daly in comméro. Quod autem vult Ptoleme in boc loco narrare eft id qo accidit in natiuitate mea. Inſpexi z iueni Joué in decima domo zin.28.gra du capricorni afpiciétem ſe cum luna z saturno z fignificabat hoc & haberé Filios 7 quod morerent:quia faturnus erat cum Joue in.io.domo.7 quia Ju piter erat in figno feminino z luna fimiliter fignificabat quod effent plures vno z o effent plures femine. Et qe Jupiter erat oriétalis:fignificabat o bé rem mafculum.z fic babui tres filios.ynum masculi z duas feminasiz oés obierunt:erat aut gradus afcendés natiuitatis mafculi circa gradu ſaturni in mea natiuitate 7 ppter hoc mortuus est cito. Et erat gradus afcédens vni us femine in pifcibus in fextili Jouis in mea natiuitate z in trino lune mee na tiuitatis:7 erat fol ylech in figno capricomi. Et erat gradus afcendés alteri femine in capricorno.z hoc est mirabile: ppter qo ſciri póto natiuitates ſem per inter fe cócordat vna cuilla.Dicit byſpalenfis volens horá generádi fili um eligere mafculum:fit Jupiter in quouis angulo vel fit foli oriétalis fitos dis bore mafculus z melius est vt fignú aſcédés fit masculinů z ét eius dis. zsi luna fit fortunata a Joue erit melius. Pro femina accipe figna feminina 7 planetas femininos z fit Jupiter occidentalis.Dicit Daly abenragel qn in guta domo fuerit aliqua fortuna 7 dns gute domus liber ab'infortunijs z af pexerit medium celi natus multos bébit filios z viuent ac ibunt ad boni.Et fi fuerit cótrarium:cótrariú fignificat. Afpice in oibus natiuitatibus fi inuene ris luminaria infortunata non babentia ullum afpectum:fortune natus bas bebit paucos filios. In ſciédo tépus in quo habent filij aſpice ad planetaz qui babuerit plures dignitates in domofilior. fien ineneris in pre orietali bébit filios in iunétute fua.ſi eú iueneris medio celi hébit eos in medio etatis fue. Et fi in feptima bébit eos in pncipio ſenectutis.Si in ärta bébit eos cirs ca finé vite fue. Silr qn ann? puenerit ad fignű in quo fuerit Jupiter vel vesz nus in radice natiuitatis bébit filiŭ in illo anno.Dicit Daly abéragel ſi iterro gat? fuerit o muliere fi eft pgnás aut no afpice ſi dim afcedétis z lunamam bos iueneris in domo filior:aut fi inueneris dim dom filior in ascendente liberű ab ifortunis:dicas qo mulier illa fit pgnas.z fidis aſcédétis aut dis Domus filioru dederit vim fuam planeta exiftétiin angulo. Et fikr fidis afce détis receptus fuerit a recipies ipfum receptus.dicas qu est pregnans. Et fi Dominus afcendētis dederit vim fuam planete cadenti ab angulo:fignificat quod non est pregan.mubio muroileanbom shoog muo Exta domus. Die ponit fignificaties.6.00m² Di cit fexta dom fignificat ifirmitates 7 feruos z fignificat finé vite z od futur fit an senectute. Dixit aduz gazo pm ons triplicitat bui? dom fignificat ifirmitates aqua lefcétias sine deterioratiões ifirmitatu.Scds fignificat uernacu los z feruos. Nota dua iter vernaculú z ferui.vernacul eni ide é o famulo z vocat feru pductici.feru eft qui est códitiois feruilis. Un dixit ada verfifie
translation: The Part of the Mother is taken in the day from Venus to the Moon, and at night the opposite, and it is projected from the Ascendant. If the lord of the Part of the Father is above the earth, and the lord of the Part of the Mother is below the earth, judge that the mother will die before the father. And if the contrary is true, judge the contrary. Daly Abenragel says that Albezen, son of Albezib, said: One day, when I was with Abolaben, and Meraefo was with him, a certain old man came to me and gave me a chart, in which was written the figure of a nativity. And he told me to look at that figure. And I asked him whose figure or nativity it was, and he replied that it was of a certain son of his. And while I was looking at it and thinking about it, Abolaben took it from my hand and looked at it for a while and said: "The first thing we see in this nativity is that this native is born of adultery, because he is not the son of him who says he is his son." I said, "How do you know this?" And he replied, "This old man says that this nativity is of his son, and the father of the native, whose nativity this is, died four years ago, and he died in the same year that the native was born." And I asked how he knew this. And he said: "I looked at the degree of the father and found it opposite the mother, and there was not more than one degree between them. And I found the Part of the Father in the second house from the Ascendant, which is the eighth from the fourth, and there was one degree between the Part and Saturn, for which reason I judged that the father of this native died in the year he was born. And this old man says that he is his son, therefore the native is illegitimate." And then the old man said: "I am not the father of this native, but he is the son of my son, and my son, his father, died in the same year of this nativity, as this good man said."

author: Alchabitius
title: The Fifth House
text: cte ecotra z proiicitur ab afcendente. Pars matris accipitur in die a venere in lunam & de nocte econtrario z proiicitur ab afcendente.Si domin partis patris fuerit ſuper terram a dominus partis matris fub terra iudica o ma ter prius moriek q3 pater. Et fi cótrarium fuerit contrarium iudica.Dicit Da ly abenragel dixit albezen filius albezib. Quadm die cum effet cuz abolabē 7 fuit cum eo meraefo venit ad me gdam senex a dedit mibi chartam ynam in qua fcripta erat vnius natiuitatis figura.z dixit mihi quod afpicerezin ea dem figuraiz ego quefiui ob eo cuius erat illa figura feu natiuitas qui reſpo dit quod erat cuiufdam filij ſui z dum infpicerem a cogitareni in ea abolabé accepit eam de manu mea z aliquantulű afpexit in ea z vixit. Primum gin bac natiuitate videmus eſt y natus iste onus est de adulterio quia nó eft fis lius illius qui dicit o filius eius eft. Ego diri vnde babes hoc z refpondit.fe nex ifte dicit quod bec natiuitas est filij ſui z pater nati cuius eſt bec natiui tas iam funt quattuor anni y deceffit z obiit eodemanno quo natus onus fuit. Et ego quefiui vnde babebat boc.z ipse dixit:ego afpexiad gradum pa tris zinueni eum in oppoſito matris z non erat inter cos plus vno gradu. Et inueni ptem prisin.ii.domo ab aſcendéte que eft octaua a quarta z erat inter pteni z ſaturnum vnus gradus ppter qo iudicauiq pr buius natian. no quo natus fuit ortus deceffit. z bic fener dicit qö eft filius eius vnde nato adulterinus eft.z tunc fenes dixit:ego no ſum pater buius nati ſed eft filius filij mei z filius meus.pater eius deceffit eodem anno natiuitatis iftius ficut bic bomo bonus dixit. Vinta domus, bic ponit auctor fignificationes gute domus Dicit quod bec do, mus fignificat filios tam mafculos qž femininos. Et fignificat di lectiones z legatos fiue nuncios 7 dona. Et futurum fit poft mortem ex laude vel vituperatione. Et dixit anduzga quod do minus triplicitatis domus filiowz pmus fignificat filios z vitam eop.Secu dus defignat dilectieōs.Leriti fignificat legatos fiue nuncios.Dicit Ptole meus in rónib buius domus cóuenit ut infpiciamus ad planetas exiſtétes In loco zenith capitum noftrop z ad locum qui ſequit illum.z est locus fortu ne z ad locum pticipem cum illis in figura.Dicit Daly in comento locus qut eft zenith capitu nostrozum eft.io.domus.z locus qui fequif eft.11.domus q est domus fortune. Et regula qua ſciemus eé filioruz eft accepta a planetio exiftétib in bis locis aut exiſterib in locis pricipatib cu eifdem ficut eſt aſce dens 2.7.domus qe vtraqs cap pricipat cú io.r.u. Et find inuenerim in bis locis planetă ifpiciemus fi quos inenerim in.4.00mo.vel.5.2 accipiemus inde fignificatione.Dicit Ptolem z luna veneréz Joué iudicabim in dan do filios.Solé marté z ſaturnum in auferédo z dabimus mercuriŭ pticipa tové cũ quocus cori qui fecum pticipet in figura Dicit Daly in comento.Si fuerit in locis pdictis aligs explanetis forunis fignificat o bébit filios. SI aligs ex infortunis nullum bébit filium z fi babuerit erunt debiles 2 panci. Et fi mercurius pticipationé babuerit cum fortunis erit fortuna:fi cuzinfor tunis erit ifonuna.Dicit Ptoleme zéppofitio.i44.quarte partisres ptis culares filiorum poteris fcire per coſiderationem qui bene infpexeris in quo libet eorum ad planetam dantem filios ita quod eum ponas loco aſcéden tis 7 ſcies cetera pricularia filiorum generaliter ficut per natiuitatem.Dicit Daly in comméro. Quod autem vult Ptoleme in boc loco narrare eft id qo accidit in natiuitate mea. Inſpexi z iueni Joué in decima domo zin.28.gra du capricorni afpiciétem ſe cum luna z saturno z fignificabat hoc & haberé Filios 7 quod morerent:quia faturnus erat cum Joue in.io.domo.7 quia Ju piter erat in figno feminino z luna fimiliter fignificabat quod effent plures vno z o effent plures femine. Et qe Jupiter erat oriétalis:fignificabat o bé rem mafculum.z fic babui tres filios.ynum masculi z duas feminasiz oés obierunt:erat aut gradus afcendés natiuitatis mafculi circa gradu ſaturni in mea natiuitate 7 ppter hoc mortuus est cito. Et erat gradus afcédens vni us femine in pifcibus in fextili Jouis in mea natiuitate z in trino lune mee na tiuitatis:7 erat fol ylech in figno capricomi. Et erat gradus afcendés alteri femine in capricorno.z hoc est mirabile: ppter qo ſciri póto natiuitates ſem per inter fe cócordat vna cuilla.Dicit byſpalenfis volens horá generádi fili um eligere mafculum:fit Jupiter in quouis angulo vel fit foli oriétalis fitos dis bore mafculus z melius est vt fignú aſcédés fit masculinů z ét eius dis. zsi luna fit fortunata a Joue erit melius. Pro femina accipe figna feminina 7 planetas femininos z fit Jupiter occidentalis.Dicit Daly abenragel qn in guta domo fuerit aliqua fortuna 7 dns gute domus liber ab'infortunijs z af pexerit medium celi natus multos bébit filios z viuent ac ibunt ad boni.Et fi fuerit cótrarium:cótrariú fignificat. Afpice in oibus natiuitatibus fi inuene ris luminaria infortunata non babentia ullum afpectum:fortune natus bas bebit paucos filios. In ſciédo tépus in quo habent filij aſpice ad planetaz qui babuerit plures dignitates in domofilior. fien ineneris in pre orietali bébit filios in iunétute fua.ſi eú iueneris medio celi hébit eos in medio etatis fue. Et fi in feptima bébit eos in pncipio ſenectutis.Si in ärta bébit eos cirs ca finé vite fue. Silr qn ann? puenerit ad fignű in quo fuerit Jupiter vel vesz nus in radice natiuitatis bébit filiŭ in illo anno.Dicit Daly abéragel ſi iterro gat? fuerit o muliere fi eft pgnás aut no afpice ſi dim afcedétis z lunamam bos iueneris in domo filior:aut fi inueneris dim dom filior in ascendente liberű ab ifortunis:dicas qo mulier illa fit pgnas.z fidis aſcédétis aut dis Domus filioru dederit vim fuam planeta exiftétiin angulo. Et fikr fidis afce détis receptus fuerit a recipies ipfum receptus.dicas qu est pregnans. Et fi Dominus afcendētis dederit vim fuam planete cadenti ab angulo:fignificat quod non est pregan.mubio muroileanbom shoog muo Exta domus. translation: The Fifth House. Here the author sets forth the significations of the fifth house. He says that this house signifies sons, both male and female. And it signifies affections and envoys or messengers and gifts. And what will happen after death, whether praise or blame. And Anduzga said that the first lord of the triplicity of the house of children signifies children and their life. The second signifies affections. The third signifies envoys or messengers. Ptolemy says, in the reasons for this house, that we should look at the planets existing in the place of the zenith of our heads, and at the place that follows it, which is the place of fortune, and at the place participating with them in the figure. Daly says in the commentary that the place which is the zenith of our heads is the tenth house. And the place that follows is the eleventh house, which is the house of fortune. And the rule by which we will know about children is taken from the planets existing in these places, or existing in places participating with them, such as the Ascendant and the seventh house, both of which participate with the tenth and eleventh. And if we find a planet in these places, we will look if we find any in the fourth or fifth house, and we will take the signification from there. Ptolemy says that we will judge the Moon, Venus, and Jupiter in giving children. The Sun, Mars, and Saturn in taking away, and we will give Mercury participation with whichever of these it participates with in the figure. Daly says in the commentary: If any of the fortunate planets are in the aforesaid places, it signifies that he will have children. If any of the unfortunate planets, he will have no children, and if he has any, they will be weak and few. And if Mercury has participation with the fortunate planets, it will be fortunate; if with the unfortunate, it will be unfortunate. Ptolemy says, and the opposition of the 144th part of the fourth part, you will be able to know the particular matters of children by considering, if you look well, in each of them, at the planet giving children, so that you place it in the place of the Ascendant, and you will know the other particulars of children generally, as by a nativity. Daly says in the commentary: What Ptolemy wants to relate in this place is what happened in my nativity. I looked and found Jupiter in the tenth house and in the 28th degree of Capricorn, aspecting itself with the Moon and Saturn, and this signified that I would have children and that they would die, because Saturn was with Jupiter in the tenth house. And because Jupiter was in a feminine sign, and the Moon likewise, it signified that there would be more than one, and that there would be more females. And because Jupiter was oriental, it signified that I would have a male. And so I had three children: one male and two females, and all died. The ascending degree of the male's nativity was around the degree of Saturn in my nativity, and because of this he died quickly. And the ascending degree of one female was in Pisces in the sextile of Jupiter in my nativity and in the trine of my Moon's nativity; and the Sun was hyleg in the sign of Capricorn. And the ascending degree of the other female was in Capricorn. And this is wonderful, for which reason it can be known that nativities always agree with each other. Hispalensis says, wishing to choose the hour for generating a male child: let Jupiter be in any angle, or let the Sun be oriental, and let the lord be male, and it is better that the ascending sign be masculine and also its lord. And if the Moon is fortunate by Jupiter, it will be better. For a female, take feminine signs and feminine planets, and let Jupiter be occidental. Daly Abenragel says: When there is any fortune in the fifth house, and the lord of the fifth house is free from misfortunes, and aspects the Midheaven, the native will have many children, and they will live and prosper. And if the contrary is true, it signifies the contrary. Look in all nativities: if you find the luminaries unfortunate, having no aspect, the native will have few children. In knowing the time when children will be had, look at the planet that has more dignities in the house of children. If you find it in the oriental part, he will have children in his youth. If you find it in the Midheaven, he will have them in the middle of his age. And if in the seventh, he will have them at the beginning of old age. If in the fourth, he will have them around the end of his life. Likewise, when the year comes to the sign in which Jupiter or Venus was in the radix of the nativity, he will have a child in that year. Daly Abenragel says: If you are asked about a woman, whether she is pregnant or not, look if you find the lord of the Ascendant and the Moon in the house of children; or if you find the lord of the house of children in the Ascendant, free from misfortunes, say that that woman is pregnant. And if the lord of the Ascendant or the lord of the house of children has given its strength to a planet existing in an angle. And likewise, if the lord of the Ascendant has been received by the one receiving it, say that she is pregnant. And if the lord of the Ascendant has given its strength to a planet falling from an angle, it signifies that she is not pregnant.
