translation: The Sixth House. Here are set forth the significations of the sixth house. It says that the sixth house signifies infirmities and servants, and it signifies the end of life and what will happen in old age. Anduzga said that the first lord of the triplicity of this house signifies infirmities and their increase or deterioration. The second signifies household slaves and servants. Note the difference between a household slave and a servant. For a household slave is the same as a domestic, and is called a purchased servant. A servant is one who is of servile condition. As Adam said in verse:

author: Alchabitius
title: Sixth House and Critical Days
text: cator. Eft pbbic verna cui no eft cara taberna.tertions triplicitas ſexte do mus fignificat qd boni vel mali eueniat fibi ex feruis z ecop vtilitate atqz opa. Et cu boc eft fignificator beſtiap z pecop z oiuz adrupediú que no equitant fignificat multitudiné vel paucitaté z fortitudiné cop z citu maebūt in ma nu ei'z qi recedét a ſuis maib.fignificat'ét carceres z retétiões. Dicit Ptole mein.60.ppone cétiloquij. Albobara fane z certe füt bore i qb declarant mutatiões morbor ad bonú vel ad malú nelociter Fzloca lúe in agulis ödra tipcluſi a circulo directo. Alteratiões do a predút has 7 idicat fút vera loca lúe in agulis almutems. Que at predút has fút loca lúei águlis būtib.i6.la tera. Et 15 postiž pceſſerit eé egritudis fz eğlitaté z nó acciderit aligd extriſe capturbet ifirmi.cúigit iuenerim B fortuna tá de fixis qz de erraticis fi gnificabit alterationé pſpera. Si vo in fortuna alterationé aduerfá nifi fuerit egritudini ipſa infortuna cótraria z in ſuo baiz. Luna vero in bis angulis fia gnificat morbos acutos 7 fol pliros. Et fikr ois planeta im pprias cófuetu dines. Paly in cóméto eiufdé dicit & Ptoleme docuit nos cam dierű de terminabiliú z qd fint a qui determinent de bono vel de malo z alterationes Fm ordine. Utilitas vo'in boc maior est medicis of astrologis.zboció quia multi philofophi cóueniút fup determinationes dicétes funt alterationes 7 plia inter nam egri z morbi acuti in ärta die z feptima die z.14.die 7.21.7 que augent ſcom boc accepta ſolo auditu z doctrina ſine ſciétia.z non ratio nant in boc nifi per illud o preterijt de experimentis fuis Sed Ptolemeus Dixit cam eiz patefecit vi fiat.z ca km Ptolemeŭ eſt qe na deuicta eſt a'moz bo in initio ei'z phibet ea'ne predat ei opa ſcoz eğlitaré nec repugnat illa morbo in hoza qua vincit:fed expectat vt fit luna in prio loco illiin quo fuit in pn morbi:qı tüc nó erit vis buoy g mouent in eo fic fuit in pn mobi.túc eni icitať vt repugnet illi ficut no agit prudés actor cu accuſato ifipiéte cũ vo luerit illi repugnare nifi ců eius virtus debilitata fuerit z in illo loco in quo careat auxiliatoribus. Et excitat illam.f.naturam in quarto ſigno a figno in quo incepit egritudo z luna exiſtente in illo qe ab omni figno quartum cótra rium eft nature eius:z ſimiliter ſeptimum:qr bec loca.f.quartus afpectus z oppofitus fignificant inimicicias 7 contrarietates. Et tempus quod eft iter initiű morbi z introirú lune in gradu quadrati in quo fuit in initio morbi per afcéfiones fignorum in circulo directo z piectiones radiorum funt ſeptem dies:z eodem modo quod eft inter lunam in initio morbi z ipfam in oppofi tione funt.14.dies:z quod eſt inter initium morbi z örtum afpectum ſcoum qui est contrarius initio morbi funt viginti vnus dies.z fecundum hunc mo dum pcedit modus aliorum dierum. Et erunt bec puncta anguli quadrati qué includit linea circuli directi:z illis punctis alia puncta funt indicatiuarz eft vt perueniat luna ad punctum qui abſcindit vnumquées arcum cui fub tendif latus de lateribus adrati per medium. Et in bis arcubus funt latera figure babétis octo angulos. Et omnia bec puncta cótraria funt punctis in gbus icepit morbus per nam. Igit obſeruet aſtrologus ad fuerit in bis pun ctis tam de ftellis firis quá de erraticis fiue fuerint fortune fiue ifortune.Et A fortune fuerit indicet quod fignificat victoria nature ſuper colera.infonie ==Start of OCR for page 1== vero fignificant qo colere ſaperabunt nam nifi fuerit infortuna cotraria mor bo z fortis in ſuo baiz.non enim fortunabit luram cum fuerit in ſuo baiz.fed remouebit morbum per hoc quod est illi contraria:z per hoc quod ifonuna est contraria morbo yt si fi fuerit frigida z morbus calidus:7 si fuerit calida z morbus frigidus z cum bis fimilibus in ſpecie diuerfitatum. Et dicit qu eéto lis in morbis prolixis fit ficut effe lune in morbis acutis quorum mais tépus erit orbis lune 7 in plixis orbis folis. Innuit etiam nobisj-ptolemeus subtis le gd:cum diceret omnis ftella ſcom quod est illi de moribus.Docuit quippe nos quod gegd immoderatu eſt in nobis z fuerit in moribus quos in coſue tudine non babemus dicit pro morbo. Et dixit iustum est hoc nifi accidat ali quid extrinfecus quod disturbet ordinem determinationis. z locuti funt de re que destruit determinationem bi qui expoſnerunt librum amphorifmoy ypocratis:z ideo non necefle eft nobis recitare in boc libro. Lota itétio pros lemei z Daly ſtat in hoc qö cauſa' quare.7.dies.7.14.7.21.funt dies cretici eft qe luna in ſeptima dic vt frequenter venit ad quartiuni afpectú loci in quo fu it in puncipio morbisz vt frequéter luna in.i4.die venit ad locum oppoſituz zin.21.die vt frequenter venit ad ſcom quartum afpectum. Ideo dicit Paly cum voluerit astrologus iudicare de die cretica debetobferuare temps quo luna veniet ad quartus afpectum loci in quo fuit in pncipio morbi z li túc có iungať cum planetis beniuolisiyel afpiciat ipfos aſpectu laudabili:vel ficos iungať vel afpiciat ftellas fixas que funt de natura fortunarum fignificat crifis erit ad bonum. Et fi coniungat vel afpiciat infortunas fignificat cótras rium nifi infonune fuerint contrarie materie morbisz cum boc fuerit infotu na in ſuo baim:quia tunc poteft fignificare bonum:sed hoc eft per accidens. Uerbi gratia:fi egritudo fit flegmatica z luna afpiciat martem fignificat boss num. Et quando luna peruenerit ad bonum locum cui fubtendit latus octo goni in equatore erit dies indicatiua. Latus octogani funt.45.gradus.zest medietas afpectus quarti.z licet non fit afpectus babet tamen maiorem có nenientiam cum afpectu iš alia locaz ideo quando luna'in quarto afpectu debet coniungi fortunis cum in medietate illius loci perueneritiam natura Fentit iuuamentum z incipit infurgere contra materiam morbi digerédoipe famiz ió tunc apparent figna digestionis in vrina z aliquádo fit crifis in är ta die.f.quádo luna inuenit ibi planetam fortunam: tunc figna digeftionis debent precedere in tempore quo luna peruenerit ad locum cui fubtendif la tus.i6.angulorum z bec est medietas octogoni.Sufficiant bec de caufis die rum creticorum. Dicit Ptolemeus in. 70.ppofitide centiloquij cum fuerit lu na in oppoſitione folis mixta ftellis nebulofis fignificat morbos infeparabi les in oculis. Et fimiliter fi fuerit occidentalis luna z in anguloz fuerint vtri qe mali occidétales afcédétes post lunama fol in angulo z vtriqs mali afcen détes ante folem z illi oppofiri.f.vnus alteri perdet natus vtrunqs oculum. Dicit Daly in comento eiufdem.maius impedimetum in duobus lamina ribus eft cu fuerint duo mali ideft faturn 7 mars afcédétes an folem z post lunaiz vn cop in oppoſitione alteri?.z fol fignificat oculum dextra.lung ves to finiftrum. Et in enitabile eft quin net amittet ytrucs oculum cuz fuerit la
translation: The third triplicity of the sixth house signifies what good or evil may befall one from servants and the utility and work of animals. And with this, it is a significator of beasts and cattle and all quadrupeds that are not ridden. It signifies their multitude or scarcity and their strength, and how many will be in his hand and how many will depart from his hands. It also signifies prisons and detentions. Ptolemy says in the 60th proposition of Centiloquium: "Albobara, truly and certainly, are the hours in which the changes of diseases are declared for good or for evil, or quickly. And the places of the Moon in angles are excluded from the direct circle. The alterations that predict these and indicate them are the true places of the Moon in the angles of the almuten. Those that predict these are the places of the Moon in the angles of the 16th side. And if 15 days have passed since the beginning of the illness, and it is equal, and nothing external has happened to disturb the sick person, then if we find a fortune, whether from fixed stars or wandering stars, it will signify a prosperous alteration. But if it is an adverse alteration in fortune, unless the misfortune itself is contrary to the illness and in its hayz. The Moon in these angles signifies acute diseases, and the Sun signifies chronic diseases. And similarly, every planet according to its own customs. Haly in his commentary on the same says that Ptolemy taught us the number of critical days and what they are and who determines good or evil and alterations in order. The utility in this is greater for physicians than for astrologers, and this is because many philosophers agree on the determinations, saying that there are alterations and many things between the nature of the sick and acute diseases on the fourth day and the seventh day and the 14th day and the 21st day, and these increase according to what is accepted by mere hearing and doctrine without science. And they do not reason in this except by what has passed from their experiments. But Ptolemy said and revealed how it happens. And according to Ptolemy, it is that nature is overcome by the disease at its beginning and prevents it from performing its operations according to equality, nor does it resist the disease at the hour it conquers, but waits for the Moon to be in the first place in which it was at the beginning of the disease, because then the strength of those who move in it will not be as it was at the beginning of the disease. Then it is incited to resist it, just as a prudent actor does not act with a foolish accused when he wants to resist him, unless his strength has been weakened and in that place where he lacks helpers. And it excites that nature in the fourth sign from the sign in which the illness began, and the Moon being in that which is contrary to its nature from every fourth sign, and similarly the seventh, because these places, that is, the fourth aspect and the opposition, signify enmities and contrarieties. And the time that is between the beginning of the disease and the entry of the Moon into the degree of the square in which it was at the beginning of the disease, through the ascensions of the signs in the direct circle and the projections of the rays, is seven days. And in the same way, what is between the Moon at the beginning of the disease and itself in opposition is 14 days. And what is between the beginning of the disease and the second aspect which is contrary to the beginning of the disease is 21 days. And according to this method, the method of the other days proceeds. And these points will be the angles of the square which the line of the direct circle includes. And to those points, other points are indicative, and it is that the Moon reaches the point that cuts off each arc whose side subtends one of the sides of the square through the middle. And in these arcs are the sides of the figure having eight angles. And all these points are contrary to the points in which the disease began by nature. Therefore, the astrologer should observe whether in these points, both from fixed stars and wandering stars, whether they are fortunes or misfortunes. And if it is a fortune, it indicates that it signifies the victory of nature over cholera. Misfortunes, however, signify that cholera will overcome nature, unless the misfortune is contrary to the disease and strong in its hayz. For it will not make the Moon fortunate when it is in its hayz, but it will remove the disease by being contrary to it, and by the misfortune being contrary to the disease, as if it were cold and the disease hot, or if it were hot and the disease cold, and with these similar things in species of diversities. And he says that the Sun in chronic diseases is like the Moon in acute diseases, whose greater time will be the orb of the Moon, and in chronic diseases, the orb of the Sun. Ptolemy also subtly hints to us when he says that every star is according to its customs. Indeed, he taught us that whatever is immoderate in us and is in customs that we do not have, he calls a disease. And he said this is just, unless something external happens that disturbs the order of determination. And those who expounded the book of Hippocrates' Aphorisms spoke of the thing that destroys determination, and therefore it is not necessary for us to recite it in this book. The whole intention of Ptolemy and Haly stands in this, that the reason why the 4th, 7th, 14th, and 21st days are critical days is that the Moon frequently comes to the fourth aspect of the place in which it was at the beginning of the disease on the seventh day, and the Moon frequently comes to the opposite place on the 14th day, and frequently comes to the second fourth aspect on the 21st day. Therefore, Haly says that when the astrologer wants to judge a critical day, he should observe the time when the Moon will come to the fourth aspect of the place in which it was at the beginning of the disease, and if it then conjoins with benevolent planets or aspects them with a laudable aspect, or if it conjoins or aspects fixed stars that are of the nature of fortunes, it signifies that the crisis will be for good. And if it conjoins or aspects misfortunes, it signifies the contrary, unless the misfortunes are contrary to the matter of the disease and with this the misfortune is in its hayz, because then it can signify good, but this is by accident. For example, if the illness is phlegmatic and the Moon aspects Mars, it signifies good. And when the Moon reaches a good place whose side subtends the octagon in the equator, it will be an indicative day. The side of the octagon is 45 degrees, and it is half of the fourth aspect. And although it is not an aspect, it has a greater convenience with the aspect than other places. And therefore, when the Moon is in the fourth aspect, it should be conjoined with fortunes when it reaches the middle of that place, then nature feels relief and begins to rise against the matter of the disease by digesting it, and then signs of digestion appear in the urine, and sometimes a crisis occurs on the fourth day, that is, when the Moon finds a fortunate planet there. Then the signs of digestion should precede at the time when the Moon reaches the place whose side subtends 16 angles, and this is the middle of the octagon. Let these suffice concerning the causes of critical days. Ptolemy says in the 70th proposition of Centiloquium that when the Moon is in opposition to the Sun mixed with nebulous stars, it signifies inseparable diseases in the eyes. And similarly, if the Moon is occidental and in the angles, both malefics are occidental, ascending after the Moon, and the Sun is in the angle, and both malefics are ascending before the Sun and opposite to it, that is, one to the other, the native will lose both eyes. Haly says in his commentary on the same that the greater impediment in the two luminaries is when there are two malefics, that is, Saturn and Mars, ascending before the Sun and after the Moon, and one of them is in opposition to the other. And the Sun signifies the right eye, and the Moon the left. And it is inevitable that he will lose both eyes when it is so.

Et fi fuerit in duabus Örtis occidétalib cóiugiű erit tardū aut copu labiť cú vetula. Et fi luna fuerit fub radijs'z pticipata fuerit cú ſaturno núž copulabif. Et si applicuerit mercurio:erit bona itelligens zbo noruz verboru. Et conuenit vt afpiciam cõingin mulierum a ftatu ſolis bos ra ſue natiuitatis ita y si fuerit ſol in duab ärtis oriétalib? cóiugiú mulieris britis talé natiuitaté: erit in ſua pueritia vel copulabiť puero poſtqz in dies procefferit. Et fi fuerit in duab ärtis occidétalibus:copulabiť tarde aut ves tulo poſtqz in dies procefferit. Et si fol fuerit pricipatus cum faturno erit ma ritus bone legis amator laboris. Et si habuerit pticipationé cũ Joue erit abe ftinétie bone nobilis cordis. Si cu marte erit fortis cordis fine vllo amore.Si cú venere erit limpidus.Sicum mercurio erit pfectuoſus in ſuis factis z fa ctor multarű rerú. Unde si dominus aſcédētis vel luna applicuerit domino ſeptime domus vel planeta a quo ſeparat luna planete cui applicat.vel fuerit dominus afcé détis vel luna in ſeptima domo ſignificat ob querés bébit ré queſitá tn cú pe titionib z pcibus multis. Et fi applicatio fuerit de ärto vel oppoſitione figni ficat pres ifta erit ti cú tarditate a labore z pena. Szfidis ſeptie domap plicuerit dño afcédentis vel pláeta cui applicat luna plaete a quo ipfa ssepârat vel fuerit dris ſeptime in afcendéte res illa leuiter fiet z cum magna valunta te mulieris z fue pris: maxime si applicatio illa fuerit de tertio ſextili. Et find iveneris aliquá applicationé inter fignificatores fignificat o res non erit.Si aligs querat a te ſi nocte illa cóplebit voluntaté fua cum muliere vel no afpi ce inueneris in qõne venere aſpiciétem afcendens.dico queſtio ſua cóple bit illa nocte. Et fi queſtio fuerit si babuerit rem cum muliere illa nocte pres terita veľ'nó iudica boc eodé mo nec plus nec minus. Si querak a te pro alis qua muliere ſi eſt virgo vel no aſpice aſcédens z dim ei? z luná z ſi eos inue neritis in ſignis fixis.dic q est vgo ſalua ab omni labe limpida. Et fi fuerit in fignis cómuniboyel mobilibus.dic y est mulier z bet vel babuit maritü.Et
translation: The significator in his nativity is in this manner. Nebulous stars are like the Twins, and the place where the water that Aquarius pours falls, the drops of Leo, and others that do not shine. Daly Abenragel says in the reasons for this house, we first want to speak in this chapter about the occasions and accidents that occur in the spirit, which are illnesses of the spirit. Afterwards, we will speak about those that occur in the body, which are illnesses of the body. Then he says, I say that demons are those who do not have Mercury with the Moon in any aspect in their nativities, with none of them aspecting the Ascendant; and the stronger one in his nativity, if it is diurnal, is Saturn; if nocturnal, Mars. And whichever of them is in the angles, this is the nativity of demons. When the Sun is afflicted by infortunes or by the lord of the house of illness, and is above the earth in a diurnal nativity, or the Moon is in such an affliction below the earth in a nocturnal nativity, the native will suffer blindness in the right eye. And if this misfortune of the Sun is below the earth in a nocturnal nativity, or above the earth for the Moon in a diurnal nativity, this damage and blindness will be in the left eye. When the Sun and Moon are both in the sixth house, afflicted, the native will undoubtedly be blind. When Mercury is conjoined with Saturn or in his square or opposition aspect, or is cadent from an angle in a vile and unfortunate place, or is combust or retrograde, or peregrine in a mute sign, the native, from any of these significations, will have a damaged or mute tongue. When Mercury is the lord of the sixth house, or in opposition to Saturn, the native will be deaf. The greater and stronger signification of muteness is when the lord of the Ascendant and the lord of its exaltation, and the lord of its triplicity, and Mercury and the Moon are all in mute signs. When the Sun is unfortunate or afflicted below the earth, it signifies affliction of the stomach. And when the Moon is afflicted below the earth, it signifies affliction of the lungs. And if Mars is in this manner, it signifies affliction of the liver. And if Saturn is in this manner, it signifies affliction of the spleen. And if Mercury is in this manner, it signifies affliction of the gall. Ptolemy says in the 89th proposition of the Centiloquium that it is detestable in the signification of illness for the significator of the question to enter under the rays, or for the Part of Fortune to be unfortunate. Daly Abenragel says in the questions of this house, if you are asked about someone ill, whether he will recover or not: look at the Moon and the Sun and the almuten of the Ascendant; and if they are free from misfortunes and do not have an aspect with the lord of the house of death, I say he will escape from that illness. And if two of them are as we said, he will likewise escape. And if the Moon and the almuten of the Ascendant, especially if she is the lady of the time (i.e., if the question is nocturnal), have an aspect with the lord of the house of death, he will not be able to be freed from death. And whenever any of the significators applies to an infortune, the illness will be strengthened until it separates from it. The time of death will be when the almuten of the Ascendant or the Moon conjoins with the lord of the house of death, or with the infortune that afflicted it, or applies to its square or opposition aspect. Here he places the significations of the seventh house. The seventh house. And he says that the seventh house signifies women and marriages and conditions and oppositions. Anduzgam

Dicit Daly Abenragel in qõninus huius domus.Si qs a te quefierit pro coniugio fi erit vel no aſpice afcendés z ei dominú z lunam z planetá a quo ſeparat z da bos pro fignificatorib qrétis z ſeptimá domá z eius dium z planeta cui applicuerit luna pro fignificatorib illius pro quo querif.z ſi querés fuerit maſculus cõiüge folem cú fignificatoib? fuis a fac eum pticipem in fignificatione.z ſi querés fuerit mulier da fibi venerez z fac ea pticipe in fignificative.poftmodú afpicias cuiusmodi applicationem béat ons afcendétis vel luna cum dñoſeptime domus a cuiufmodi applicationé beat planeta a quo ſeparat luna cum planeta cui applicat.vel venus cum fo le. Unde si dominus aſcédētis vel luna applicuerit domino ſeptime domus vel planeta a quo ſeparat luna planete cui applicat.vel fuerit dominus afcé détis vel luna in ſeptima domo ſignificat ob querés bébit ré queſitá tn cú pe titionib z pcibus multis. Et fi applicatio fuerit de ärto vel oppoſitione figni ficat pres ifta erit ti cú tarditate a labore z pena. Szfidis ſeptie domap plicuerit dño afcédentis vel pláeta cui applicat luna plaete a quo ipfa ssepârat vel fuerit dris ſeptime in afcendéte res illa leuiter fiet z cum magna valunta te mulieris z fue pris: maxime si applicatio illa fuerit de tertio ſextili. Et find iveneris aliquá applicationé inter fignificatores fignificat o res non erit.Si aligs querat a te ſi nocte illa cóplebit voluntaté fua cum muliere vel no afpi ce inueneris in qõne venere aſpiciétem afcendens.dico queſtio ſua cóple bit illa nocte. Et fi queſtio fuerit si babuerit rem cum muliere illa nocte pres terita veľ'nó iudica boc eodé mo nec plus nec minus. Si querak a te pro alis qua muliere ſi eſt virgo vel no aſpice aſcédens z dim ei? z luná z ſi eos inue neritis in ſignis fixis.dic q est vgo ſalua ab omni labe limpida. Et fi fuerit in fignis cómuniboyel mobilibus.dic y est mulier z bet vel babuit maritü.Et
translation: He said that the first lord of the triplicity of the seventh house signifies women. The second signifies contentions. The third, participations and mixtures. Ptolemy says in the reasons for this house, it is fitting that I look at the state of the Moon in the nativity hour of the man for marriage, so that first I look at the Moon: if it is in the two eastern quarters, the man's marriage will be in his youth or he will marry a young girl after he has advanced in years. And if it is in the two western quarters, the marriage will be late or he will marry an old woman. And if the Moon is under the rays and participates with Saturn, he will never marry. And if it applies to Saturn, he will marry a laborious and wicked woman. And if it applies to Jupiter, the woman will be of good abstinence and good ornament. And if it applies to Mars, she will be bold and proud. And if it applies to Venus, she will be beautiful and of good reception. And if it applies to Mercury, she will be good, intelligent, and of good words. And it is fitting that I look at the marriage of women from the state of the Sun in her nativity hour, so that if the Sun is in the two eastern quarters, the marriage of the woman who has such a nativity will be in her youth or she will marry a young man after she has advanced in years. And if it is in the two western quarters, she will marry late or an old man after she has advanced in years. And if the Sun participates with Saturn, the husband will be of good law, a lover of labor. And if he has participation with Jupiter, he will be of good abstinence, noble of heart. If with Mars, he will be strong of heart without any love. If with Venus, he will be pure. If with Mercury, he will be proficient in his deeds and a doer of many things. Daly Abenragel says in the questions of this house: If someone asks you about marriage, whether it will happen or not, look at the Ascendant and its lord and the Moon and the planet from which it separates, and give these as significators of the querent; and the seventh house and its lord and the planet to which the Moon applies as significators of the one about whom the question is asked. And if the querent is male, conjoin the Sun with its significators and make it participate in the signification. And if the querent is female, give her Venus and make her participate in the signification. Afterwards, look at what kind of application the lord of the Ascendant or the Moon has with the lord of the seventh house, and what kind of application the planet from which the Moon separates has with the planet to which it applies, or Venus with the Sun. Therefore, if the lord of the Ascendant or the Moon applies to the lord of the seventh house, or the planet from which the Moon separates applies to the planet to which it applies, or the lord of the Ascendant or the Moon is in the seventh house, it signifies that the querent will have the thing sought, but with many petitions and prayers. And if the application is by square or opposition, it signifies that this will be with delay and labor and pain. But if the lord of the seventh house applies to the lord of the Ascendant, or the planet to which the Moon applies applies to the planet from which it separates, or the lord of the seventh is in the Ascendant, that thing will happen easily and with great willingness of the woman and her part, especially if that application is by trine or sextile. And if you find no application between the significators, it signifies that the thing will not happen. If someone asks you if he will fulfill his desire with a woman that night or not, look if you find Venus aspecting the Ascendant in the question. I say his desire will be fulfilled that night. And if the question is whether he had intercourse with that woman last night or not, judge this in the same way, no more and no less. If you are asked about a woman, whether she is a virgin or not, look at the Ascendant and its lord and the Moon, and if you find them in fixed signs, say that she is a virgin, safe from all blemish, pure. And if she is in common or mutable signs, say that she is a woman and has or had a husband. And

Audiui ſigdem a patre meo cui deus parcato cum ipe fuiffet cú bis qui ſe absconderunt a facie impatoris.cú abrabam filio almos thedio quotidie viſitabat eum occulte albaſten filius abrahe astrologus:q pcepit vt afferret concha eneá magná aqua plena in qua ponés ſcabellű có fuluit ei vt deſuper ſederet in maiori pte diei.z hoc ideo pcepit vt faceret ers rare astrologos imperatores in effe abfconditoriz cuz impatos quereret ab astrologis fuis ubi ille eet:dixerunt o effet in medio maris.Dicit haly abéra gel quando mars fuerit in octaua domo damnificans dim octaue domus mors nati erit ex ferro. Et ſi ſaturnus fuerit in octaua domo danificás dim octaue domus m028 nati erit ppter carceré z penas. Quádo luna fuerit in octaua domo cuz cauda draconis fignificat' mors nati eſt ppter feceſſum vel medicina laxatiua. Quádo mercurius fuerit in octaua domo cũ cauda fi gnificat q mors nati erit ppter mala opera aut ppter toxicum aut facta nis gromantie. On gradus occidétis z eius dris ambo dánati fuerit nato figni Acant malam mortem. Qn fol fuerit dänatus in natiuitate diurna vel luna innocturna ſignificar illud idé.Qn fignificatores Dánati fuerint fup terram manifesta morté fignificant a patentem. Et qn dánati fuerint fub terra mois erit abſcódita z occulta.Quádo aliqua infortunaruz fuerit in octaua domo vel iuncta domino octaue domus ſignificat fortem z malam moné.Et fi alt qua fortunay fuerit in octaua domo vel iuncta domino octaue dom fignifi cat bonam z pulchram morte. Et fi iupiter vel venus fuerit in bac domo for tis z liber a radijs infortunarum natus erit fortunatus 7 laudatus z vinet annis.73.7 forte plus. translation: If the question is about a girl who says she is a virgin, say: she is corrupted and has had relations with others. If someone asks you about a woman, how many husbands she will have, look from the lord of the tenth house to the degree of Mars, and as many planets as you find between them, so many husbands she will have. And if Mars is in the seventh house, look from Mars to Jupiter in the same way and judge according to that, and you will be certain with God. Here are placed the significations of the eighth house. The eighth house is the house of death and fear, and it signifies inheritances of the dead which the heirs should possess after death. It also signifies the end of the years of life after old age. Anduzgamus says that the lord of the triplicity of this house signifies fear. The second signifies precepts and ancient things. The third signifies inheritances of the dead. Ptolemy says it is fitting that you look at the state of the degree of the killer, which planet is there or aspects it, and according to the nature of that planet will be the judgment of death. So if Saturn is the lord of death, it will occur due to long illnesses, due to consumption, namely rheumatism, and due to a defect of nature, etc., such things. And if Jupiter is the lord of death, death will be due to apostemata of the throat and lungs, etc., of this kind. And if it is Mars, death will be due to continuous and acute fevers. And if it is Venus, death will occur due to illness of the stomach and liver and heart, and by flux of blood and apostemata going through the body and fistulas. And if it is Mercury, death will be due to epilepsy and due to foolishness and loss of sense. Haly says in the commentary on the 15th proposition of the fourth part: Ptolemy divided death for us into two kinds: one comes from illnesses. The other comes from a thing in which the physician has nothing to see, such as one who dies by sword, spear, fall, fire, and bite of beast and wild animal, or drowning in water, and many other occasions. And when the governing planets are safe from misfortunes and in their own dignities and powerful, and when no contrary planet is elevated over them, then death will occur due to illnesses. But another death occurs when the planets are unfortunate or weak or in alien dignities contrary to them, or when contrary planets are elevated over them. To narrate all the ways in which death occurs would be too long here, and whoever wishes to find this will find it in the fourth part of Ptolemy's Quadripartitum. Ptolemy says in the 74th proposition of the Centiloquium: When Mars is corporally conjoined to the head of Algol and the Moon does not aspect the ascendant, nor is Fortune in the 8th house, and the lord of the anareta of the luminaries is opposite Mars or in its fourth aspect, the native's head will be cut off. But if the luminary is in the midheaven, he will be hanged. And if malefics aspect from Gemini and Pisces, his hands and feet will be cut off. Haly says in the commentary that when Fortune is in the eighth house, it prohibits a bad death. Indeed, I heard from someone who was with the very spirited son of Haly at sea, who, when he wanted to enter the sea, which was too tumultuous with winds agitating the waves, rebuked him, saying: I do not fear death at sea. For Jupiter was in my nativity in the house of death and oriental, which prohibits a bad death for me. And I saw him die in bed. It also happened to me that Adam's servants showed me the nativity of their lord's son, and I found the Sun in the midheaven, and he was the lord of the anareta and hylech, and Mars in his fourth aspect, and Saturn in the angle of his earth, the ascendant being Pisces. Therefore, terrified, I delayed giving judgment, saying: it is necessary that before I judge, the days of nourishment be completed. But when I was very solicitous about the boy, investigating his movements or deaths, I understood nothing from which I would fear for him the truncation of hands and feet and hanging, since he was gentle and modest. But when he reached 30 years, Adam's men entered the house, and fleeing, they were accused of fraud against the one who ruled the city, and they were caught in that house, and he with them lost his hands and feet and was hanged. And I saw him without hands and feet, hanged. Ptolemy says in the 59th proposition of the Centiloquium: when you are asked about an absent person, do not judge his death until you remove drunkenness from him, nor a wounded person until you remove bloodletting, nor acquired substance until you remove money entrusted to him. For the judgment is the same for these men. Haly says in the commentary there: he who deals with judgments judges by forms that are close to the truth. Therefore, there is no difference in the strength of judgments between a sleeping person and a dead person, and between a wounded person and a bloodletting person, nor between him to whom money is entrusted and him who acquired it. Indeed, I heard from my father, may God spare him, when he was with those who hid from the emperor's face, with Abraham, son of Almos, whom Albashten, son of Abraham, an astrologer, visited daily secretly, who advised him to bring a large bronze shell full of water, in which he placed a stool, and advised him to sit on it for the greater part of the day. And he advised this so that he might make the emperor's astrologers err in the matter of his hiding, and when the emperor asked his astrologers where he was, they said that he was in the middle of the sea. Haly Aberagel says that when Mars is in the eighth house, damaging the lord of the eighth house, the native's death will be by iron. And if Saturn is in the eighth house, damaging the lord of the eighth house, the native's death will be due to prison and punishments. When the Moon is in the eighth house with the Dragon's Tail, it signifies that the native's death is due to a secession or a laxative medicine. When Mercury is in the eighth house with the Tail, it signifies that the native's death will be due to evil deeds or due to poison or acts of necromancy. When the degree of the occident and its lord are both damaged, they signify a bad death for the native. When the Sun is damaged in a diurnal nativity or the Moon in a nocturnal nativity, it signifies the same. When the significators are damaged above the earth, they signify a manifest and open death. And when they are damaged below the earth, death will be hidden and secret. When any unfortunate planet is in the eighth house or conjoined to the lord of the eighth house, it signifies a strong and bad death. And if any fortunate planet is in the eighth house or conjoined to the lord of the eighth house, it signifies a good and beautiful death. And if Jupiter or Venus is in this house, strong and free from the rays of the unfortunate planets, the native will be fortunate and praised and will live 73 years, and perhaps more. Here are placed the significations of the ninth house. The ninth house signifies peregrinations and long journeys, and it signifies epistles and envoys and rumors and dreams. Anduzgamus says that the lord of the triplicity of this house signifies peregrinations and everything that happens to him in peregrinations; the second signifies faith and religion; the third signifies wisdom and

author: Alchabitius
title: The Tenth House
text: fomnia.Dicit Daly Abéragel in rõnibus huius domus afpice in itinerib? na ti pamitus a dño triplicitatis domus itineris:qm qn dis triplicitatis domus itineris pmus fuerit in bono ſtatu z forris fortunatus z receptus fignificat onatus ibi per itinera z mouebit ſe de uno loco ad aliuz 7 erit fortunatus in itineribuis.Et fi fuerit in diuerſo ſtatu ab eo qd dixim habebit impedi méta z grauamina in itineribus z motib ſuis z erit uilipéſus in eis z extras betur z expellet de terra z loco ſuo nec i ſuis itinerib aliquá utilitaté vel lu crum babebit. Et si dominus triplicitatis domus itineris.fecúdus fuerit in bono ſtatu fortiatus z receptus narus erit bone legis religiofus a custos legis: Et si fuerit in diuerſo ſtatu erit male legis nominabit malus:7 de ma lis operibus z incredulus. Et si dñs triplicitatis tertius fuerit in bono ftatu natus erit veridicon ſomnioz:7 quicquid videbit in ſomnijs veridicum eritt nec mentiek.Dicit idem baly ſcias y Mercurius h3poffe ac fignificationez propria in cauſa legis z legalitatis ſepatim ab alijs planetis. Etſcias y qi fuerit in domo ſaturni vkin afpectu eius natus erit pfundarum cogitationú firmus in credulitate:laudat res alterius müdi magis oz istius:z credit eas ac multu cogitat in illis 7 amat easiz tenet o res illius mundi fint meliores oz istius.abbonet ludos ioculationes z folatia:bumilis 7 patiés laboris ac indigétie maxime si fortune cadétes fuerint non afpicientes afcendens.Etfi Mercurius fuerit in domo Jouis:vel in eius afpectu natus erit boni nois bone legis z legalitat 7 legis i fuerit obferuator. Et si fuerit in domo mars tis:vel in eius afpectu: 7 fuerit idem aſpectus de quarto vel oppoſitõe nat rit bomicidairafcibilis malefactor male legis z vilipedet lege fuam. up Ecima domus, bic ponit fignificationes decime est domus regni z fublimatióis z exaltationis memorie a vocis in imperado z magiſterioų atos matri. Et fignificat dimidiū ans domus.Dicit anduzgaz dis triplicitatis decime. Primus A gnificat opus nati z exaltatióez ſuam z ſedis ſublimatióez.ſcos fignificat vos cem imperij z audacia in imperado.tertius fignificat ſtabilitatem atos dura bilitatem eius.i.qtum durabit in dnatioue fua.Dicit Ptolemeusin. 5.p20 pofitione centiloquij. Afcendétia inimicoų regni ab eiufdem afcendente cas dentia funt z afcendentia dominantiű in illo angulo eius.Ascendentia vero miniſtrantium ſuccedentia angulus.Liuitatum autem afcendentia in earuz edificationibus iudicant omnia que in eis occident. Que vero ascendentia funt in regum ordinationibus quarūdam ciuitatum proniciant omnia que fub eomm regno fient:z afcendens apparitionis alicuius fecte in illis ſcilicz civitatibus demonstrat quicquid fub illa fecta futurum est in ipfis ciuitatis bus.Dicit ibi baly in comento o ſigna cadétia funt quoi nullus eſt angus lus vel poſt angulum: funt quattuos:fcilicet duodecimű tertium ſextů zno num ab afcendente. Et dicit & notum eft omni astrologo & cum fuerit afcé dens natiuitatis:aut aliquis angulus eius afcendens aut aliquis anguloz inceptionis regni fueritos natiuitatis alicuius cui regnum congruat perfis cietur ei regnum in illo regno:erites ei in illo honor 7 no in alio. Quippe res ges antiqui omnium nafcentium in regnis fuis natinitates obferuabant: z cuiufcus afcendens deprehédebant domů inimicorű regniuel ſextum uel tertium vel nonum interficiebant ipfum puerú qe regnü ipfius effet contra regnú eop. Et paucis interpofitis dicit q afcendens alicuius ciuitatis eft fis gnum cuius afcenfione incipit quis collocare primú lapidem in illa.Ascend dens fecte eft afcendens fignú in tpe quo illa fecta incipit preualere fup alia fectam z fufceperut eam imozantes:z illud fignificat quátum durabit: 7 ge qd accidet in illa qždiu durauerit. Dicit Daly abenragel qn fignú medij celi fuerit ex fignis igneis maxime si fuerit leo vraries:z ſol in eo:z ps regis fue ritibi in afpectu fortunay natus fine vlla dubitatione rex erit. M Adecima domus. Dic ponit fignificatiões vns deime domus. Dicit o vn decima domus est domus fortuner fiducie atos laudis: zami cop z miniſtrop z auxiliator:z ſignificat postremam medietatez medie vite. Dixit Andu gaz dominus triplicitatis domus fi ducie primus fignificat fiduciam.Secudus fignificat amicos. Tertius ſigni ficat vtilitates ſeu profectus eorum.Dicit Ptolemeus in.39.ppone centilo quij:eſſe malu vndecime domus z eius domini in intronifatione regű figni ficat quid accidet in confiliatorib? ſuis z eius ſubſtantia de maloiz eodé mo do erit malum eſſe ſecunde ſignificans: modicu adipifcetur.populus cuz illo rege.Dicit baly in comento. Aftrologi coueneruntfuper hoc domus decima in omni ordinatione regum est locus eiusiz vndecima auxiliatowz eius z ei ſubſtatie z aſcendēs eſt populi:z fecunda ſubſtantie populi:z ideo impedit vndecima cófiliatores:z fecúda ſubſtantiam populi. Dicit baly abe ragel in rõnibus hu domus.Afpice ad dim triplicitatis huius domus p mum:iinueneris eű receptú fortunatú z fortem indica y natus bébit ami cos multos lucra vtilitaté z bonú peis. Et fi fuerit infortunat Danat nat erit ſolitarius ab hominib nó bis amiciciam cú aliquo z retrabet z elonga bit ſe ab illis qui procurant amorem z ſocietatem ipſius. Et si dis triplicita tis ipfius domus.fecúdus fuerit fortis fortunatus z receptus erit fortunat bonoratus 7 diues abundans in bono ſtatu z bone vite. Et si fueritab boc Itatu diuerfus erit laborioſus:z indigens ac paupertatis z miferie manifes ſte. Et si dis triplicitatis ipfius domus tertius fuerit fortunatus fotis ac in bono ſtatu natus habebit filios remanfuros poſt eum in bono ſtatu z dini tijs z fortuna ſua. Et si fuerit infortunatus debilis a cadés ac in malo ſtatu natus no babebit filium q poſt en remaneat nec heredezfue generationis. Et ſcias q melior ſtatus of eſſe poſſit in amicorú cauſa est vt dominus afce dentis fit receptus a domino vndecime domus eodeni domino vndecime domus exiftente fortuna z in bono ſtatu.quonias quando fic fuerit babebit amozem z conuenientiam cum ſocijs z amicis. Aodecima domus, bic ponit fignificatiões 12.dom. q.i2. Domus fignificat inimicos z labores triſticia atos inuidias 7 fu furrationes z ingenia atqz bestias que equitant z fignificat fine S
translation: Daly Abenragel says in the reasons for this house: look at the journeys of the native, and then at the lord of the triplicity of the house of journeys. For when the lord of the triplicity of the house of journeys is first in good state, strong, fortunate, and received, it signifies honors there through journeys, and he will move from one place to another and will be fortunate in journeys. And if he is in a state different from what we have said, he will have impediments and burdens in his journeys and movements, and he will be despised in them, and he will be extracted and expelled from his land and place, nor will he have any utility or profit in his journeys. And if the lord of the triplicity of the house of journeys is second, and is in good state, strong, and received, the native will be of good law, religious, and a keeper of the law. And if he is in a different state, he will be of bad law, will be called evil, and will be of evil works and incredulous. And if the lord of the triplicity is third, and is in good state, the native will be a truthful interpreter of dreams, and whatever he sees in dreams will be true and will not lie. The same Daly says: know that Mercury has power and proper signification in the cause of law and legality, separately from other planets. And know that if he is in the house of Saturn or in his aspect, the native will be of deep thoughts, firm in belief, praising the things of the other world more than this one, and believing them and thinking much about them and loving them, and holding that the things of that world are better than this one. He will abhor games, jokes, and comforts; humble and patient of labor and indigence, especially if the falling fortunes are not aspecting the ascendant. And if Mercury is in the house of Jupiter, or in his aspect, the native will be of good name, good law and legality, and will be an observer of the law. And if he is in the house of Mars, or in his aspect, and the same aspect is from the fourth or opposition, the native will be a murderer, irascible, a malefactor, of bad law, and will despise his law. The tenth house. Here he places the significations of the tenth house. It is the house of kingdom and sublimation and exaltation of memory and voice in command and magistracies and also of the mother. And it signifies the middle half of the house. Andugaz says: the lord of the triplicity of the tenth house. The first signifies the work of the native and his exaltation and the sublimation of his seat. The second signifies the voice of command and boldness in commanding. The third signifies its stability and durability, that is, how long it will last in its dominion. Ptolemy says in the 5th proposition of the Centiloquium: the ascendants of the enemies of the kingdom are those falling from the ascendant of the same, and the ascendants of those dominating in that angle of it. But the ascendants of the ministers are the succeeding angles. The ascendants of cities, however, in their constructions, judge all things that will happen in them. But the ascendants that are in the ordinances of kings of certain cities predict all things that will happen under their kingdom. And the ascendant of the appearance of any sect in those cities, namely, shows whatever will be under that sect in those cities. Daly says there in the commentary that the cadent signs are those of which there is no angle or after the angle; there are four: namely, the twelfth, third, sixth, and ninth from the ascendant. And he says that it is known to every astrologer that when the ascendant of a nativity, or any angle of it, is the ascendant or any angle of the inception of a kingdom, or of the nativity of someone to whom a kingdom is suitable, a kingdom will be perfected for him in that kingdom; and there will be honor for him in that, and not in another. Indeed, ancient kings observed the nativities of all born in their kingdoms, and whose ascendant they found to be the house of the enemies of the kingdom, or the sixth or third or ninth, they killed the child, because his kingdom would be against their kingdom. And after a few interpolations, he says that the ascendant of any city is the sign whose ascent begins when someone places the first stone in it. The ascendant of a sect is the ascendant sign at the time when that sect begins to prevail over another sect and the inhabitants have adopted it; and that signifies how long it will last, and what will happen in it as long as it lasts. Daly Abenragel says: when the sign of the midheaven is of the fiery signs, especially if it is Leo or Aries, and the Sun is in it, and the part of the king is there in the aspect of fortune, the native will undoubtedly be a king. The eleventh house. Here he places the significations of the eleventh house. He says that the eleventh house is the house of fortune, trust, and praise, and of friends and ministers and helpers; and it signifies the last half of middle life. Andugaz said: the lord of the triplicity of the house of trust, the first, signifies trust. The second signifies friends. The third signifies their utilities or profits. Ptolemy says in the 39th proposition of the Centiloquium: the evil of the eleventh house and its lord in the enthronement of kings signifies what will happen to his counselors and his substance from evil; and in the same way, the evil of the second will signify that the people will gain little with that king. Daly says in the commentary: astrologers have agreed on this: the tenth house in every ordination of kings is its place, and the eleventh is of his helpers and his substance, and the ascendant is of the people, and the second is of the substance of the people; and therefore the eleventh impedes the counselors, and the second the substance of the people. Daly Abenragel says in the reasons for this house: look at the lord of the triplicity of this house, the first. If you find him received, fortunate, and strong, indicate that the native will have many friends, gains, utility, and good from them. And if he is unfortunate and damaged, the native will be solitary among men, will not have friendship with anyone, and will withdraw and distance himself from those who seek his love and companionship. And if the lord of the triplicity of this house, the second, is strong, fortunate, and received, he will be fortunate, honored, and rich, abundant in good state and good life. And if he is different from this state, he will be laborious, and indigent, and manifestly poor and miserable. And if the lord of the triplicity of this house, the third, is fortunate, strong, and in good state, the native will have children who will remain after him in good state, and riches, and his fortune. And if he is unfortunate, weak, and cadent, and in bad state, the native will not have a son who remains after him, nor an heir of his generation. And know that the best state that can be in the cause of friends is that the lord of the ascendant is received by the lord of the eleventh house, the same lord of the eleventh house being fortunate and in good state. For when it is so, he will have love and agreement with companions and friends. The twelfth house. Here he places the significations of the 12th house. He says that the 12th house signifies enemies and labors, sadness and envy, and whisperings and cunning, and beasts that are ridden, and it signifies the end.

translation: Anduzgar says that the first day of the triplicity of this house signifies enemies, the second signifies labors, the third great beasts. He concludes by saying: 'Best of all, it signifies.' Daly Abenragel says in the judgments of this house: 'Look first at the lord of the triplicity of this house. If you find him fortunate, strong, and elevated, judge that the native's enemies will lean upon him and do whatever they wish. But if he is cadent, afflicted, combust, or peregrine, the native will lean upon his enemies, and they will be hindered, and the evil they intended for the native will befall them. And if the lord of the triplicity of this house is fortunate, the native will have moderate sorrows and anxieties. But if he is in an adverse state, there will be many impieties and sorrows, and labors, impediments, and great losses will befall him in what he possesses and in his body. And if the lord of the third triplicity of this house is fortunate, the native will be dialectical and of good eloquence, and fortunate in his reasons and causes, and he will prosper against any adversary. But if he is in an adverse state, he will be weak in reason and impeded in speech, and he will have no right against his adversaries and evildoers.'

Et dicit fi interrogatus fueris & ſubſtatia.z uolueris ſcire qe planetarú fit do minator five fignificatoreiz fuerit ſeba dom que est domſubſtatie..gras dus arietis:q2 aries est dom martis hét ibi mars que fotitudines. Et eft exaltatio ſolis z ió fol hét öttuor fortitudines ratione illi?. Et éét ſolis triplis sii citas z róne illi bét ibi tres fortitudines:z est terminus iouis:z rone illi hét ibi duas fortitudines:7 facies martis:z róne illi bét ibi vnam fortitudinem. Soligit bétibi.7.fortitudies z mars sex z Jupiter duas patet ergo q fol bet ibi plures fortitudines.Et talis planeta.f.babés plures fortitudines vocať almutamidest vincens.ergo fol in tali figura eflet fignificator ſubſtatie z co dem modo faciendum eſt, de alijs. X poteftatibus quoqz planeta rú accidétalibus. Itaps priuat fic. pu auctor determiauit de dignitatibus planetay céntialib. Dic determiat de dignitatib iplop accidétalis biz expedir ſe breuiffime dicés:qé vna gignitas accitalis pláctaų į vocať baiz z eft hec vt planeta diurn fit in die ſup terra z i nocte fub terra.Et pláe ta nocturn fit i nocte ſup terra z i die fub terra. Et et ſi sit plaeta mafculing fit in figno masculino z feminin'i figno feminino túc dé'plácta eé i ſuo baiz.i. in ſua ſikitudine:z erit túc fortitudo fua ficut fortitudo viri in loco fui pfect lucriates fotune. Et fi cópleta eſt expo įme dije alchabicijitroductonj ad magifteriú iudicioz astroy. Quida bo iterrogauit de ida abſéte vtrü eét mostu vel viu z fuit afcendés qonis.20.gradus leonis z icidit talis figura. H ४ ચોવીધ P.18.2 16.0225 2 20 18 τι ㅍ 11 43.27mp 18.42.४ 83611 04.26. Interrogatio de abſente utrum ui uat uel ne. 8 4.٧ 8.28. 20 81 815.25. X
translation: The twelve houses also signify colors. Here are the significations of the houses as to colors: The first and seventh are white. The second and twelfth are green. The third and eleventh are yellow. The fourth and tenth are red. The fifth and ninth are the color of honey. The sixth and eighth are black. Afterwards, it says that each of the planets has an accidental power in some of these houses, which is called joy. Mercury rejoices in the Ascendant. The Moon rejoices in the third house. Venus in the fifth. Mars in the sixth. The Sun in the ninth. Jupiter in the eleventh. Saturn in the twelfth. Then it says that the angles signify strength, order, and perfection. Those cadent from the angles, however, signify weakness and detriment, except for the ninth and third houses. The third signifies a suitable thing, revealed with fame, and also that these are the joys of the luminaries, as it was already said that the Sun rejoices in the ninth and the Moon in the third. And the twelfth and sixth houses signify concealment and vileness of things. Afterwards, it says that the angles, namely the first house, the tenth, the seventh, and the fourth, and the lords of these angles, signify greatness of honor and fortune, and remoteness from fall and misfortune. And the presence of the lords of the angles in the cadent houses from the angles signifies misfortune and disgrace of falls. And the second house succeeds the tenth, and its lord signifies medium fortune, that is, it signifies fortune, but not as great as the tenth. And the fifth house succeeds the fourth, signifying medium fortune through gifts and the cause of children. And the eighth house and its lord signify medium fortune through the cause of ministers and helpers. And the eighth house and its lord signify medium fortune through the cause of inheritances from the dead and from hidden things.

translation: Afterwards, it sets forth the significations of the lords of the angles when they are present in those angles. And it says that the presence of the lord of the Ascendant in the Ascendant signifies his fortune through himself and through his own acquisition. And if he is in the seventh, it signifies acquisition through wives. And if he is in the tenth, it signifies acquisition of magistracies. And if he is in the fourth, it signifies acquisitions through inheritances and through the cause of fathers and through the production of waters, the population of lands, and from ancient and radical things (another letter has 'and radical things'). The presence of the lord of the tenth in the tenth house signifies fortune through the king and great kingdom and high magistracies. And if he is in the seventh, it signifies kingdom through victory, contention, and through the cause of men. And if he is in the fourth, it signifies kingdom through the cause of the king's ministers and through the cultivation of lands and through the building of cities and the division of rivers and through the guardianship of cities and from ancient things. And if he is in the Ascendant, it signifies kingdom through ingenuity and through the king's ingenuity and from the things of the common people.

translation: The presence of the lord of the seventh house in the seventh house signifies fortune through negotiations and through conventions and commendations, that is, deposits and through wives and through benefactors. And if he is in the fourth, it signifies fortune through conventions and negotiations and through the causes of fathers and inheritances and the cultivation of land. And if he is in the Ascendant, it signifies fortune through conventions and negotiations and through the causes of medicine and astronomy and through spiritual things and ingenuity and other similar things. And if he is present in the tenth, it signifies fortune through conventions and negotiations and through men and through the causes of the kingdom. The presence of the lord of the fourth house in the fourth house signifies fortune from fruit and through the causes of fathers and through ancient things. And if he is present in the Ascendant, it signifies fortune from the cultivation of land and fruit through depth of counsel and ingenuity; and if he is present in the tenth house, it signifies profit from the cultivation of land and fruit through the cause of kings and magistracies. And if he is present in the seventh house, it signifies fortune from the cultivation of land and fruit and from the part of the wife and through the cause of men and benefactors and through negotiations. Then it says that these things signify the lords of the angles when they are present in those angles. And as it was said about the angles, so you should understand about the other houses in their own way, according to the nature of each house. It says that it introduced the lords of the angles so that they might be an example in the other houses. When you wish to know the ruling planet, the author teaches how to choose the significator, and it can be divided into two parts: in the first part, it does what has been said. In the second part, it gives an example there. For example, the author's opinion is this: that in knowing the significator of a thing, we must look at two things. First, we must look at the house in which the nature of that thing whose significator we seek is located. Second, we must look at the planet that by its nature signifies that thing. For example, if a question is asked about substance, we must look at the second house, which is the house of substance; and with this, we must look at Jupiter, who by his own nature signifies substance. And if a question is asked about a woman, we must look at the seventh house, which is the house of a woman, and with this, we must look at Venus, who by her own nature is the significatrix of women. And so for others, according to the proper natures of the houses and planets. It is to be understood, however, that the planet we choose from the part of the house we must hold as the principal significator. And the planet that signifies the nature of the thing we must make a participant in the signification. Therefore, the meaning of the text is: when you wish to know the ruling planet or significator of a thing, look at which of the planets has more strengths in the house of the thing, from the strengths mentioned in the chapter on their strengths and dignities. And the one who abounds in the number of strengths, that one is the ruler or significator. And it is to be understood that in interrogations, the Ascendant or the first house is always attributed to the querent. The planet abounding in the number of strengths in the house of the thing about which the question is asked is the significator of the thing sought. The author gives an example in the text. And he says: if you have been asked about substance, and you wish to know which of the planets is the ruler or significator, and the second house, which is the house of substance, is, for example, the degree of Aries, because Aries is the house of Mars, Mars has strengths there. And it is the exaltation of the Sun, and therefore the Sun has four strengths by reason of that. And it is the triplicity of the Sun, and by reason of that, it has three strengths there; and it is the term of Jupiter, and by reason of that, it has two strengths there; and the face of Mars, and by reason of that, it has one strength there. Therefore, the Sun has seven strengths there, and Mars six, and Jupiter two. It is clear, therefore, that the Sun has more strengths there. And such a planet, that is, having more strengths, is called Almuten, that is, the victor. Therefore, the Sun in such a figure would be the significator of substance, and in the same way, it must be done for others.

translation: Also, concerning the accidental powers of the planets. Thus, the author first determined the essential dignities of the planets. Here he determines their accidental dignities, and he explains himself very briefly, saying that there is an accidental dignity of the planets called Hayz, and it is this: that a diurnal planet is above the earth by day and below the earth by night. And a nocturnal planet is above the earth by night and below the earth by day. And if a masculine planet is in a masculine sign and a feminine planet in a feminine sign, then that planet is in its Hayz, that is, in its similitude; and its strength will then be like the strength of a man in the place of his perfect gain of fortune. And if the exposition of the first part of Alchabitius's Introduction to the Art of Judgments of the Stars is complete. Someone asked about an absent person, whether he was dead or alive, and the Ascendant of the question was the 20th degree of Leo, and such a figure occurred. Question about an absent person, whether he is alive or not.

Inueni etiá lunam feparatam a venere euntem ad coniunctionedo mini afcendentis z tranftulit naturam domini domus mortis ad dominuz afcendentis. Doc fuit certius fignum mortis. Quinto ueneris. Lirca qua pté eſt intelligenduz quádo dicit anni fridarie ſaturni funt.i i intelligédum eft annus guberna tionis in vita nati. Ad cuius euidentiam eſt ſciendum & in natiuitate diurna icipit fridaria a ſole z gubernat ipfum.io.annis. poftea gubernat ipſum ves nus ſcơm quátitate annoy ſuorum. Deinde mercurius: fic km ordinezpla netarum eundo vsqzad finem vite. Si autem natiuitas fuerit nocturna inci pit gubernatio a luna z gubernat ipfum fm quatitaté annoy fuoy. Poſt lu nam gubernatipſum ſaturnus ini quátitaté annoru ſuoyiz fic from ordiné planetay.De bac mã est capľm ppui in quarta differétia zibidiffusius los quet de bac domino cocedente. Quando autem dicit in littera:maximi voz giij inteligendum eſt de annis quos dat planete in duratione rerü ſcilicet in ſes ctis z in alijs magnis rebus. Quando auteni dicit maiores z medij z mino res intelligendum est de annis quos dant planete in natiuitatib boium ad viuendú:z ſicut dico de saturno ita de alijs planetis intelligas. Dicit Ptole meus in. 82.ppoſitióe prime partis maius ac fortius opus ſaturni est infris gidare z deficcare modicu ppter fortitudine frigous:z videt hoc eé pro го quod eft elongatus multi a calore ſolis & humidis fumofitatibus terre.Dic Albumazar redarguit Ptolemeń z poná deo dante ratiões fuas in fine bu ius partis de naturis planetay.
